description:
The plastic board has a spinner and holes where the Donald Duck playing pieces fit.

Roll and move except that you spin the spinner rather than roll a die. Move forward the amount spun. Land on +, move on extra spaces. Land on -, go back spaces. The first person to reach No. 100 is the winner.

Contents
1 Game Board.
1 Spinner Piece.
6 Donald Duck Playing Pieces.

French name: Visite A Disneyland
Dutch name: Reis In Disneyland

boardgame: Duncan Hamilton Invites You to Race at Oulton Park
description:
The board is a rendition of the Oulton Park circuit which is in Cheshire, England.

The player must negotiate the track in the fastest time to win. Magnets under the board move the cars when you turn the steering wheel

Duncan Hamilton (1920 - 1994) was a British racing driver

Target game, circa 1935, when militarism was very popular in German society. The title means 'Machine Gun with Target'.

The gun is a crank operated Model 08 Heavy Machine Gun on a sled mount. Made of wood with metal barrel. The gun fires wooden bullets at four card targets enclosed within the game box lid. Complete with charger clip [for automatic loading] and 10 wooden bullets.

The targets are worth between 1 and 4 points. Most points earned wins the game.

"The Photo-Recognizing Game"

Intended to be a party game I suspect.

Each player receives a scoresheet. A page of 12 photographs is chosen and on the word "Go", all players (or teams) write down as many of the people on that page that they recognize.

The winner is the player or team with the most correct answers.

The ever-hopeful publisher recommends that you buy another copy so that you can play with a bigger group of people.

Steam Torpedo: First Contact – R&D is a non-collectible booster pack for the tactical submarine battle game Steam Torpedo that includes ten new submarine compartment tiles, thereby allowing players to build new submarines and create new strategies.

Objective: score most points by flipping the ball/marble into the marked holes
 - acting by turn -
very similar to 'Tiddlywinks' but using a catapult (cardboard) and a marple
at least 2 different editions 1935

Taken from the the game's official web site:

“Tangram Fury” is a competitive and amazingly addictive table-top game based on the ancient Chinese puzzle known as a “tangram.”  Players attempt to be the first to recreate images using the seven pieces of a standard tangram puzzle. Fun for everyone! Tangram Fury can be played individually, or in teams, with as few as two players.

Players compete against each other to be the first to build tangram puzzles shown on cards. The first player done, wins the card. The first player to earn five cards, wins the game.

Tangram Fury has two levels of play: “Fan” and “Expert”. The Fan level is easier, because the shapes on the cards have borders that show how each of the seven tangram pieces fit together to form the shape. The Expert level only shows a silhouette of the shape and players must figure out which pieces go where.

The game includes four categories of tangram images: people, animals, fish & birds, and objects & shapes. Each category is represented by colored cards. Rolling a multi-colored die determines the categories.

In Kurukshetra, players fight the epic battle of Kurukshetra following the story as it was first told in the hindu epic Mahabharata. Two families, the Pandavas and the Kauravas fight for control of the kingdom.

It's a strategy boardgame played in two phases: In the first phase, players struggle to obtain different advice from Krishna that will help them further on. In the next phase, players battle the heroes of the Kaurava family. Special Pandava family members and Krishnas advice are extra efficient when facing the different Kaurava warriors.

The winner of the game is the one who defeats most Kaurava warriors and scores most victory points.

Race to catch the Pokemon!

From the box: "Ready, Set, Go!" It's an all-out race to catch Pokemon! Every player plays on ever turn in this Pokemon capturing frenzy. But watch out! It takes quick reflexes and fast thinking to snag the Pokemon you need to win! Features 36 all-new Pokemon from the Black and White televesion series!"

The game uses pattern-matching skills to claim cards after capturing pokemon on the board.

Players turn over 3 cards each round. Each card lists 2 pieces of data which can be used to match one of three pieces of data listed on the game board for each Pokemon. You only need to match one thing to be able to capture the Pokemon. After you've played all three cards, you then check to see if you match any of the pattern cards set by the side of the board, ranging from 2 to 5 points. If you can claim a pattern, you keep that card and get the points. Once all 8 pattern cards are claimed, the game is over. It's all explained on one small two-sided rules sheet.

The game is simple: a sort of "treasure hunt". Based on the exploration and identification of two "mysterious" objects, the game recreates perfectly the basic concept of Voyager: exploring mysterious places to study mysterious things.
The aim of the game is unveil the "mystery". To do that you must identify an object and a point moving along a path of boxes to connect 15 locations on a world map. There are moves by launching a special die on which the face with 1 shows the logo (V) instead of the number.
If the die result is V can perform special actions. 
The 15 places and 15 objects are depicted on maps.
During the game, players get Voyager chips that have a value between 1 and 3. Who gets the highest total wins.

Each player chooses an animal and play it simultanously.
When one and the same animal is present on several cards, the first player to imitate his voice wins the card. The player with the most points is declared "Grand Winner".

The are also several other variants of this card game.

Each player takes a set of 28 cards. All players chooses one animal they are course the game placing the card of this animal in front of him. The game begins. 
All players turns around the top card of their deck. When two identical cards are on the table the owners of the cards must imitate the voice of the opponent's animal. The winner gives all his cards in front of him to the loser. Whenever a player draws a donkey, he must chancge his ainmal. The game continues until someone gets rid of all his cards. He is the winner.

Hidden Empires II is a print-and-play game for 2 to 6 players where each player assumes the role of an ant colony. Players deploy worker ants on an abstracted hex-area map to look for and forage food. Colonies use food to produce more workers to produce more food, etc. Eventually colonies grow large enough to produce reproductive male and female ants. Players score points for each reproductive ant released during a nuptial flight.

A deck of cards provide environmental events and different genetic traits for the various ant species. Conflict between colonies is possible but is not the primary focus of the game. All events, traits, and general game play are based on the natural history of actual ant species.

Hidden Empires II is a major update to Hidden Empires and includes a larger map, less random traits, and clearer rules divided into basic, advanced and optional sections.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Pawn Stars Board Game Pay Out is based on the hit History Channel Show Pawn Stars. As in the television show it is the player's goal to get the best deal for the items coming into the shop.  Each turn four items come into the shop and are placed on the playing board.  Players roll the three game dice and combine the showing dollar amounts to see who can estimate the actual value of the item.  The person coming closest wins the item that round.  However, if the player over-estimated the actual item value they take a profit loss and log the item as trash on their score sheet. If the player winning the item that round under-estimated the item they make a profit and the item is scored as a treasure on the score sheet.  At the end of the day, profits and losses are totaled up and the player with the largest profit is the winner.

You must prepare several dice.
This game is sequel game of Sun of Austerlitz in Game Journal#33.
This game covers the battle of Waterloo in 1815, the last battle of Napoleon.
This game uses the same engine as its sequel game.  Players must draw a command chit at random to decide which formation will work next.
The rule is longer than usual GJ's games 12 pages.
The game can be played 4 to 6 hours based on its explanation.

198 counters, 1 game map, rulebook

In this classic word game, players use their seven drawn letter-tiles to form words on the gameboard. Each word laid out earns points based on the commonality of the letters used, with certain board spaces giving bonuses. But a word can only be played if it uses at least one already-played tile or adds to an already-played word. This leads to slightly tactical play, as potential words are rejected because they would give an opponent too much access to the better bonus spaces.

The perfect edition of Scrabble for the Book Lover! Bonus points are awarded with the use of any author name, book title, or literary character.

Party guests are given a card with the names of countries on them, but they are all messed around, anagrams and the like.

The guest or team who get the most right answers, wins.

The guests are read a short story with blanks at certain points. The game is for the guests to fit in the correct words during the evening. The most right answers, wins.

The box also contains two more modest party games. Public Notices asks all participating guests to fill in the gaps on well known notices. And Change a Letter tests their knowledge of animals. Each player is given a list of words and they have to change just one letter to make it the name of an animal.

Named after the famous music chart that is broadcast nonstop in the last week of each year, this game will test players' musical knowledge ranging from the sixties all the way to the zeros.

The game features two modes. You can play one after the other or just choose one. Firstly, the easier popquiz. Throw the dice, answer the question and reach the end. Secondly, the difficult knowledge quiz. Questions get harder and only the true music lover will reach the end.

objective: be the first shuffling all your pucks into opponent's territory
main game:
 - acting by turn -
each player is shuffling his 6 pucks (black or white) into opponent's territory with a tensed shirr;
try to push back already placed opponent's pucks, too.
1st round ends after all pucks are moved;
bad luck: if you hit opponent's shirr and your puck is pushed back into your own territory;
2nd round: remove successful positioned pucks and start  playing the game with the residual pucks
3rd round: dito... 
related to:
jakkolo, shuffle-board games...
remarks: mainly positioned for army people - see cover

There are 50 cards divided into two families, 25 cards with Philibert the quirky cat and 25 cards with Philibert's wardrobe and clothes.

The 25 cards with wardrobes are placed face-up on the table. The 25 cards with Philibert is placed face down as a deck and is drawn. The first child that identifies the missing garment in the right color from the Philibert's card gets this card. The winner is the player with the most cards at the end of the game.

There are more variants available.

Tzing is a circus horse. Tzang is his tamer, but he always forgets something in his equipment. We must help him find what he has forgotten.

All cards are spread face-up on the table. Equipment cards are shuffled, top card is drawn and all players are looking horse from the card what equipment is missing. The player who finds the right one wins the card. When all equipment cards have been checked, the game is over. The winner is the one with the most cards.

Promotional game.

In a turn, you draw the top card and give the top clue to the next player. If he knows the correct answer, he claims the card. If he gives the wrong answer or passes, the next clue is given to the player on his left, who will claim the card if they get the answer right. And so on, around the group, until the card is won.

Most cards, wins.

The publisher, a coffee supplier, claims that this is one of 60 games they have published!

Objective: collect as many points as possible knowing most of the questions 
The game has various sets of quiz cards ranging from maths, geography, arts, music to inventions (8).
Upon completing the circuit by touching the two probes to the correct question and answer a light turns on;
additional sets (21) were available;
there are 2 different editions:
small (cardboard) and large (wood)

Comparable with games like Electric Contact Quiz and or Knapp Electric Questioner

The board is slanted and it has five holes, each scoring either 10, 25, 50, 75 or 100 points. It is placed at one end of a dining table or on the floor, and players must roll balls at it, hoping to get the ball to stay in one of the holes. The player with the most points, wins.

Bizarre title but that might have been its attraction.

Standard trivia party game. Guests each get a card with six questions on it and then have to work out the answers until the host asks for them to be swapped around. Now you get new questions to answer, and so on. Guest with the most correct answers, wins.

Ronnie Waldman (1914 - 1978) hosted 'Monday Night at Eight', a long-running BBC radio show. One section of the show was 'Puzzle Corner', hence the name of this game.

Party game. Guests are given a card with 24 'questions' on it. The question takes the form of two clues to two diffent subjects, but the correct answers will rhyme.

Example
Recreation Ground - Answer is Park
Sign - Answer is Mark

The guest with the highest amount of correct answers, wins.

One in the Dainty Series of games.

A standard roll and move game, published as a promotion by Champion Spark Plugs in the mid-thirties.

The track winds around and features special squares where you have to obey the instructions. You can also take shortcuts and longcuts, if you know what I mean.

First to finish, wins.

Game published by Chevrolet in their promotional publication called Chevrolet Motoring Fun Game Book.

In this game you are provided with a scene of the family enjoying a picnic. The game is to see how many things you can find that begin with a different letter of the alphabet. I think it was intended to be played in the car on a long journey. So, after a pre-agreed time limit, the first player must pass the picture over to the next player, probably in the seat next to him or her, and that player must see if they can find more than the first player, and so on, until everyone in the car (hopefully not including the driver) has had a turn. The board suggests that if you can get 18 right that would be rated as a 'Fair', 20 right 'Good' and 22 'Very good'.

The storyteller has 30 seconds in which to tell a "shaggy dog story" including three key words listed on a card taken at the start of that player's turn.

The trick is that the storyteller is trying to conceal which three words of the story are on the card.

After the story has been told, the other players may confer and jointly have three guesses as to which words were on the card.

description:
The object of the game is to earn as many euros as possible.

The game takes three rounds.  Each player receives a set of fifteen cards with pictures of eight coin and seven banknote values. There are twelve European countries marked with flags.  During the rounds players try to gain flags by strategically playing money cards and having the highest deposit for each country.  Flags can change hands each round.

At the end of the three rounds, players score according to the flags they have collected.  Countries whose flags share a common border allow a player to multiply the value of euro in these countries by 2, 3, or 4.  Two countries multiply values by two, three countries by three, etc.

The winner is the player with the most money.

A self-published tactical combat module for Advanced Squad Leader, consisting of a rule leaflet, map section, pre-cut counters, and scenario cards, expanding the game to the Vietnam War. New units and rules include helicopters, war dogs, claymore mines, and other equipment and situations unique to the conflict in SE Asia.

description:
A basic dictionary game.  Players create definitions to words and try to determine which is the real answer.

Choosing the correct definition gets a player 2 points, having a player's definition chosen gets 1 point, and the card reader gets 3 points if nobody chooses the real definition.

Points are tallied and the winner is the one with the most points at the end of the game.

Objective: be the very last of the round keeping Ivanhoe in your hands 
'A Parlor game based on the story of Sir Walter Scott'
- acting by turn -
total 31 cards (15 duplicates + Ivanhoe)
1 entire pack is dealt - if someone holds a pair it's laid aside
2 player who holds Ivanhoe informs the others
3 during a turn you must draw a card - if you 'pair' ==> the 2 cards must lay down
4 player who holds Ivanhoe has the advantage drawing 2 cards
(from one or another or the same person)
5 when a player obtains Ivanhoe card must be shown to others
and then put back in his/her hand
6 after all cards have been formed into pairs player holding Ivanhoe wins
remarks:
Cards are: Ivanhoe (1), Lokesley (Robin Hood), De Bracy, Wamba the Jester, Princess Rowena, Rebecca, Sir Brian de Bois Guilbert, Isaac of York, Prior Aymer, King Richard, Friar Tuck, Prince John, Hubert, Cedric the Saxon, Athelstane, Reginald Front de Boeuf

The Game of Merry Christmas is probably the earliest known version of Mall Madness type game.  In the game, players roll the dice and move to make their way through the streets of the village. Players visit the local shops and spend their money to purchase all the items on their families Christmas wishlists.  The first player to buy all their items and returns home will be the winner in this vintage Parker's Brothers game.

The whole family can experience real-life hunting – rules, safety, decoys, waterfowl identities, fun, excitement, laughs! Great fun for a hunter, birdwatcher, or anyone interested in waterfowl because it involves 2-4 players in hunting strategy, bird identification, and safety practices used by good sportsmen. The object is to be the first player back to the Clubhouse with the "bag limit" of birds (at least 4 bird pictures with matching description cards).

Starting at the clubhouse, roll the dice and move that number of colored spaces. You may move either way on the board, and you may choose to take an inside path to reach the color space that you want. At each space you collect a card of the matching color: Green for a bird picture, orange for a bird description, or yellow for a situation card which explains a safety tip or legal point. When drawing a situation card, follow the instructions; it may send you to the Dog Training School, Sporting Goods Store, or Skeet Shooting Field! (The tan square has no matching deck, so no card is drawn.) When you have collected four different bird cards and their matching descriptions, hurry back to the Club House to claim your win!

Duck Hunter is packaged in a handsome, full-color box which contains the game board, instructions, four tokens, two dice and three card decks. One deck contains beautifully executed, full-color pictures of birds, the second deck has full descriptions of birds and their habitats and the third deck contains situation cards which remind players of proper hunting procedures, present comedic pitfalls, and provide penalties and bonuses.

The bird pictures are by internationally renowned wildlife artist Bob Hines. He was the only National Wildlife Artist to be employed by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service.

The idea is the players get to fight for Victory Points that provide provinces and Industries.

The board is a map of Argentina,  and each province has assigned a number from 2 to 12. The player in his/her turn, throws two dice at first, and the resources are distributed according to locations in the province that has the number of dice. Then, s/he can pay resources for locations in the province or in an industry.

The player who achieves 16 VPs at the end of his/her turn, is the winner. This is essential to gain control of several provinces and industries and to maximize the powers of the Officer Corrupt, Unionists and the Stockbrokers.

Promotional game, published by Pluto Water, a leading laxative supplier around 1910 onwards.

The game is a simple spin and see the result. The spinner has a remarkable 24 'sections' it can point towards after it stops spinning. No skill needed, just record the game with paper and pencil.

description: Osprey miniatures rules covering the Interwar period of the twentieth century: Russian Civil War, Very British Civil War and all points in-between. The rules are intended for games using 28mm miniatures.

Promotional item by Red Crown, the oil and petrol supplier.

The first one to reach the end of the 'road', wins.

boardgame: Zombie Survival 2: There Goes the Neighborhood!
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Let's face it – zombies are many things, but graceful isn't one of them. Thankfully, you didn't listen when the wife told you to clean the yard. Those lawn gnomes and pink flamingos don't seem to stop them. They do, however, slow 'em down! Maybe it will be enough!

Zombie Survival 2: There Goes the Neighborhood!, an expansion to Zombie Survival: The Board Game, introduces Outside Item tiles, three additional Store Decks, a new card type ("The Fate Deck"), Damage tokens, and lots more Zombies!

description:
From the website:
"In the game are general knowledge questions and situations of personal connection. Participants choose a temporary ally with whom to solve each activity.

If you answer about history, sports, nature, or agree on ice cream flavors, or how to react in case of being kidnapped by a pirate ship... the ally of your choice... may be different."

Trivia route where the only way forward is taking allies with others players.

You chose a temporary ally to answer a question, if both says the correct answer, both move one square from the board. You must select who are the perfect ally for each situation... but if s/he is in first place, do you answer with him/her?

From Publisher Website

Axle tribes is the game of automotive combat set within a post apocalyptic world. Please note, that this is a game for the gamers - it is not for profit, nor will it ever be. In addition, the rules will literally be shaped by the players - each new edition will contain rule changes and additions that are suggested by YOU. Please feel free to jot down any ideas as you play and submit them to the Drunk Dwarves webmaster.

And without further delay - we present our first published game: AXLE TRIBES.

The Earth has been blighted. Long ago an event, the great end, turned the world as we knew it into a smitten landscape of broken glass and twisted jungles of metal rod. All that was green and blue, has been reborn in hues of dead browns. The air tastes bad - and it's hard to believe we were ever meant to live here.

Between the endless stretches of waste there are monstrous cities ruled by powerful land lords, where the land still clings to life. But if you live there, you live their way - or not at all. That's why many of us band together and try to survive in the wastes of the bad lands - among the mutant freaks and the harsh ever-present sun. The Axle Tribes rule this land, that is - everything left worth ruling. The Axle Tribes fight, they fight over food, water, and the parts needed to make and maintain our steeds. I am a warrior, my steed is my life, and the life of my tribe.

Game Description Notes

This free set of miniatures rules is meant to be played with Hot Wheels (or similar) model cars on the table top. (Serious players often customize their vehicles with weapons, armor-plating, etc.) Both players control a Tribe and each is allowed to build a set amount of vehicles (called Steeds) to fight it out.

Players work within an agreed upon dollar cap to create a variety of vehicles. Various weapons and vehicle upgrades make each Steed more distinctive, but also add to the cost. The game progresses in Turns. (During a Turn each vehicle is activated once.) Vehicles may move, fire, etc. when activated.

Ten-sided dice are used as a randomizer to decide control rolls, to hit rolls, etc. Maneuvers are based on 45 degree turns. There are special rules for collisions. Each steed has its own record sheet where damage is recorded. Printable counters are included to help track damage and speed.

The victory conditions of the game are left up to the players. A total destruction match in which only one side is left standing is the simplest way to play.

Promotional game, published by Milton Bradley, but for Coca-Cola.

The board has six lanes, each numbered. To move a horse, throw three dice. The three numbers on the dice means that those three horses move forward one space. Of course, if the same number is rolled on two of the dice, that horse moves forward two spaces. Treat three of the same number accordingly.

Crossing the finish line is a little different for this game. If a horse is one square from the finish, it can only move forward if only one die shows that number (so if two or three dice are that number, the horse cannot move.

I would think the game plays best with 2, 3 or 6 players.

Promotional game made by the Frisch's Big Boy chain of restaurants.

There are two panels to cut out and you can make little cards for them. Once you have your cards, you can play.

Shuffle the cards and draw one, to determine what the batter does: One team is called Big Boy and one is called Buddy Boy. The instructions say to use a button or small token as the batter.

And that is it, draw a card, determine the result, move your button player marker if needed. I have seen games with a little more skill!!!

Promotional game from the Gulf petrol company.

Each player is given a 'pad' on which to write their answers. This pad is called in Britain, a Magic Slate. You write on it, then peel the cover and, hey presto, the writing disappears.

Everyone plays at the same time. A word is read out and each player spells it as best they can. The first player to get an agreed number right, wins.

I suspect this was intended as an in-car game, to pass the time on a long drive. That connection would explain the fact that it was made by a petrol supplier.

A promotional game from Quaker Oats, probably in the 1920's. Simple spin and move game.

First you have to make a spinner yourself. Having done that, you have to spin a 1 before you can start moving along the track.

You start the game at Home and, to win, you must advance to the Grocery Store. Along the way you must follow instructions. They are of the variety, 'Forget honey, go back to the start', that sort of thing.

Of course, as usual in these basic games, you need to spin an exact number to finish.

Published to coincide with the 1964 New York World's Fair.

It is a simple track game. Movement is a little different though. The game is supposed to be played with special Coca Cola bottle caps, a set of 120 with illustrations of the fair. You are supposed to draw a bottle cap out of a container and move the number of spaces of the last digit on the cap. 120 caps is an awfully large amount of Coca-Cola to drink before you can play!

If you land on a red or black space, follow the instructions.

The first one to reach the centre of the board, wins.

Pocket version triva board game from "Das Kleine Quiz" serie. The question are presented on a notepad, with multiple choice answers. This travel game is designed to ask some questions during a car drive, a flight or something like that.

A small trivia game about coctails; with 100 questions and answers from the "Das Kleine quiz" serie. The game itself is like a notebook (with spiral binding). Each page contains 2 questions and 4 possible answers, the back side contains the correct answers with additional information.

A small trivia game about Berlin; with 100 questions and answers from the "Das Kleine Quiz" serie. The game itself is like a notebook (with spiral binding). Each page contains 2 questions and 4 possible answers, the back side contains the correct answers with additional information.

boardgame: Was gibt es Neues? Das Spiel

description: A word game played on an hexagonal grid, with tiles for consonants and generic tokens for vowels. Every player have an initial dotation of vowels, each turns he get two consonant, and wins when he uses all of his vowels.

Objective: give correct answers collecting most points after agreed time
- acting by turn -
typical 'local (= Cologne)' trivia game (dice/cards/local map) moving around and finding correct answers (music, arts, people, building....)

content:
300 image cards, 1 board, 6 meeples, 1 d6, 1 coloured die, rules

"It's War!! ...and it just got a whole lot nicer"
Just when you thought it couldn't get any better, Nice: the Expansion comes along! With over 50 new powers, more scenarios, more miniature bonuses, vehicle rules, civilian rules, even new ways to play! Powers like Goodzilla, Star Student and Combine; Fireball, Mechanic and Shindig Pandemic; even all the benefits of lobbing shoes at your opponent. Scenarios that involve taking a grand tour of the gaming board, miniatures bonuses for Fish-Handedness, and so much more...
Continuing with the now familiar - "No miniatures - no Problem!" shenanigans, the rules still allow you to have a cucumber and a croissant in a pitched battle to the death! Only now they include Vehicles and Civilians. Even the vehicles gain bonuses for how big they are and how many guns they have strapped to them. Civilians respond to the fight going on around them; either by running for cover or attacking like a mob - but they still need to be weary of falling debris...

Promotional game published for promoting the International Harvest company, who made agricultural vehicles and machines.

You compete against other farmers and aim to be the first one to harvest your crop. You can chose either wheat, hay, cotton, or corn to plant (what you choose makes no difference to your tactics or ambitions).

You throw dice to move up your section of the board. At intervals there are white boxes. This is where you must change your implement before moving past the white boxes. Again, it doesn't affect your tactics about which implement to use next, you must use all of them at some stage if you want to win the game. First to do just that, wins. At times you will be instructed to draw a 'Whoa' card. As usual, this will perhaps help you or hinder you.

This game was distributed by International Harvester dealers. The playing board is made of vinyl and folds up easily to fit in the box. The game consists of 2 small dice, Whoa cards and Implement cards.

Objective: collect as many question cards as possible making the correct and fastest reply
1 draw a question card and read content to everyone - e.g. 'river'
2 turn the wheel - it determines the initial letter of the river to be named
3 the fastest reply receives the card (1 winning point)
4 if answer was wrong return an additional card

remarks:
predecessor of 'Stadt-Land-Fluss'
can be played as teams, too
due to the spelling of Cologne 'Cöln', which was recommended between 1900 and 1919, game had to be published at that period

Translated, Our Five Senses.  This is an educational game (entirely in French) to help kids learn about their senses.  You roll and attach the puzzle pieces that correspond to the sense you have on your play board (hearing, smell, touch, sight, or taste). This game also comes with a corresponding educational guide for parents to assist them in helping children explore their five senses. Comes with 5 player boards, a die, and 6 puzzle pieces per board that attach to it.

The Rolling, Racing, Frantic, Flipping Action Game of Strategy and Chance

Taking Place on a 3-D Board, Players take turns rolling a ball around a circular game field.  As the ball slows, it drops through one of a number of holes which hits a flipper.  The flipper levers over revealing the number of spaces a player moves one of their four cones.  The object is to get one of your cones around the game field back home.  But beware, for an opponent landing on your cone will send you back to the start, and the eight spaces on the flippers, which lever into a central dumping well, will flip your cone out of the game permanently.

Crossword Picante is a bilingual crossword game utilizing foam tiles with letters from the English alphabet on one side and letters from the Spanish alphabet on the other.

Players take turns forming words crossword style in either language, replenishing their hand of letters after each play.

Play ends when the letters in the chile pepper shaped bag are exhausted.

The object of the game is to make the names of the states, building each name, syllable by syllable, beginning with the first syllable.  There are two decks of cards, one for the states east of the Mississippi River, and one for the states west.  The decks contain one card for each syllable in each state name (the card backs show a picture of the state). One deck is used in a game.

To start, one deck is split into six piles with the word side up.  The first player takes a card with the first syllable of a state.  Only one first syllable can be taken, and only four states can be started at a time.  A player may then take as many additional syllable cards that are available in the open piles for the states they are building, including from uncovered cards.

If a player cannot use any of the top cards or no first syllables are available, the player turns up a new set by putting each top card face down next to each pile.  If the player still cannot use any of these newly turned cards, play goes to the next player, who can then turn up a new set if necessary.  When all word side facing cards have been used, the next player may turn back over the covered card and use it if they can.

Completed states are set aside and a player can then start a new state, up to four.

When all states are built, the game is over and the largest number of states wins.  Alternatively, the largest number of cards may win.

An alternate rule set is included to use the picture side, building up to six states, by matching pictures.

Based on the most famous and popular Roguelike video game, NetHack, this two player board game adaptation is divided into three boards representing three different areas from the video game: the Mazes of Menace, the Castle and Gehennom. Each player's token moves along a fixed path and draws an encounter card on each space from the deck corresponding to the board they are on. The encounters are either monsters to be fought by die roll or items, which assist in various ways and some of which are essential to progress between boards. Players can either move carefully (they get to see the encounter card before deciding whether to proceed) or quickly (they have no choice but to face the encounter but move further if they face it successfully). To win, the players must reach the end of the board, take the Amulet of Yendor, and return back along the path to the start to escape the dungeon.

An extension for the game "Kart Sur Glace". These many small extensions to add together or not in the game.

- one more Kart (you can play to 6 players)

- 6 Bonus like...
"Wind" : opposing karts turned upside
"SnowStorm" (malus): reverse its position with the last of the race
"Twister": you add and move a token "Twister" or move a token already present (you lose a card Action if you hit a token "Twister")

- 6 new "MegaPowwaa" for the 6 karts
Now, you choose a card "MegaPowaaa" from the two cards available.
Examples: 
Orange: mechanical arm // telekinesis
Red: scrambler // scanner

- new type of game : 
Kart Ghost
In turn, all players can move free "kart ghost". 
If it hits a kart, the kart is on the roof.

&

Kart Flag 
A team game, like the "Catch Flag" in Paintball or "LaserQuest"

- Pit Stop Card:
Fill card "bonus" and take a "Action Card"

- White Card (Bonus & Mega Powwaa):
for your imagination...

The LEGO Heroica series represents a classic dungeon game. The players move through tunnels, collecting treasure and fighting enemies. Conflict is resolved by dice rolls; the die results can be a number or a symbol, which might allow the player to use a special weapon. Instead of a character sheet, the players have equipment "backpacks" which can be filled with weapons, potions, keys, etc, depending on what they find during their adventure.

Heroica: Ganrash is a mini-expansion set packaged in a polybag, which was exclusively sold at Toys-R-Us Canada stores in 2012. It includes gold swords ('Blades of Bounty'), a Luck Potion, a goblin warrior, and a goblin guardian. It can be combined with any or all of the other games in the Heroica series; it is not playable on its own.

A children's game modelled on pin-the-tail-on-the-donkey, only in this case, the object is to pin bananas in the proper place on a linen-like cloth with bright lithographed cartoon-like characters and the image of a boy holding a banana.

Bananas with green tips go into the fruit bowl, bananas that are all yellow and ripe go into the boy's hand. 
The players are blindfolded, and the one who pins his/her banana nearest to its correct position wins.

The game promoted the banana as the healthy "friend of little folks".

The goal of the game is to take or block the opponent's checkers.

Each player's pawns are placed on the front rows. In his turn player moves his piece (pawn or stack).
To move, a pawn jumps one hex or be stacked one ofof his pawn directly next hex. 
   A stack of two pawns jumps 2 squares.
   A stack of three pawns jump 3 squares, etc..
   A piece by jumping over an opponent's piece "takes" and removes it from the game

Contents:

     Game board
     11 pawns for each player

Two great games that collide in fun! Mondo-like gameplay, Carcassonne tiles, twenty minutes of fun.

Place your Carcassonne tiles (seventy or so per player) in the middle of the table, face up. Each person has an identical play sheet with spaces to create a map of twenty four tile. Flip a goal card and set the timer. Players start filling their play sheets in real time, no turns, to score points by completing cities and roads, and competing to best satisfy the selected goal.

Solitaire rules included.

SPinOut is a short game playable with the Series: Shibumi game system. It uses the 4×4 board, 15 white balls and 15 red ones.

				
				
					RulesThe board starts empty. Two players, white and red, take turns placing a ball of their colour either on a empty board hole or stacking on a 2x2 platform of existing balls.

A player, as an alternative, can choose to remove a ball (white or red) belonging to a 2x2 platform that supports not more than one other ball on each higher level. If a ball is removed then any others that it supports will drop. Any ball that directly supports two or more on the level above is pinned and cannot be removed. The removed ball must return immediately to the board in the place left free by the top ball just dropped (spinout rule, as for a car out of control!). A player is not allowed to remove a ball if the other player just removed a ball in his turn.

Aim: the goal of the game is to create the most three-in-a-rows on the surface of the Shibumi pyramid (4 triangular faces). Rows can intersect but do not wrap around corners or edges. 
The game ends when the board is full with the 30 balls. Players are not allowed to pass and if a player has not legal moves the other player ends the game by placing his remaining balls to complete the pyramid.
In case of a tie for number of three-in-a-rows, the winner is the player who has the least number of balls on the surface of the pyramid.

Back of Box:

Advance to GO! The MONOPOLY Brand introduces the new San Diego edition showcasing America's Finest City. This game puts San Diego's most valued historical landmarks, cultural treasures and word-class tourist attractions up for sale.

Includes custom board, Golf & Beach cards replace Chance & Community Chest cards, "Sunshine Dollars" from the San Diego County Credit Union, yellow "Ranch Estates", blue "Beach Homes", and 6 custom pewter tokens: Surfer, San Diego Union Tribune, Sunglasses, Sailboat, Beach Cruise Bicycle and Golf Cart.

The Outlaw expansion has a very limited print run and was made available to backers of Dark Horse on Kickstarter.

This mini expansion adds a unique Special Role called the "Outlaw" to the game. This role may come into play when a Jail Action is triggered, with a player successfully rolling a die to escape the scene of the crime. Once claimed, the Outlaw special role will have an interesting ability that allows a player to use the result of a rolled black die to bump opponents off already claimed actions. This powerful ability is kept in check by the owner of the Sheriff role having the added ability to use the result of a silver die to capture the Outlaw player who then loses the special role and must spend the remainder of the turn sitting in Jail.

This expansion gives players a teaser of some of the mechanics that will be added to the pending full box expansion for Dark Horse.

boardgame: Wire to Wire  The Horse Racing Game
description:
A dice based horse racing game, where you can play multiple roles of a jockey, a handicapper, or an owner.

Horses and Jockeys are rated with a rep value (the higher the value the better the horse or jockey) a common feature of all Two Hour Wargame rules. Horses are also rated by style of running and effectiveness of within their style.  The styles are Frontrunner (breaking hard from the gate), Stalkers (sits off the lead, then tries to close on the home stretch) and Closers (who run hard and the end). Finally it has a Class Rating, that is a race variable, that effects the horse on the day of the race. (making predicting the performance of a horse on any race, more troublesome).

It is fulled with horse racing terms and explanations, and comprehensive system for rating a horse to determine the odds of any race, and a full on campaign system, with buying, training and racing your horses and choosing your jockeys.

Product Description from the website:

THE SPORT OF KINGS, THE SPORT OF EVERYMAN

Once a year in early May, the sports world stops and takes note. It's the running of the Kentucky Derby, the first leg of the Triple Crown of thoroughbred horse racing. It's a time where the casual fan and the hardcore handicapper share the rail as they watch to see if there will be another Triple Crown winner.

But horse racing is much more than the Derby. It's the come from behind winner, the wire to wire runner, the long shot that defies all odds as it bolts across the finish line in dramatic fashion.

Wire to Wire brings the excitement of thoroughbred horseracing to you in three different ways.
As a jockey you make all the decisions as to setting the pace, moving up, and finally when to make a run for the finish line.
As a handicapper you bet on the horses, not only to win but also in the Daily Double and Exactas.
And as an owner you take your horse from auction to winner’s circle by deciding which races to enter and which jockeys to hire.

In Wire to Wire we provide over two hundred horses in thirty races for you to use. And if that’s not enough we’ll show you how to build your own horse! Wire to Wire can be played solo or competitively, head to head. We've included a track, horses, and everything else you need to run your races. In fact, we've provided a Power Point version that you can play right on your computer!

So lay out the track, grab some dice and get ready for some fun.

Wire to Wire, THE horse racing game!

boardgame: Mad TV: El Juego de Mesa
description:
Based in the popular PC Game development by Rainbow Arts in the early ninety’s.
In the game, the players are tv programmers for a famous channel. They are new and in training period. The best player during the first two weeks is the winner the others are fired.

To get it, the players must: buy programs, programming them in the timetable, buy news, to contract ads, create their own tv series and shows…

The game is modular. The players can add or remove the modules to change the difficult and the duration of the game.
The game has different levels of difficult; apart from two modalitys of game. The player define at the beginning if the target of the game is a economic victory (profits) or a romantic victory (victory points that get conquest the heart of Betty).

The games is of free distribution. The author share it non-profit as tribute to the original pc game. The game is in testing phase.
The images and pictures are from the pc game. Other images and pictures are from internet from unknow authors. If one of this unknow authors recognize his art. He/She is inveted to contact with the designer to accredited the picture or remove it from the prototype.


Juego basado en el popular juego para PC desarrollado por Rainbow Arts a comienzos de los 90.
En el juego, los jugadores representan a programadores de televisión para una conocida cadena. Son nuevos y están en periodo de prueba por lo que el que mejor lo haga las 2 primeras semanas mantendrá su puesto de trabajo y los otros dos jugadores serán despedidos.

Para lograrlo los jugadores deberán: comprar programas, programarlos en la parrilla de programación en el horario mas adecuado, dar noticias, intercalar publicidad cumpliendo los requisitos de los patrocinadores, crear sus propias series y programas.

El juego es modular lo que permite añadiendo y quitando modulos aumentar o disminuir la dificultad y duración del juego. 
Esto permite tener varios niveles de dificultad; además también tiene dos modalidades de juego que deberán definirse al comienzo de la partida: Victoria económica (mayores beneficios) o Victoria romántica (por puntos de victoria conseguidos conquistando el corazón de Betty).

El juego es de libre distribución ya que el autor lo hace sin ánimo de lucro. El juego esta en fase de pruebas.
Las imágenes y diseño grafico tomadas para el prototipo son mayoritariamente del propio juego de PC al que el autor rinde tributo y otras imágenes de internet de autoría desconocida cuyos autores pueden ponerse en contacto con el diseñador para acreditarles o eliminarles en la utilización de su material para este prototipo.

MELI CADO is a word card game, in which people will make words but all together playing on the same word to make it evolve.

The goal is to get rid of your cards first.
Each player receives 7 cards at the beginning of a round.
Then the dealer shows up 2 cards representing letters on the center of the table. The first player is designed and to start
can add one of his letter before, after or covering one existing card to make a new correct word

When the first player has played any player can add a card to make the word change
For instance "CR" will become "CRY" then can become "DRY" and then "DIY"

Obviously the new word formed must exist and must be new.
Some cards are different colors, red for example can never be covered, the green can be placed at the ends etc.

Also during your turn You can make a 'Meli' and move the letters of the word to make an anagram.
Along the same lines you can also make a 'Cado' and remove a current letter of the word (which must form a new word) and ... offer it to an opponent.

When a player has no card in his hand the round stops and each p[layer count the remaining cards his has, which is his penalty score.

You also have purple cards which will give a special rule for the round: i.e: reverse(you have to write from the right to the left, insertion: allow players to insert cards in the word...

description:
Card game of table tennis.

The goal is to win the game what is played in two winning sets of 11 points.

Player plays cards in his turn. There are 4 type of cards in this game:
- Red cards impose a particular constraint to the opponent.
- Green cards increase the possibilities of their owner.
- Violet cards change the game for both players.
- Orange cards change the other cards

Each card has a name, a particular effect, and acts for a specified period.

On each card are shown:
- The effect of the card: "what"
- To whom it is addressed: "who"
- "wg" Period of time, "when"

Broadway is a tableau laying game for four players (two partnerships, with partners sitting opposite) using a double-six set of dominos.  The goal of the game is to have the most points, points are scored each turn where a player lays a tile that matches more than one individual tile segment already on the tableau.

A game is played over a series of seven hands, with each hand’s tableau building upon a double. The first hand starts with the double six, and successive hands continue on down to double blank.

At the beginning of each hand, thoroughly shuffle the tiles face-down. Each player then draws seven tiles. The player having the double corresponding to the current hand leads it face-up to the center of the table, oriented horizontally from his point of view. Thereafter, each player, in turn, places one tile from his hand onto the table, face-up and oriented horizontally from his point of view, such that at least one segment of the played tile matches at least one tile segment that is already part of the tableau. The tableau usually takes on an irregular, blob-like shape and need not remain closepacked; unusual shapes and enclosed empty spaces of any size are both acceptable. Not all segments need to match, but the more that do, the more points the play is worth. One matched segment (the most common play) scores nothing. Two matched segments score one point for the partnership that played it. Three matched segments score five points. Four or more matched segments score ten points. Points are recorded as they are made during play.

(from the rules found on pagat.com)

Objective:
be the first climbing up the board to the top by knowing the correct answers & describing the challenges confronted with (data out of the 80's)
 - acting by turn -
A quiz game for people who just turned 40 and want to test their knowledge about trendy things from those times.

Can you remember 'Schwarzwaldklinik', Rubik's Cube,...? 
You must explain or guess these 'terms', sometimes in a creative way! 
Whoever's guess is correct advances on the scoring board faster than other players.
More fun is achieved by reaching marked/coloured fields on the board; allowed ways of explaining are significantly reduced;
family: Series: Das Ü Party- and Quizgames (Spiel&Spass)

description:
Book and trivia game for protecting our Planet.

There are 250 cards with trivia questions rather easy, affordable at eight years. When correctly answering to a question, you can take a piece of tree and begin to build.
If the tree has built to be ready the players have saved the Earth.

SMS Madness is a card game composed of 50 cards divided into 5 decks of 10 cards : "no look!", "upside down!", "quick!", "nonsense!" and "photo/mms!".
The goal is for the players to send their answers via text messages to the game master.

BEFORE THE GAME
The players have to check the following : 
• The numbers of all the players are recorded in their cell phones. 
• Their phones make sounds when they receive text messages (bleep, music, etc.)

THE GAME
The first player is the game master. He picks a card and reads the instructions to the other players. Each deck of cards is a particular type of skill challenge :
- "no look!" : the players write and send their SMS without looking at their cell phones.
- "upside down!" : the players write and send their SMS with their telephones upside down.
- "quick!" : the players write their SMS and send them as fast as possible.
- "nonsense!" : the players write a sentence that uses the letters on the card. The letters are the acronyms of a sentence that players make up and send as fast as possible to the game master.
- "photo/mms!" : the players take a picture with their phone and send the MMS to the game master.

When the 1st text message is received, the game master counts down from 10 to 0. Only the messages received between the first SMS and 0 are accepted.
The best text message wins. If more than one is correct, the quickest wins.

Next round, the game master changes, and the former game master becomes a player.

The first player who wins 3 cards wins the game.

Objective:
gain as many winning points as possible placing words using crossword approach; 
 - acting by turn - 
comparable with Scrabble, but with following differences:
1. the faster the more points (hour glass) - if you exceed max. time (~30 sec) ==> no points at all
2. the far to the edge the more points 
3. no rating for letters
remarks:
even you can play this game with children (no time limit) Noris has published different editions for this segment, too

Australian game. Each player is given a set of four plastic spinning tops in their chosen colour. The board has an area where you should start spinning your tops, and various holes into which you hope a top will fall, giving you points. Any top not falling into a hole does not score. After spinning all your tops, add up your score. Highest score wins.

Objective: 5 different games for kids to train memory
 - 168 images/pictures, 4 large pictured boards -
game 1: memorize as many details of a large picture as possible
game 2: train your associative memory of pictures in combination with colours
game 3: train sequences (similar to game 6)
game 4: memorize as many images as possible
game 5: functional activities (e.g. what belongs into my suitcase?) to describe items
game 6: memorize sequence of cards predicting as many as possible

Objective: help children solving as many questions as possible - improve their knowledge
 - acting by turn; game must be assisted by adults/older children -
roll the (coloured) die and move your meeple
draw 1 of more out of 500 questions with 6 (coloured) areas:
- colours

question samples: colour of your heart? stuff dogs like? ...

content: 
cards, 6 meeples, 1 d6, 1 coloured die, rules

Welcome to world of Dark Legacy. A world ruled by the ruthless battle mage by the name of the Kaiser. The planet of Titan is at war. The Kaiser is bringing the world to its knees. This is a war where anything is possible and anyone can become a legend. It is here where your journey begins.

Will you join the Kaiser and his allies in the total domination of the planet or will you be part of the opposition? You get to decide how this story will tell itself. Make sure to choose wisely, for the fate of the planet lies within your hands.

Make your choice!

Dark Legacy is a unique strategy game that mixes the best elements of Magic the Gathering, Dungeons and Dragons and Chess and wraps them all up into a in-depth sci-fi/fantasy story. In Dark Legacy, players are no longer bystanders of the battle,  they get to be part of it as a war general within one of six different factions. With the unique and various weapons, armor and relics, that a player can equip themselves with, players can become whomever they imagine themselves to be.

In Dark Legacy, it is the players objective to destroy their opponents and their armies, by building a deck of magic spells that no one can withstand, rolling the d20 in their favor and strategically summoning their army to be the strongest. Just like in real battle you will have to anticipate the probability of the unexpected. Don't make choices too hastily for they may be the demise of your army and yourself.

Dark Legacy is a game that can be played one-on-one or in team battle.

Here are the type of cards that can be played in Dark Legacy:

     Enhancement cards are for summoned creatures, weapons and the Hero. These cards can either strengthen or weaken that which it is place upon.
     Creature cards are just that. They are the faction specific cards that each one of the six different factions use to strengthen their army to win the battle.
     Magic cards are the spell cards used to tip the balance in battle. There are faction specific magic cards and general magic cards.
     Armor cards are only for the Heroes in battle. This gives each Hero extra defense points on top of their life points.
     Weapon cards are only for the Heroes in battle. There is a wide variety of weapon cards and as the Hero levels up there are stronger and more magical weapons that become available to them.
     Relic cards are ancient cards with abilities that are available to every player if you find them. There are relics that will fit every players style of fighting.


The Evolution System for Battle

     Before the battle begins, players must decide who is going first.
     At the beginning of every battle each player draws five cards.
     Each player is now allowed to place one card on the equip side of the mat. This card has to cost 0 and the player has to have the correct skill level to put that card down.
     There is no damage permitted for the first round in battle, meaning that no one can attack or cast spells that deal damage until it is the first person’s turn again.
     The player that is going first must begin the battle by drawing a card. (At the beginning of every turn players will draw a card.)
     In order to cast any of the player’s cards they must roll the d20. There is only one roll per turn, unless there is a card in play that allows for another roll. The number that you roll will be the


number of casting points you have to cast cards from your hand.

     In order to cast spells you have to have a high enough SKILL LEVEL (SL) and have enough CASTING POINTS (CP).
     The player can then cast any number of cards as long as they have the proper SL and CP.
     If the opponent has the ability to counter the spell they can do so after the card is cast.


Now the second round begins.  At the beginning of the player’s turn they must draw a card and then decide weather to attack or cast and use the cards abilities to help you win the battle.

Charging Field

     Players may put any card, within their faction or subsection, on this field face down. This card is now charging. This field is to be used for cards that are above of your skill level. 
     You may place cards that are in your skill range, but that would defeat the purpose of charging the card. In order to cast the card that you put on the charging field, it must charge for the difference of your skill level and the skill level required to cast

description:
These were exclusive promos offered to those who supported Briefcase on its funding campaign on indiegogo from 11-02-2012 to 11-03-2012.

The exclusive promos included the following Company Card Sets:

Beauty Clinic (3 Company cards + 1 Randomizer card)
Casino (3 Company cards + 1 Randomizer card + 3 1cm Dice)
Dot Com (3 Company cards + 1 Randomizer card)
Football Club (3 Company cards + 1 Randomizer card)
Leasing Company (4 Company Cards + 1 Randomizer card)
Model Agency (3 Company cards + 1 Randomizer card)
Multiplex (3 Company cards + 1 Randomizer card)
Shipyard (3 Company cards + 1 Randomizer card)

These Company cards enrich the variety and add more options to the basic game.
Shibumi Set Game. Abstract two player. Players take turns placing 3 balls of different colors and then removing a ball without ever getting 3 balls of the same color to directly touch each other.

This card game consists of 140 cards, 112 element cards and 28 elementary particle cards. Several different games can be played with the same set of cards. The basic game is played like UNO. Players have to match element cards with same period or same group. The elementary particle cards are used as action cards or penalty cards. One of the advanced version is played like mahjong. More rules of advanced is being published in the official web site soon.

This game is pure pre-WWII propaganda aimed at encouraging Germans to enlist in the military. It is a simple roll-and-move affair, with the objective of reaching the final square first while obeying special instructions for each of the illustrated spaces. The game instructions are printed on the underside of the box lid.

The board has two sides, each with a distinct game and message. The title side shows the wonderful experiences awaiting recruits to the army, navy and air forces: being taught how to make a bed properly by a kindly drill sergeant, learning how to gamble with cards, eating gourmet food from a field kitchen, meeting pretty ladies at a military dance, and engaging in a variety of sports, drama and musical activities. Along the way, recruits also learn how to shoot, how to march, how to swim a river in full kit, how to climb a mast and how to fly, before marching happily out the gate as their service comes to an end.

The second side of the board has a similar roll-and-move game that sends players on a grand tour of a newly expanded Germany. The map shows a Germany that includes Austria and the Sudetenland (with the former borders of Czechoslovakia still outlined), but does not yet extend into Poland. This clearly dates the game to late 1938 or early 1939.

Menglianggu（孟良崮） is a two-player game that simulates the Battle of Menglianggu during the Chinese Civil War, May 10 to 16, 1947.
NOTE: Menglianggu is a region in Mengyin County, Shandong Province - see Menglianggu Campaign.

One player commands the CCP（中國共產黨）and the other player commands the KMT（中國國民黨）.  Each turn represents one day and each hex is about 2.5 km across.  There are three kinds of basic units, including infantry, artillery, and HQ.  The scale of the basic unit is one regiment size.  Players activate the divisional HQ to command every unit in range to barrage, move, fire, and progress respectively.  You can use tactical chit to order the support of air crafts and the farmers.  You need to be aware the supply line in every action.  The goal is differenct from both sides in different scenarios.

There are two scenarios in this game: "Battle of Menglianggu" and "Southern Sandong Campaign".  The first scenario simulates the real history from  May 13 to 16 in 1947 (4 turns).  The goal of KMT is to save the Reorganized 74th Division.  The second scenario simulates the beginning of the Southern Sandong Campaign from May 10 to 20 (11 turns).  KMT needs to eliminate half of CCP force.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Welcome to the Slash! movie, set in Camp Bloodshed USA, where every teenager is a target and every dark corridor hides terrible danger. Of the eight teenagers spending the night at the Camp Bloodshed cabin, only one will be alive at dawn.

From three to six players will take part in this hour-long bloodfest. To win, players have to guess which characters will die and when. They must then use their action cards to make it happen, tempting some with sinful activities, while protecting others with virtuous deeds. Meanwhile, lurking in the darkness, Slash! awaits, and every time his card is drawn, one hapless teenager dies. Who will live through the night at Camp Bloodshed?

Script Poker is cardgame with letters played like Texas Hold'em. Five consonant-cards are in the hands of players, five vowel-cards on the table common to all players. 
The players bet and then a fourth vowel-card is drawn on the table, The players bet again and the fifth vowel-card is drawn, Players bet last time. 
The player whose word has the most consonants win all bets. 
The richiest player at the end of the game is the winner.

Objective: gain as many winning points as possible by lobbing/tugging your donkey into target/shed
 - acting simultaniously - 
game can be played with teams - or round robin
1. place the donkeys on one side of the table
2. starting at the same time each player pulls (very carefully) on a string to move his/her donkey towards the shed
3. construction of the donkey often leads to tumble over - if you haste
4. depending on previous agreed arrangements you may 'rebound' or start again
remarks:
publishing date around mid 1930's

In this educational game 3 to 6 players roll a die and move their pawns along a track through ancient Europe (and the Mediterranean , answering questions to collect coins as rewards.

The goal is to have the most coins by the time the game ends, which happens when the bank runs out of coins.

The main purpose of the game is to teach players about ancient Rome.

In this game players have basis with some pieces and they must hold a position indicated with special spinner in the time of an hourglass.

Who slips up and let the pieces fall from the basis is removed from the game. Winner is the last player in the game.

Objective: move your 5 balls to the outer circle; beaten balls are pushed down - try to keep your very last ball(s) alive

Each turn:

     Each player starts moving through the inner circle with 5 balls; as long as the balls are hit by others they must be moved back to their starting point;
     As soon as they moved all around the inner circle balls are shifted (transition points) into the outer circle 
     Hunt all other balls - hitting them they are pushed down delivering winning points to the very last remaining player 


The title has a deeper meaning, too: "to be rolling in the aisles"

Third Edition Released!Rule as Royal Regent within the powerful countries of Medieval Europe, govern her ancient cities, control her trade guilds, command her armies, and sway her religious faithful in Rivaling Royals™, a game for two to five players.

Evade ruthless adversaries while manipulating the powers of Royal Privilege, the Sways of Religion, the Stimulus of Trade, and the Deadly Forces of the Military. Work to create an alliance of cities, trade guilds, and militant nobility, or -- try to undermine the power of your rivals plundering their territories, vanquishing their troops, and usurping the prerogative powers of state for yourself.

Inspire, deceive, trade, and fight your way to fortune and fame as you attempt to reign over the medieval countries of Europe!

Rivaling Royals™ includes:


             20 Trade Guide Cards
             16 Medieval European City Cards
             16 Troops Cards
             15 Royalty Cards
             6 Event Cards (Invasion, Reformation, The Plague)
             4 Regent Cards (France, England, Spain, Scotland)
             4 Espionage Cards
             4 Execution Cards
             2 Letters Patent Cards
             2 Assassin Cards
             And everything else you need to play
        
    


Pursue religious and temporal powers by using your Royal Family to capture the titles of Bishop, King, or Queen in England, Scotland, Spain, and France, transforming your Royal House into the Supreme Sovereign of Western Europe.

Can you evade the dagger of Assassins, the treacherous edicts of Rivaling Royalty, govern ancient medieval cities with their contentious Trade Guilds, and seize the ultimate title of Regent over the most powerful countries of Medieval Western Europe? Find out in Rivaling Royals™ of Europe!

In Dino-Duell two players each control 12 dinosaurs, with four dinosaurs each of three different types.

The dinosaurs start the game in the back rows of a 6x7 game board, and on a turn, a player moves a dinosaur horizontally, vertically or diagonally to a free field or to a field with an opponent's dinosaur; this last move is possible, however, only if prior to the move those two dinosaurs are separated by exactly one dinosaur that belongs to the capturing player and if all three dinosaurs involved in the capture are of different types. Landing on an opponent's dinosaur this way removes that token from the board.

The first player to place four of his dinosaurs adjacent in the river that runs through the middle of the game board wins.

The beginning of the XVI century was of great transformations in the world, previously unknown lands were discovered by European explorers adventurers ... The New World! More than "discover" it was necessary "conquest" and "colonize" ... Live as one of the early settlers who had the mission to transform the Island of Vera Cruz in what we know today as Brazil!

Vera Cruz is a board game for 2-4 players created by Julio Trois, whose theme is the colonization of Brazil. Each player is a Portuguese colonizer who must establish his colony, planting sugar cane, catechizing or enslaving Indians, to be the 1st to build the Great Mill!

The mechanics are worker placement, management and trading resources, and variable phase order.

Duck Pins is a bowling game that was created by J Pressman early in the 1900's. Duck pin bowling is a game that is played in the state of Rhode Island and is somewhat similar to the Candlepin Bowling that is done in the Northeast, especially Massachusetts and New Hampshire. Each frame consists of 3 balls and not just two. Unlike Candlepin bowling where the fallen pins stay on the lane as "deadwood" which can be used to help or hinder a second or third shot, in Duck Pins the pins are cleared from the lane before the next shot is taken. One can score a STRIKE, a SPARE or a TEN (3 balls to knock all the pins down!)

4 card extension for Startup Fever available on Board Game Geek. The following 4 cards are included: BOOM, CRASH, HOUSE RULES and BOARD GAME GEEK. Multiple BGG extensions can be added to Startup Fever.

The centerpiece, the BOARD GAME GEEK card is exclusively available as part of this set on Board Game Geek and gives the owner of the game in game benefits of one money - because (s)he supported

BOOM/CRASH change the rules of the game for the duration of one year

HOUSE RULES allows the owner of the game to write up their own event card.

boardgame: The Big Idea: La Science-Fiction Médiévale
description: The Big Idea: La Science-Fiction Médiévale is the first (French) expansion pack (called "Genius pack 1") for The Big Idea. Out of the box, you'll find 80 new cards about Sci-Fi and Medieval times that will change the way you were creating your inventions and add a whole new dimension to the game.

boardgame: Ravensburger Spiele Minis: Was bin ich?
description:
"Spiele Minis" is a series of small surprise boxes (i.e., you don't know what you get) from Ravensburger. They are meant to be carry-on or gifts, suitable for children ("Mitbringspiel"). Some of the cubes contain actual games, others contain magic tricks, puzzles etc.

"Was bin ich?" ("What am I"?) is a small deduction game, comparable to "What's My Line?". The active player straps a card to his forehead and guesses the depicted item, with a maximum of 10 questions. The inactive player(s) answer with yes or no. The first player to score 5 cards wins the game.

The box contains 20 cards and a blue headband.

boardgame: A Touch of Evil: The Coast
description: A Touch of Evil: The Coast is packed full of new material for A Touch of Evil including four exciting new Heroes, a new full size game board of the fog-shrouded coastline to add to the main Shadowbrook board, new Town Elders, and a host of new mysteries to be solved. The Coast also features tons of new game cards including decks for new Locations to explore, The Smuggler's Cove, Lighthouse, a haunted Shipwreck, and the long cursed village of Tidewater; and much more! With four fearsome new Villains to hunt like The Ghost Ship, Siren, or the dreaded Dreamweaver, the Heroes must fend off deadly curses and contend with all manner of creatures from the dark depths to keep evil at bay, forcing it back into the icy waters that break along the rocky shores of The Coast.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
"Spiele Minis" is a series of small surprise boxes (i.e., you don't know what you get) from Ravensburger. They are meant to be carry-on or gifts, suitable for children ("Mitbringspiel"). Some of the cubes contain actual games, others contain magic tricks, puzzles etc.

"Bockspringen" ("Leapfrog") is a simple dice-rolling racing game for 2 players. Each player gets a set of 2 sheep (black or white) and leap-frogs his way to the finishing line. Each player rolls a d6 in turn and leapfrogs his sheep a number of times equal to the die result. The first player crossing the finishing line wins the game.

The box contains 2 black and 2 white wooden sheep, 2 blue wooden bars (start and finishing line), and a die.

"Spiele Minis" is a series of small surprise boxes (i.e., you don't know what you get) from Ravensburger. They are meant to be carry-on or gifts, suitable for children ("Mitbringspiel"). Some of the cubes contain actual games, others contain magic tricks, puzzles etc.

"Piraten-Schatz" ("Pirate's Treasure"?) is a small roll-and-move game where players compete to collect the most treasure cards. First, a circular board is laid out randomly using the treasure cards (containing treasures in the range from 1-6). Players take turns rolling a d6 and moving the pirate ship clockwise along the treasure cards. When the ship stops at a card, the player may take it if his die result is equal or higher compared to the treasure card. Alternatively, the player may place the conch on the treasure card and roll a second time, potentially getting a second treasure card (or none at all!). The player with the most treasure cards in the end wins the game.

The box contains 21 treasure cards, a wooden pirate ship, a small plastic conch and a die.

The Spiral Ball is a game of balance. The large ramp gradually gets the balls placed by the players according to dice. When a ball is placed, it may cause to land some other balls placed below.

In his turn player roll the 2 dice, number die indicates number of balls player may place on the spirale and color die indicates the section where the balls must be placed. But be careful of placing the marbles because it may cause the tumble and some of the marbles may roll away from the spirale.

The first player who places all his marbles on the spiral will be the winner.

boardgame: Smithsonian Mission Museum: Case of the Missing Mummy
description:
From the Publisher:

A Mummy is on the loose in the museum! Your mission is to work together to reveal the spell that will stop the mummy from escaping into the city. As a team, you’ll decode secret messages, answer questions and complete puzzles to lead you to the spell that returns the mummy back to its case.

- Track the mummy using teamwork to collect the three artifacts on your mission card!
- Encourages positive teamwork and cooperative play
- Learn about Smithsonian’s coolest artifacts along the way!

Includes:
300 Challenge Cards
8 Mission Cards
16 Artifact Tiles
5 Movers
1 Red Revealer
1 Timer
1 Pad of Paper
1 Pencil
1 Set of Rules

From Publisher:

Is that Smithsonian fact unbelievably true or outrageously false? Sometimes it’s hard to tell the difference! Each player takes turns writing down the correct answer to a question, along with one they made up. Everyone guesses which answer they believe is a fact and which one is fiction. The player who gets most tokens by guessing correctly or bluffing the best wins!

- It’s a trivia game with a twist. You don’t have to have the correct answer just the most believable one!
- Fascinating facts and pictures accompany each question
Rewards creative thinking and creative writing

Includes:
290 Cards
60 Tokens
1 Answer Pad
6 Pencils
1 Set of Rules

From the Publisher:

Which animal will win the race? Open the magical Scanimation door to see who will move on each round. You’ll be strutting, galloping, and fluttering along as you race around the track. Guess correctly to win! Based on the popular book by Rufus Butler Seder.

- A first game for preschoolers just learning to play games
- Players can hop, fly or run, just like the animals do!
- Each player takes a turn opening the Scanimation door to see which animal goes next.

Includes:
1 Game Board with Magical Scanimation Door
2 Dice
5 Animal Movers
4 Guess Tokens
1 Set of Rules

Der Weg zum Ziel ein deutsches Spiel (subtitle)
Objective: be the first finishing the chosen travel route
 - moving by turn - 
1  each player chooses a travel route (blue, brown, green, red)
(each route touches 28 cities and 10 'bad luck' places)
1a with more than 4 players routes should be used backwards, too
2  roll the 2 dice (three times) - if the total result shows a number you need to enter a city/place ==> move forward; if not, don't move
3  ending on a place with 'bad luck' follow instructions printed cover inside

remarks:
game was published ~ 1936/8
a few years later ~ 1940 an updated version has been published ==> 'Auf der Reichsautobahn durch Großdeutschland' (Neu-Ausgabe)

Der Weg zum Ziel ein deutsches Spiel (subtitle)
Objective: be the first finishing the chosen travel route
 - moving by turn - 
1  each player chooses a travel route (blue, brown, green, red)
(each route touches 28 cities and 10 'bad luck' places)
1a with more than 4 players routes should be used backwards, too
2  roll the 2 dice (three times) - if the total result shows a number you need to enter a city/place ==> move forward; if not, don't move
3  ending on a place with 'bad luck' follow instructions printed cover inside
