a few years before ~ 1936/8 a predecessor version had been published ==> http://boardgamegeek.com/boardgame/116974/auf-der-reichsautobahn
differences: borders are changed, Austria included, meeples are replaced by pewter cars ==> Fröha, Hilpoltstein (German producer of high quality meeples)

Phineas and Ferb is an unreleased LEGO Games set that was originally scheduled for release in 2012. Its LEGO set number would have been #3868. It was based on the Disney Channel TV Series "Phineas and Ferb". LEGO dropped the entire LEGO Games product line before this game was released, so it was cancelled. However, the assembly instructions were made available online.

Step Game is for very young players. The goal is to bring the first of his play the other side of the course. One piece consists of two balls attached by a cord. Player roll the color dice and advance his game piece one or the other side to a plot of the color indicated.

boardgame: ABC DRS3: Das Radiokultspiel für unterwegs
description:
Travel board game version of Swiss Radio Station DRS 3's call-in game show ABC DRS 3.

Gameplay involves a Candidate player being asked to provide a word beginning with a known letter in response to a definition. The more words the Candidate can correctly identify within 60 seconds, the higher their score.

Players take turns as Moderator, Listener and Candidate during the game.

The game's question and answer content is in Swiss German. The box details and instructions are in Standard German.

The game comes with

100 question cards
6 microphone cards
1 hourglass
1 20-sided die

boardgame: ABC DRS 3 Das Radio Kultspiel-jetzt auf Hochdeutsch
description:
Standard German version of the Switzerland radio's ABC DRS 3 call-in word game. (The original game is in Swiss German.)

The Candidate player has 45 seconds to provide a word (first letter is known) in response to a definition read aloud by the Moderator. The Listener players wager on how many correct answers the Candidate can provide.

Game comes with

200 question cards with 4000 questions
14 wager cards
6 microphone cards
1 45-second alarm clock
1 scorepad

Question cards are combinable with ABC DRS 3 Original and ABC DRS 3 für unterwegs.

A trivia game with a tactical component and direct confrontation (using cards to buy other questions/cards, stealing cards from other players).

The game consists of 300 question cards in five categories: geography & history, humanities & arts, science & technology, economy & politics, entertainment & sports.

To win the game, a player has to collect one card from each of the five categories. Each of the cards contain two or three questions. To win a card, a player has to answer all the questions (except for the "guesstimation" one) on a card.

The cards are shuffled and two cards are placed, face up, on the table. The front side shows the card's category and what types of questions it contains. Questions come in different levels of difficulty, and there are yes/no questions and "guesstimation" questions where a number has to be guessed. The active player chooses one of the two cards. If they can answer all the questions on it, they win the card, if not, the card is removed from the game. Then the pool is filled up so that there are two cards again, and it's the next player's turn.

A player can have more than one card from the same category. These extra cards can be used to avoid having to answer one particular question, or three from the same category can be exchanged for one from a different category.

For questions marked as difficult, the active player can ask the questioner for a possible answer (which the questioner has to make up), turning it into a yes/no question. Not doing so, however, has an advantage as well: If a player can answer a difficult question without help, they earn an additional turn.

Special rule for the "guesstimation" questions (only when three or more are playing): Instead of trying for a new card from the pool, the active player can decide to "attack" a player of his/her choice. The next player not involved in the attack reads the guesstimation question from the top card of the card stack. The attacked player has to state a number, then the attacker states his/her estimation. The player whose guess is closest to the correct answer may steal a card of his/her choice from the other player; that card now counts as one of their own.

The game ends as soon as one player has won one card from each of the five categories.

Shield Wall is a low complexity wargame on the Battle of Hastings. The game uses traditional hex-and-counter mechanics to simulate the combat and emphasizes the ineffectual assault of Norman archers, the combination of Norman foot and cavalry units, and the defensive strength of the Saxon shield wall. The game also highlights the difficulty that the Saxon leaders keeping the shield wall intact. Saxon Units may involuntarily pursuit the Normans down the hill and lose their high-ground advantage after successfully causing the Norman units to route.

The game features rules for demoralizing of the armies, rallying the troops, and Saxon reinforcements. Also included are optional rules for army fatigue which increases the difficulty for both players.

The Saxon Player wins by holding the hill or eliminating the Norman king, William. The Norman player wins by taking the hill or killing the Saxon king, Harold. Either player can win by eliminating all units.

The game features about 147 counters and a 22x17 inch gameboard.

Red State/ Blue State is a new family board game that has political overtones.  The goal is to win 270 electoral votes before your opponent so that you can send your candidate to the White House.  You do this by answering question about all things concerning the United States.  The game board features a map of the United States with the American flag superimposed over it and shows the states' boundaries.  Let's say that you grabbed a Georgia card.  You would place your token, either Daniel David Donkey- Democrat, or Edward Elephant-Republican, on Georgia and answer the question.  If you are correct, you win the electoral votes for Georgia.  Then, you would place a Georgia-shaped piece that is either red(Republican) or blue(Democrat) on Georgia to show that you won Georgia's electoral votes.  Your opponent would take a turn and you continue this until one of you reaches 270 electoral votes.  The questions range from American history to pop culture.  It's a fast-paced American trivia game.

boardgame: Championship Horse Racing    Sport of Kings
description:
This is a horse race simulation board game.  In this game a roll of a D6 is referenced to a chart to determine the movement of each horse.  Each horse has a separate card with its movement chart thereon. The charts have three rows of numbers, each for a different section of the track. Turn order is by lane in the first round and then till the finish, turn order is by leader. Lane changes are permitted only as the very first move of a pawn (horse) on its turn.  The first lane change is allowed as a forward diagonal movement of the pawn.  Additional lane changes in a turn are at a cost of one movement point each,  So, if you have five movement points and make two lane changes as the first two movements of your pawn then you can move four spaces forward along the track. There are areas on the track where lane changes are prohibited.  
   A bit of statistical analysis was made by Cincinnati native Howard Hughes in creating the horse cards.  The horse cards reflect the performance of real horses in that those that excel in sprints fair poorly in long distance races and vice versa.  There is no provision for betting in this game.  The components are somewhat primitive, consisting of; horse movement cards, pawns and coin money all punched from a perforated sheet of card stock.  The box art has a beautiful charcoal drawing of a jockey on his horse making their way to the starting gate.

Flinke Flieger is a racing game played in rounds between two teams (or two players). Each round, two players (one from each team) race each other, trying to deliver an egg to their team's nest.

To set up the round, each player receives a seagull on a stick, which he has to hold by the stick during the race. The seagulls have a backpack, in which a flat paper egg is placed. Now, a spinner is spun, determining the special conditions for this round: it may be a straightforward race, or there may be special conditions, such as the players being required to run backwards or to spin twice before delivering the egg.

If a player drops his egg, he is out of the race for that round. If both players succesfully deliver their eggs, the fastest player's team receives an additional egg.

The game continues until fewer than three undelivered eggs remain at the beginning of a round. Now, players inspect their eggs by flipping them over to see which ones "hatch". (Those that hatch have a chick painted on the back.) The team with the most chicks wins.

boardgame: Twilight Struggle: "Anni di Piombo" Promo Card
description:
A promo card published by Asterion for the release of the Italian Edition of Twilight Struggle

Card number "111 Optional"

US event - Mid War - 1 ops

"if there is at least one nation in Western Europe not Controlled by USSR, remove from that nation all USSR Influence but 1 (if there more than one nation not Controlled by USSR, the USA player choose the affected nation)

Remove from play if used as event.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The game contains four player boards with images of different environments (concert hall, countryside, kindergarten, street), 32 tiles with images of things making sounds and compact disc.

When player hears the sound corresponding to his environment he must find the tile presenting the source of the sound. Sounds are played in random order until one player will gather eight tiles.
In the second variant players listen to the sounds and catch the tiles no matter which environment they are related to. Game ends when all tiles are taken. Player with most of tiles wins.

Promotional game published by the National Refining Co., to advertise the brand name En-Ar-Co Motor oil plus White Rose gasoline.

A simple roll and move game with spaces good and bad, and a shortcut of course.

They also supplied a checker's board on the reverse of the Auto Game board.

See Also:

     En-Ar-Co Automobile Tour A similar 1919 game also issued by En-Ar-Co, with a different track.

For Motherland! is a miniatures game without miniatures, which enables players to fight platoon-level combined arms engagements during WW II.
There will be various types of combat arms. Such as tanks, artillery, AT self-propelled guns, trucks and many more.

Players takes role of officers and leading their platoons towards victory thru different types of objectives. It can be sturm, rescue mission, covert operations behind enemy lines etc. There will be mission generator in future expansions.

Every unit in the game has its own characteristics. 
Such as: military drill, speed, attributes, etc.
For officers there will be one more characteristic: Respect. If you are new officer, you must earn Respect from your soldiers if you want command them better.

Everything that players need for a game will be in a core game.

Promotional item made to advertise the merits of using Peter Pan bread. It is a simple roll and move game along a track, but it does have a little originality in the shortcuts or detours available. You start at the Peter Pan Ranch and must get to the Range Rider Stockade.

Linked to a TV series.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Peter Pan

description:
This edition is nearly the same as UNO, except with cards having artwork showing characters from Glee.

Cards that are different from traditional UNO:

Sabotage card -- allows you to select an opponent whose hand you wish to view, then allows you to swap a card you don't want with one you desire from the hand of the opponent you selected
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

description:
Spectacular Tumble is a two-player game for the Shibumi set.

Play begins with a full pyramid of balls, two of which are black scoring balls and the rest evenly divided between the players' colors.

Players Red and White take turns removing balls but they may not remove the scoring balls or leave any empty position in the bottom 4x4 layer of the pyramid.

The game ends when there is only a single 4x4 layer of balls; a player scores points when balls of her color are adjacent to the scoring balls.

From the product page (http://victorypointgames.com/details.php?prodId=189):

For years we've pondered the question: "Why did the chicken cross the road?" Well, never fear, because our very own VPG designer, Nathan Hansen, has the answer! WHY?! is a 1 to 5 player game featuring the perfectly normal, seemingly sane chickens, running headlong into traffic, causing a massive 42 car pileup! We ask you again, WHY?! What could have possibly been so important?

Each turn, players draw a card and follow the instructions to move their chicken, and either move current cars on the board or draw new ones into a random lane! Anything can happen to you as you cross this dangerous road to get to the other side.

A quick, easy to learn game, designed for children but challenging for all players!

Game Data:
Complexity: 1.5 on a 9 scale
Solitaire Suitability: 8.5 on a 9 scale
Scale: Each player plays a chicken attempting to cross the road and avoid incoming traffic

Changes from First Edition:

• All components upgraded to Gold Banner standards, including a mounted map, laser-cut counters, and full-sized poker cards.
• Rules booklet reformatted and some rules changed concerning car movement

What's in the Box?:

• One 8.5" x 11" highway game map
• One 4-page, full-color Rules booklet
• 40 thick, multi-shaped, laser-cut game pieces (minor assembly required)
• 25 Event cards
• One 8.5" x 11" mounted, jigsaw-cut game map
• One bright red, 9 1/4" x 5 7/8" Standard cardboard VPG game box
• One beautiful box cover sleeve
• One "Wipes-A-Lot" napkin
• One charcoal dessicant packet

boardgame: Jamie Swise Mystery Games: Who Killed Lowell Weenaman?
description:
Mystery party game for 3 male and 3 female characters.

Local television personality Lowell Weenaman, better known as Captain Weenie, was found murdered last night at Readenweep Racetrack.  Mr. Weenaman was making a personal appearance, in costume, as part of the track's season opening festivities.  The Captain Weenie Show, which totally lost its school age audience years ago, had become the rage of the age 25 to 45 crowd.  While scratchy cartoons and local magicians provided the bulk of the show's content, the program took on grotesque proportions when Weenaman peddled his Captain Weenie VCR Covers and Weenamagic, a do-it-all food processor.

This is a role playing game for 6 players, great for a fun evening.  The suspect characters include three local T.V. news personalities, a female sulky driver, a state racing board official, and a track character named Paddock Pete.  Cast yourself and friends in these roles and play to learn who murdered this most unusual nostalgia act.

The Time: December 1, 1985   The Place: Galesburg, OH

Note: This game includes an audio cassette tape which, although not mandatory, is played at the start to set the mood.

Auction was first published in Abbott's New Card Games; a significantly revised version called Auction 2002 appeared in GAMES and in a self-published booklet.

Players have got to bid poker combinations for cards in an open line in the middle of the table - with these cards they should improve their hand and get the best possible poker combination for when the round ends.

Each turn a card is drawn from the pile and added to the line, afterwards there's an open auction - the cards in the highest combination are exchanged for the last cards on the line. The remaining cards on the line dictate the score at the end of the round.

The winner is the player with the highest score after four rounds or the first one to achieve 100 points.

Cosmic Court is a children's game based off of the Warner Brothers movie Space Jam starring Michael Jordan and the Looney Tunes Cartoon Characters.  You play as Michael Jordan, spinning and moving around the 3D board.  This game could be classified as a low dexterity game, as you attempt to make "shots" by throwing a small purple basketball at the court to make a basket.

Tenka Fubu is a diplomatic game on the Japanese Sengoku period. This game was designed for beginners.

The map includes only the central part of Honshu. The game concept is based on Feudal Lord but has simplier rules.

The players will control one of the six feudal lords. Each player conducts diplomacy and military operations. The Battles are resolved by a fire power combat system, in which fortress affect battle results. Also random events have various impact on the game play.

The game winner is determined by the total wealth of controled provinces.


The first edition was combined with Tanks and The Battle of the Bulge in one box. The product name was "Simulation games for beginner #1".

The second edition is Wargame Japanese History #28 game.

2nd edition contents:

    1 rule book
    1 map
    2 countersheets
    1 play sheet


[Number of players] 2 to 6 people
[Play time] About 60-120 minutes



"Tenka Fubu" (天下布武) was Oda Nobugana's seal. It has several meanings: "spread martial arts virtues in the world", but also often understood as "take over the world by force". 
https://ja-m-wikipedia-org.translate.goog/wiki/%E7%B9%94%E7%94%B0%E4%BF%A1%E9%95%B7?_x_tr_sl=ja&_x_tr_tl=en&_x_tr_hl=fr&_x_tr_pto=wapp#%E6%94%BF%E7%AD%96
天下 under the sky / the world
布 spread / take over
武

A baseball game that encourages people to smoke whilst playing.

It is a game that you play with a lit cigarette. It has pieces of paper on which you guess if the play will be a single, double, triple or out. When the center of the paper that has a baseball field printed on it is touched with a lit cigarette the paper burns and I assume somehow t is an indicator of whether the play is an out, single, double or triple.

The fact that one or two of these games have survived is a miracle.

This is an expansion kit for Feudal Lord. It requires that game and cannot be played alone.
This kit adds 257 new historical leaders, 7 special leaders, markers as well as units for 8 new clans, extended rules, 3 historical campaign scenarios and many FAQs to Feudal Lord.

Ziunta is a trivia game with questions - answers about the Soule, part of the Basque country in France.

Each player is one of the typical character of the world of Soule and shall, for each person, together six items belonging to his character.

For this, the player navigate with his character on the game board, representing the map of the Soule rolling the dice, and answering the questions.

The questions relate to the Soule and offer three choices each time. They can be read in French (front) or Basque (back). In addition, on each card, there is a small comment about local culture.

With correct answers players continue his move and thus increase its chances of hitting the boxes to win needed objects.

The first player to fill the carton with 6 objects wins.

boardgame: Feudal Lord extension leader counter sheet Nr.1
description:
This is expansion kit for Feudal Lord.
Add new 100 historical leader counters to the game.

This extension counter is designed for Sunset games's second edition. But can fits into the Epoch's first edition.

A quick chess-like game for two players.  Each player controls a small team of Pokemon and tries to take out the enemy Victini (king piece).  The pieces have power ratings like Stratego, but a weaker/equal attacker gets to draw a "plus power card" in hopes of luckily winning the fight.  A piece is flipped over to its reverse side (which usually has weaker power) when damaged once, and destroyed when damaged a second time.  Lost pieces count towards the cost needed to summon the legendary Pokemon, impressive 2x2 pieces with high power but mediocre movement.  The Victini is protected by its egg, which can absorb three hits.

Yakari wants to deliver a secret message in the form a feather to each of his friends. However, a wolf has spotted Yakari and is following him. Though Yakari isn't sure whether the wolf is dangerous, it'll be better if he manages to avoid him.

Yakari: Geheime Botschaft is played on a board with ten fields placed in a circle: eight of the fields show one of Yakari's friends, and the other two show a tipi. At the beginning of the game, a Yakari pawn is placed on one of the tipis, and a Wolf pawn on the other tipi. Each player receives his own set of feathers to deliver.

The players now take turns moving Yakari. They spin a spinner, which will show a number, an eagle or a wolf.

If the spinner shows a number, the player moves the Yakari pawn that many spaces, either clockwise or counter-clockwise. (Yakari cannot move onto the space with the wolf, so the player must choose the other direction for movement in this case.) If Yakari ends his move on a friend for which the player has not yet delivered a feather, he now delivers one. If Yakari ends his move on a tipi, the player may move the wolf pawn to any field.

If the spinner shows the eagle, the player may move Yakari to any unoccupied field. Likewise, if the spinner shows the wolf, the player does not move Yakari, but may move the wolf to any unoccupied field.

The first player to deliver all seven of his feathers wins.

Objective: win most money if dice show 2 or 3 matching symbols
 - acting by turn - 
1. each player buys in - pays same amount of money into the bank
2. each player's individual wager is put onto the board   
3. active player rolls the 3 d6 dice (numbers/colours/symbols): 
if 2 of them show correspondent tops (the right way up ==> see board image) you'll get 12 times the money you bet; 3 matching tops: 36 times
no matching top: bank wins
game stops after agreed time or if bank goes bankrupt
remarks:
game was published ~ 1920

Horse-themed dexterity/action game for children.

Components: one metal tin, one cardboard chit, 40 cards

The first player to collect 6 cards wins the game. The cards are shuffled and placed face down on the table. The active player puts the metal tin on or next to the cards, compresses its lid and places the chit on the lid. Then the lid is compressed at the sides, which catapults the chit into the air. If the chit doesn't land on one of the cards, the active player selects a card to be returned to the box and it's the next player's turn.
If the chit does land on a card, the active player turns that card over and does what the card requires: text-based cards are read aloud and the player tries to complete the task (e.g. pretending to be an excited horse, making a few steps on all fours with a book on one's back). If he/she is successful, he/she earns that card. Other cards are earned by being the fastest to grab the tin or the chit. If the chit lands on the tin, the active player gets to steal a card from another player.

The game ends when one player has collected 6 cards.

boardgame: Krimi Total Fall 8: Die Legende der Sturmklinge
description:
General info on "Krimi Total": Murder mystery game in which players act out their role and try to solve the mystery.

Info on this particular case: This case takes place in a fantasy setting.
Nemetor, a defeated dark warlock that has been believed dead is raising an army of the undead. The western kingdoms are in danger! Their only hope is the "Sturmklinge", a powerful sword that has been kept in the stronghold of the "Fishers" since the last war. The Fishers are a group of scholars that collect knowledge and secrets to safeguard them in their fortress. The "Council of the Nine" is getting together to decide who shall take the "Sturmklinge" into the battle against Nemetor. But at night a body is found, and one of the Fishers has disappeared. Have Nemetor's henchmen already reached the Fishers' stronghold? Do the want to stop the Council? Or is there a traitor among the Council members? This is what the players have to find out.

In "Die Legende der Sturmklinge" there are 8 obligatory roles (can't be played with less than 8): 4 male, 4 female. One optional role, the scholar and host (a Fisher), can be either male or female. The roles include typical fantasy characters: an elf, a barbarian, a merchant, a knight, a priestess, a mage, an amazon and a dwarf.

Each player has strips of images of different sizes that will position on the board in order to match his illustrations, overlapping with those already in place on the game board.
The winner is the one with the fewer images on hand at the end of the game where the deck is exhausted..

Baseball Card Game "Club-House Baseball". Invented by an ardent Canadian fan and fine tuned by Ernie Harwell, Hall Of Fame announcer and the voice of WJR Detroit and the Detroit Tigers, contains every conceivable play in baseball.

Two decks 69 home team (white) and 69 visiting team (blue) plus 13 pinch hit cards (red) are the cards you need to play 9 innings of baseball in 10-15 minutes.

RULES:
Shuffle the two decks separately and place face down. Visiting Team (blue) turns over cards until there are three out. Home Team White) repeats this procedure until one inning is completed. If for instance, a "stolen base", "passed ball", etc. is turned up with no runners o base it is disregarded.

S1 and D1 cards simply advance a runner an extra base as compared to to ordinary singles and doubles.

Pinch Hit cards can be used only from the seventh inning until the end of the game. They cannot be used with bases empty. They must be re-shuffled after each use.

Pitching Change cards - Defensive team has the option of changing pitchers. If accepted, each team must re-shuffle all cards and discards in own deck.

READ EACH CARD. Every game situation is covered.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A children's game that promotes colour recognition, attention and fine motor skills.

Components:
- 1 wooden shirt
- 16 wooden buttons
- 1 wooden needle with thread
- 1 spinner
- 10 winning chips

The active player spins the spinner that is shaped like a pair of scissors. The player chooses one of the two colours selected by the spinner and gets a button in the corresponding colour. If the player already has a button of the same colour he/she is allowed to sew it onto the wooden shirt and receives a winning chip. The first player to collect three chips wins the game.

In Count Me In! – aka, 1, 2, 3 ... ganz viele! ("1, 2, 3 ... lots!") – players work together to try to lay down cards in the right order. If they complete two rows of ten cards each, they win.

At the start of the game, shuffle the deck of fifty cards – five cards each with numbers 1-10, with ten different images appearing as pips on the cards: horseshoes, mushrooms, springs, and so on – and deal five cards to each player.

On a turn, a player must lay down a card, if possible. Cards must be laid in numerical order, so the first card in a row must be 1, the second card 2, and so on. At the same time, the same image cannot be placed into a row twice. If a player can legally play a card, she does so, then draws a replacement from one of the two draw stacks; if she can't, she lays a card aside out of the game, then draws a new card. (The cards have color-coded icons on their backs – red, blue, etc. – which can aid players in deciding which card to draw.)

If the players create two rows of cards numbered 1-10 before both decks run out and they can't play any more cards, they win; if not, they lose.

A competitive variant is included in 1, 2, 3 ... ganz viele! with players taking cards numbered 1-7 from two colors (e.g., red and blue), then laying them out in two rows. On a turn, a player turns over one of the number 1 cards, then one of the number 2s, and so on. If she reveals a card that shows an image already present on a card revealed earlier, her turn ends and she flips all cards face-down again. If a player reveals cards numbered 1-7 without revealing matching images, she wins the round. The first player to win three rounds wins.

boardgame: The Magical World of Disney Epic Card Game
description:
What if Buzz Lightyear met the Seven Dwarfs?

The idea is pretty basic:

A storyline is laid out before you. It is your job, as Author of your own story, to populate it with characters. Each character's vitality is worth so many points to the story. At the end of the game, the Author whose characters have the greatest total vitality wins the game.

Sounds simple enough? But it can't be that easy. It isn't.

In The Magical World of Disney Epic Card Game, you're sharing your storyline with other Authors. There may even be multiple storylines at once. Characters come and go from various locations, each having a different effect on the story. The locations themselves might even change right out from under the feet of your characters.

Characters might be unexpectedly shifted around. Objects and other characters can affect the story. Not to mention Effects, Events, and Spells that can change everything in a blink of an eye.

You could have Snow White and Cinderella go up against the Queen of Hearts and Cruella DeVille. The Seven Dwarfs could even team up with the evil Jafar to battle The Hunchback of Notre Dame with Lightning McQueen.

You can download everything for FREE! All the files you'll need are available in PDF format.

Four cards is placed in the middle of the players with the different color lines.
In his turn player must place one of the three card from his hand on the table so that it follows the line and the sequence of decreasing or increasing numbers with the other cards on the table. If player can't play a card he draws a new card, places it if possible or passes. The game ends if any player gets rid of his cards. He is the winner.

Players place the cards on the game board following the spiral from inside to outside provided that the background color of the card matches with color on the board, and the illustration is not already horizontally or vertically on the same row.

Player who has the fewer cards in hand at the end of the game, when the deck is exhausted is the winner.

A game of manipulation and concentration.
The goal is to earn as much Bojonautes cards as possible by building a tower made ​​up of plates and modules that are in balance!

In his turn player places a star board or module on the star board until the demanded number of modules are on this board.
If the player places the new star board he wins 1 Bojonautes card. If the player places the last board of last module succesfully wins 2 Bojonautes card.
If the tower breaks then... Bobadaboum the party continues from the start.
When all Bojonautes cards have been won the game ends.  Who has the greates number of these cards wins the game.

Contents:

     9 Bojonautes cards
     4 "star" boards
     16 modules

9 plates (all except the treasures) are placed face down on the game board.
In his turn player turns one plate face-up. If it is island or treasure hunter he turns a second plate face-up. If the second is also island or treasure hunter so that they form a couple (island and treasure hunter) he wins a treasure to be place in front of him. Otherwise he turns the plates back to face-down.
If he finds a shark he removes it from the game.
If he finds a telescope he may watch one plate not showing it to others.
If he finds a pirate he may steal a treasure from his opponent to aim and shoot his treasury with cannonball.

The game is over if all treasures have been found.
The player of the greates number of treasures wins.
If tied the cannonball firing decides the winner.

Contents:

     1 wooden game board with 3x3 grid
     22 wooden plates representing: 7 treasures, 4 islands, 4 treasure hunters, 3 pirates, 2 sharks, 2 pirate telescopes
     1 cannon and three cannonballs

A children's game that uses the Toystick (http://www.toystick.de/), an electronic device that has to be acquired separately and can be used with a variety of books and games. When used with a compatible book or game, the stick reads texts or plays music.

"Die Schatzkammer von El Mirador" is the first game for the Toystick. The players are adventurers searching for treasures in the legendary cave of El Mirador. They have to avoid hidden traps, and whoever collects the most treasures wins the game.

The Toystick functions as a random movement generator that tells the players how many spaces they can move on the board. It also tells them when they have hit one of the hidden traps, which ends the current player's turn. The board has special spaces for treasure markers. If a player reaches a treasure space with a suitable treasure (as marked on one of the treasure maps), they earn that treasure. Another element in the game is the guardian, which the players move to one of the guardian spaces when they've collected a treasure. All player tokens in that segment of the board are returned to the pyramid in the center of the board. Equipment chits can be used to escape from traps or the guardian, to exchange the position of treasures etc.

The game ends when one player has collected the required number of treasures (6 or 8).

Splastwo is a simple two-player game, playable with the Series: Shibumi game system. It uses the 4×4 board, 15 white balls and 15 black ones.

Rules

The players draw lots to their colour. White colour starts.

The board starts empty. Two players, white and black, take turns placing a single ball, or two balls in a line or three balls in a line – orthogonal, contiguous and at the same level - of their colour, either on empty board holes or stacking on 2×2 platforms of existing balls.

A player scores one point when he completes the first, the second or the third level, and two points when he puts the last ball of the pyramid, thus completing the fourth level.

Players are not allowed to pass and if a player has no legal moves the other player ends the game by placing his remaining balls to complete the pyramid.

The game ends when the board is full with the 30 balls and the winner is the player with the highest score.

The game can’t end in a draw.

Rules for this game are included in the soft cover Shibumi Rule Book

Time to play Cookie Monster! In Kookiz, players want to grab as many different types of cookies as possible, preferably big stacks of cookies to keep them out of the hands of other players.

The deck in Kookiz consists of 80 cards, with eight cards of ten types of cookies. Before starting the game, shuffle the cards, deal each player a hand of four cookies, place four(if there are 2 or 4 players) or five(if there are 3 or 5 players) cookies in the center of the table, then deal out the other cards evenly to form face-down draw stacks for each player. This is a real-time game, there are no player turns. Everyone plays simultaneously at the same time (as fast as they can :D ), and performs one of the following actions:


    You can place two or more cookies of the SAME type in a face-up stack in front of you.
    You can play a single cookie from your hand on top of a card in the center of the table, then place that stack before you. You can only take this action if you are holding ONE copy of the card.
    You can play a single cookie from your hand on top of another player's stack to claim that stack and place it before you. You can only take this action if you are holding ONE copy of the card.


After any action, refill your hand to four cookies, drawing from your own face-down stack. The round ends when a player has played all of her cards; this player scores three bonus points for ending the round. Everyone else scores one point for each type of cookie they have before them.

The player with the highest score after five rounds wins.

"The A-Typical Card Game of B-list Inventions"

Proto-Typical is a "Simply Epic, Free-Market Parody."  Everyone knows that what the world needs now is MORE STUFF! And what's the best kind of STUFF? You guessed it! NEW STUFF! You have just inherited a somewhat successful, multi-national corporation with access to a handful of cutting edge patents that will help you build some AMAZING NEW STUFF! Of course, it just so happens that your friends have also all suddenly (and suspiciously) inherited their own companies as well. Now, you must race to finish and begin production of as many prototypes of NEW STUFF as possible. The inventions are bizarre, corporate espionage is inevitable, and the profit to be made is unfathomable!

Proto-Typical is played with two different decks of cards:

     The Blueprints Deck contains double-faced cards with the invention and its value in millions of Globos (The global currency of the future) on the reverse, and blueprints for the invention on the reverse.  The blueprint also lists component types needed from the Resources Deck.
     The Resources Deck contains single-faced cards, which are either component inventions listed by type, or other resources (Scientists, Venture Capitalists, Celebrity endorsers, Advertising Agencies).


Game Play:

     Player performs one of three actions:
         Obtain resources - Draw one resource card from Resources Deck
         Build a prototype - The player plays all the resources needed for one buildable idea (this may include resources in the public domain and resources from your hand modified by scientists), along with any value enhancing resources. Player takes the buildable idea OBVERSE-SIDE UP and any cards used from the player’s hand and places them as the completed prototype into the player’s asset pool
         Kill an idea - Permanently discard (obsolete) a buildable idea by placing it and one or more resource cards needed for it from player’s hand into player’s assets, REVERSE-SIDE UP.  This is a liability. (Liability amount is the value of the idea less the resources used)
    
     Discard or draw cards so that player has exactly 5 cards
     Reveal blueprint if any are still available so that exactly 3 show.


Ending the Game:

     Play continues until there are no more buildable ideas, i.e. all blueprints have been prototyped or obsoleted.
     Each player then tallies the total of their assets by adding the $G values of completed prototypes (as modified by human resources), $G4 for each resource card and subtracting obsoleted ideas.
     Highest score wins the hand.

Fantasy game related to the french cartoon TV serie called Code Lyoko.

The players need to collaborate in oder to defeat X.A.N.A., a virus that attacks both the real world and the web world.

Codigo Lyoko uses two boards, cards and dice.

The game goal is to find 4 diary parts, belonging to Franz Hopper, lost in the virtual world. These diary fragments are the clue to fight against the supercomputer called X.A.N.A.

But it is not easy because there are waiting for you hundreds of virtual monsters (counters and cards) and dangers. The players, 1-4, have to face these threaths through the board related with the virtual world moving from level to level. There are 5 levels, each one with different landscapes. The last one is the place where Aelita (one of the main characters), should activate a virtual tower using the Lyoko Code in order to came back to the past.

It is important too to take an eye in the real world (second board) because X.A.N.A. is trying to control the heroes friends in order to win.

Summarizing, the players have to fight against monsters and face dangers using dice, looking for the diary parts moving through the board and bringing Aelita to the 5 level in order to came back to the past.

Realm Coins is a small game played with only coins (25 copper, 25 silver, and 25 gold) and a pouch.  It is a blind bidding game where you bid coins to win more coins.  The goal of the game is to be the first player with a stash valued 40 or higher.

There are special abilities on each side of the three different kinds of coins that will influence how players bid.  The copper coin's sides are a peasant and a boar.  The silver coin's sides are a knight and a gryphon.  And, the gold coin's sides are a crown and a wyvern.

Since all players play simultaneously this game can be used to determine who the starting player of a much larger game will be.  Realm Coins can easily be played in just a few minutes.

Presented at Play 2008 in Modena (Italy), Pinguini Contro Vampiri is a very simple game with rules similar to Uno. The game was sponsorized by AVIS (Associazione Volontari Italiani Sangue), the most important Blood Donors association in Italy, and the objective of the game is educate children to the importance of blood groups.

It contains 55 cards: 48 Penguin cards and 7 Vampire cards.

The first player plays a Penguin card, that shows a blood group and all related blood groups. Following player should play a related card (with the same symbol), otherwise comes in Play a Vampire card, that lightly modify the rules of the game.

If one player has no cards, the game ends and all players has won. If the last Vampire is played, the game ends and all players have lost.

boardgame: Cars 2 World Grand Prix Board Game
description:
Race your favorite character from Cars 2, through Tokyo, Porta Corsa or London. Don't spend too much time in the pits and watch out for oil slicks. Every roll of the dice can affect any player - so you never know who will be first over the finish line.

This game should not be confused with the similarly titled Cars 2: World Grand Prix Board Game by Ravensburger or White Castle

Trivia game where players move around the board answering the questions about sports, culture, techincs, history etc.
Correct answers are rewarded with point-cards
Player who accumulates 10 point-cards, must answer the final question to win the game.

Racing game of bikers.
The goal is to complete the first ten laps of the neighborhood on the game board first. In his turn player roll the dice and advance the number indicated. There are some obstacles like : radar, traffic lights, ice etc. and some special cards allow to neutralize these to move at full speed

The goal of the game is during 9 years (9 rounds of the game board) with two kinds of players - the authors and publishers, to compete or join forces to produce a maximum of comic books, and total and maximum points.

The aim of the authors is to acquire a maximum class while providing a minimum of money.
The purpose of publishers is to acquire as much money while ensuring a minimum of Fame.

The game ends when the first of the two editors has nine full turns of the game board and then the scoring begins where authors calculate their royalites, publisher calculate the books sold etc.

This is technically two expansions to Great Western, from Martin Wallace, which was published in Spielbox magazine number 92 (edition 2/2011).  These two are published in Spielbox 6/2011.
It is still a railroad game, but no longer set in southern England. The game objective is still to connect cities in the railroad.  As the names suggest, one side of the "board" is set in the Harz Mountains, with the players branching out from Leipzig through Central Germany.  On the other side, the players start in the Nuremburg/Fürth locale, and spread out through Bayern (Bavaria).

The first magazine (2/2011) included the start player marker and chits for the entire series.

The player must provide only the dice in order to play.

The object of the game is to gain wealth or influence by owning events and power points/chips which each represent 10,000 protestors at your event.  Wealth & Influence win the game.  Being driven to Poverty produces the loser.

A Monopoly-inspired game for 1-4 players inspired by Wisconsin politics in early 2011.

The game of MasterChef as the official game of the TV show!

To become MasterChef game, you have to win as many points as possible before the arrival square is reached by one player. Throughout the journey, players will participate in events related to the world of the kitchen, which will challenge their knowledge and culinary skills!

A Monopoly type game featuring different colored landing spots which requires answering trivia questions about the state of California. A roll of the dice determined which kind of question had to be answered in order to move on to another space.

Bagration 1944 is the codename of the Soviet offensive against the Heeresgruppe Mitte (Army Group Centre German). In two weeks, it will largely achieve its objectives, destroying a large number of divisions and opening a gap of 400 km wide in the German front. For the first time, the Wehrmacht can not stabilize the situation. The game covers the first two weeks of the battle. Another will focus on the following weeks. Both are played independently but may be related to a great campaign.

Bagration 1944 is a game for two players, one representing the commander of German forces and the other the commander of Soviet forces. It is played on a map covering the theater of the time. It is divided into hexagons representing 20 km from one side to another. It uses pre-cut cardboard pieces in representing military units that participated in this campaign: the German side of divisions and army corps on the Russian side for the most part. A full game lasts 10 turns, each representing two days of real time.

Ziplock game with:
- one A2-size map
- 192 counters
- 16 pages full color rules (both French and English)
- A quick reference aid
- A german display for operation points
- A soviet display for operation points
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
SURVIVE THE WINTER...
Hunt rabbits and squirrels. Fight wolves and boar. Forage for nuts and herbs. Hunt efficiently. Know when to hoard your bounty or race back to the village when the need is greatest. Survive the winter and live another summer.

HUNT OR GATHER...
Hunt or Gather is a fast paced and exciting blend of strategy, memory, and dice rolling fun. Each player gets to control two characters, a hunter and a gatherer. Those characters must pool their resources, because in the end only the weaker of the two counts.

Characters set out from the village, hoping to collect plants or hunt animals. With success, their territory grows making it easier to find the path home. If they fail, they may leave with their life if it's a harmless deer, bunny or squirrel, but death is instant if they roll poorly against a deadly bear, wolf, or boar. Good thing they have a lot of cousins to take their place, so you can set off again and keep playing.

From the publishers website:

For years on end something mysterious happens every year on a snowy winter night: snow is falling and the snowflakes keep falling thicker and faster and the pile of snow slowly turns into a wonderful castle – Castle Evermore.

The snow piles up higher and higher, gradually covering objects up. Can you recognize the different sizes of the growing castle?

Castle Evermore is a different size on each card. If the castle size on one of your own cards fits between two of your opponent’s cards, you are allowed to pinch his cards.

Whoever has the most cards in a row without any gaps lying in front of him at the end of the game is the winner.

Gods of Olympus is a Board Game for players aged 12 to adult. The game can be played in 2-6 individual players or teams and it includes a variety of challenges. There are 7 categories of challenges- 5 power challenges -Knowledge, Performance, Visual, Physical, Logical- plus General challenges and Battle challenges. Each category has at least 10 different types of tasks of different difficulty levels and styles.

The game is staged in Ancient Greece when Gods were ruling over the earth and influenced the lives of men.  The players enter the Labyrinth and go towards the center to beat the Minotaur and become Heroes.  On their way, players gather Powers, by completing challenges.  Once they are heroes, they need to go back and get out of the Labyrinth. Only a true God can escape the mythical Maze alive. On their way back they need stronger powers that they gather by completing Hero challenges. The first to escape the Labyrinth is the winner – a God of Olympus.

The story: The Gods of Olympus decided to welcome one human among them as one of their peers. But it cannot be any human. He/she has to be worthy of becoming a God, the best they can find, one who is a true hero and of special character. Each city of Ancient Greece selected their best representative and sent them to participate to the Gods of Olympus Challenge.  Each city selected the bravest, the strongest, the smartest and the most talented citizen to represent them in the Gods Challenge and gain protection of the Gods for their city and the people living in it.

You are the representative of one of the cities of Ancient Greece. As your city representative you will have to enter the Labyrinth, face multiple challenges that require skill, strength, knowledge, reach the Centre of the Labyrinth and face the Minotaur. To reach the centre of the labyrinth you need the 5 magic powers you gain on your way, but you also need luck and a good strategy.  You have to act smart, solve the challenges and move fast inside the labyrinth and out.  On your way, you'll have to avoid dangerous creatures like the Gorgons that can turn you into stone. You may also have to fight your opponents in fair battle, especially if they attack you. Only the first getting out of the labyrinth can become a God of Olympus and anyone can still win the game no matter how advanced the other players are, provided they play smart.

boardgame: All Is Lost to Me: Pavia 1525
description:
Hailed as the first modern battle, Pavia marked the rise of hand-held firearms as a tool of warfare. The arquebus became the bench-mark for an entire species of guns. It offered a wooden stock, shaped to nestle in the shoulder, which now meant a gun could be aimed. Politically, France’s defeat at Pavia changed the balance of power in Europe. As a prisoner, the French king Francis I wrote : “To inform you of how the rest of my ill-fortune is proceeding, all is lost to me save honor and life, which is safe …”
The game map represents the battlefield of Pavia. A hex grid has been superimposed on the map to regulate movement. Each hex represents about 200 yards. The game has 12 turns, each turn representing about 30 minutes. Each combat unit represents from 400 to 800 men or horses or guns.

The game starts with the Spanish army having arrived to relieve the siege of Pavia by the French army. Play is composed of 12 turns. Each turn comprises two identical sequences of phases, one for each player.
Sequence of play
The active player’s turn is organized into the following phases :
a) Out of command check
b) Rally
c) French Commander Initiative Check
d) Movement
e) Passive Player Reaction Movement
f) Defensive Fire
g) Offensive Fire
h) Melee

268 pawns and counters

Game design : Lionel LIRON
Original Counter Art : Lionel LIRON
Counter Design : Ton Cundiff
Box Art : Mike Mirfin
Game Board : Michael W. Kennedy
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
NOTE:  The Carnival: Wild! Die is an expansion for the base Carnival game.

In the regular (base) Carnival game, players roll three dice and choose two of them to assign to the tableau and take the assigned actions based on the the value rolled:

1 = Deck Draw
2 = Discard Draw
3 = Hand Draw
4 = Hand Trade
5 = Midway Trade
6 = Midway Steal

The Carnival: Wild! Die  a way to add even more re-playability to Carnival.  This die can REPLACE one of the three used in the base game.  It is rolled with the two other regular dice.  If Wild! Die lands on values 1-5, then the regular actions associated with the value can be performed.  However, if the "!" side (this is where the "6" normally is) is rolled, then any value can be assigned to it and that action taken!

Test Your Skill

The Lucky Dice game board is a single card that was included with the Carnival game.  However, the game card is not really necessary to play.

The following items are needed: 

     3 dice (d6)
     a piece of paper
     a pen


Draw a grid on the  paper that mimics the grid on the Lucky Dice game card.  This will be used to score the rows, columns, and 2 diagonals.  The scoring is as follows:

     Doubles = 5 points
     Triples = 10 points
     Straight = 15 points


To play, roll the 3 dice.  Assign the three values to any unused squares within the grid.  Repeat 2 more times until the grid is full.  Add up the scores for the rows, columns, and diagonals to get the total (final) score.

Alternatively, if you have 9 dice available, roll three at a time and just place the dice on the spots within the grid on the Lucky Dice game card.  After all dice have been placed, sum the scores for the rows, columns, and diagonals to get the total (final) score.

Issue game in Command Magazine Japan #102

Covering the planned landing in Kanto area, Japan (Operation Coronet) at the end of WW2.
Using the same system as Battle for Germany by SPI.

One player controls the U.S. 1st army landing along Kujuukuri-beach and the Japanese western force.
The other player leads the U.S. 8th army landing along Sagami-bay and the Japanese eastern force.
Each player gains victory points by conquering towns and cities.
Each hex represents 6km.
The game ends at the end of 8th turn.  And each turn is 1 week.

Danger Meteorites offers a thrilling adventure in our galaxy.
Each player has four rocket and space probes. The goal is to place these on each of four solar system planets to win .
But there is need to be careful and avoid the crossing meteorites.
Player rolls 1 or 2 dice in his turn. If the wrong face (one of six) appears on dice, he must start at the beginning with all the probes.

Distribute all the cards between the players. Everyone keeps his cards face down in front of him without looking. 
In his turn player turns a card face up in the middle of the table.

If it is a "Gauls", it nothing happens. 
If it is a "Roman", The first who shouts it wins all the cards from the table. He shuffles his cards and continues the play.
If it is "Boar" The first who shouts "Pig"! draws 2 cards from any  any players.

Player who wins all the cards is the winner.

The game board has to be assembled with 4 parts to create a big 3D arena. The race track has twenty-eight boxes of which sixteen are risky: firing a crossbow may shoot a pawn in it at any time.

The same bow determines, according to the box where the ball comes from, how many spaces you advance.

In turn, the player must skillfully position the crossbow to try to remove an opponent's pawns lying on its trajectory. Then he moves his pawn the number indicated by the ball.

A kind of Lotto for Christmas time. Instead of numbers, the game uses a traditional deck of Neapolitan cards, 40 in all in the traditional Italian-Spanish suits of Clubs, Coins, Cups and Swords. Each player has a mat showing 25 cards making several combinations of 5 cards each. The scores go from the single central card to 5 cards combinations, or Sinco.

The man on the box cover photo, is the designer himself, Emilio Salvatore. The other people are members of his family, daughters, wife and nephew in particular.

boardgame: Mit dem KDF Wagen durch Großdeutschland
description:
Objective: be the first reaching the target & back passage
 - moving by turn -
choose a route  e.g. North-South A: Flensburg-Hamburg-Hanover-Nuremberg-Munich-Vienna 
each player rolls the die - ending on a marked space follow instructions printed on the board (e.g. slow down, roll an odd number, move back...)
after target was reached/passed turn back to starting point
board shows alternative routes 
remarks:
game was published ~ 1940 - at least 2 different editions
inspired by a test drive 1938 done by engineers of VW, comparing the new developed VW with Opel, Ford cars...
meeples: the well known/wanted 'Fröha' cars 
rare collector's item

There are 28 cards in this game: 20 "slap" cards + 8 "special" cards.

The object is to provide a maximum of slaps to the Romans!

In his turn player draws a card and keep it face up in front of him.
"Baffes": slap cards - these are the points you earn in battle.
"Banquet" pass the next round, it's time for wild boar!
"Julius Caesar" give your best card "slap" to your neighbor on the right.
"Magic Potion" your neighbor left his cards mixed and makes you draw a card at random!

When all the cards were drawn, each one counts the number of data slaps to the Romans. Whoever has the most slaps won!

With a map of Switzerland as board, Cantuun is a call for travel along with four young and curious people, who began a challenge on Facebook: be the first to arrive in canton Uri after having visited each and every Swiss canton. The game pursues two clear  objectives: to add to one's knowledge on what one thinks to know on Switzerland, and to have a good time.
(translated from the french official description)

Cantuun is a trivia game about the cantons of Switzerland. Each player has a set of card representing each Swiss canton, and plays them hidden to answer the questions. Each correct canton selected brings points, and the first player to reach 26 points wins. There are three different types of questions (easy, hard and speed questions), which makes the game more dynamic than a regular trivia game.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Green Hell uses the popular Fire & Movement system (DG Folio Series) to examine two different situations in Burma during World War II: Operation Thursday and The Battle of the Admin Box. Contained in World At War Magazine #28.

Operation Thursday simulates the British gliderborne assault into Burma in March 1944, after Gen. Slim ordered Gen. Wingate to land behind the Japanese lines to support the Allied offensive in Burma, particularly Gen. Stilwell’s advance to the strategic town of Myitkyina.

The Battle of the Admin Box is a simulation of the Japanese infiltration that surrounded the Indian 7th Infantry Division, and was also moving to trap the Indian 5th Infantry Division in order to annihilate both before the arrival of the Indian 26th and British 36th Divisions from the north. Holding up the entire Japanese plan was the headquarters area of the Indian 7th Division, which formed a defensive perimeter known as the “Admin Box” in the village of Sinzweya.

Game Scale:

        Game Turn: ? days
        Hex: ? mile / ? km
        Units: Company to Regiment (mostly Battalion)


 
Game Inventory:

        One full color map
        One dual-side printed countersheet (160 1/2" counters)
        One rulebook (including Fire & Movement Standard & Burma exclusive rules)


(from the S&T press website)

description: In Safari Park, players try to become expert zoologists. To achieve this, they must travel through the various animal parks and answer the questions about animals and nature. They must choose wisely which parks to visit according to the card objectives that they receive at the beginning of the game.

boardgame: Super Dungeon Explore: Candy and Cola
description:
Candy and Cola is a single miniature expansion for Super Dungeon Explore. It was provided with some copies of the game as well as a separate purchase.

Candy and Cola has the following abilities:

- Luck: Can select between potions and hearts, as known from the Riftling Rogue.
 - Cola: Rolls a blue dice in each activation and gains potions according to the number of stars.

They also have the following special action:

- Vending Machine: For 2 actions they can remove a potion and place it on another hero (range 6).

Finally, they can spend potions:

- Soothing Soda (1 potion): Immune to status effects.
 - Sugar Rush (3 potions): +2 actions.

It is worth noting that Candy and Cola can carry 3 potions!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Wargames Rules for European Battles of the Seven Years War (1756-1763) by Barry Lee

Useable with 10-28mm figures. Covers the Seven Years War and includes 2 Scenarios (Minden and Zorndorf). Units of 3-6 bases/elements with 2-6 Units per Brigade. Includes Army Lists and notes for all protagonists. Playable by 2 or more players per side. Quick Reference Sheet included. Fastplay.


        Rules now available in Free PDF form at MindenRose Yahoo Group**

Remember when you were young and you would look out the window on road trips and name off the out-of-state license plates that passed by? What about the different road signs? Scavenger has turned this travel activity into a travel game, perfect for your next road trip.

Scavenger travel games are compact travel tins with magnetic game pieces. Each magnet represents the images (either license plates or road signs) you need to find.

How to play:
Each player receives a travel tin. While traveling look for the license plates or road signs that you still need. Mark off your finds by moving the matching magnets over to the other side. The person who finds the most is the winner.

The game was designed for multiple players but works equally well as a solo game.

This is a simple spin the spinner game. The spinner has a metal ball that is spun around and where it stops determines the result of the play. You are the quarterback and you can either chose to spin the Running Spinner or try to throw using the Passing Spinner in order to get results for the play. There is also a Extra Point/Kickoff/Field Goal Spinner and a Punt Spinner.

A simple betting game where a deck of cards is used to race 4 horses down the track. Method of movement is simply by turning over a card. The suit determines which horse moves. The action comes from betting on which hose will Win, Place and Show.

Heist is a solitaire card game in which the player uses his/her con artist to infiltrate companies, houses, banks, etc to complete the given missions.

Players must combine the skills of con men wisely and should be ready for the great heist (last mission of the game)

There are 4 types of cards in the game.

Event Cards: These cards have random events on them and they also have a map piece illustration, which represent the game board. Players draw event cards to create the rooms explained in the plan cards and they also try to resolve the events.

Plan Cards: These are cards with missions on them. Players need to complete at least 3 missions to run the great heist. Every mission needs some skills to be completed.

Con Cards: These are character cards with different skills. Players combine them to complete missions.

Item Cards: These are cards representing various items, helping players to complete missions.

In Match & Matrimony you are trying to marry your daughters off during the British Regency era.

The game is played over a number of rounds.  Each round two eligible suitors are available to be won.  Each player secretly chooses a daughter to send in an attempt win over one the suitors.  Each daughter has unique values in 4 attributes, and each suitor has a preference for one attribute.  Some suitors are worth more than others, and in addition players are attempting to collect sets of suitors to increase their worth.

All but one of your daughters will be married by the end of the game, so it is important to choose suitors wisely.  It's not enough to marry your daughters to just anyone.

Paper-and-pencil game where from 2 to 4 players must score the most possible points by filling a row or column of an 8x8 board with their own symbol over several rounds.

During a round players throw a dice in their turn and then tick a space on the board with their symbol (dot, circle, square or cross); the points in the dice mark the distance this space must have to either the player's starting row (at one of the sides of the board) or one of the spaces occupied by the player, and the way to that space must be free (if there are occupied spaces in between they must include the player's own symbol).

When a line is filled with one symbol each player gets a point per own symbol included in the line and the player to end the round gets one extra point. A game includes a number of rounds to be agreed.

Livid Visage is a combat based Card Game where you control a team of characters to defeat your opponents crew. Play events to manipulate the board state and get livid.

Each player constructs a 40 card deck from a set pack that contains all the cards of the game. The game starts with each player putting in to play a character with powers 3,2,1,0 from their deck and as the game progresses each player is allowed to play a new character in their character zones. Each player has 5 character zones in which they can play characters in. Additionally there are Event cards that help players manipulate the board state and acquire an edge over the opponent.

The goal of the game is to defeat all of the opposing characters and leave your opponent with no characters to defend with. When this happens that player loses the game.

Saaguan is an abstract strategy game for 2 to 6 players designed by Andrew Cooke, an Englishman living in Yunnan, South China.

The game can be played in an hexagonal board of size 6 or in an octaboard each side 5 squares long. In both boards the outermost ring of the board is called corridor and the inner part is the arena.

Board starts empty.

In each turn, players can:

     Add a triangular piece on the corridor, provided that it has less than 2 of his pieces already in there.
     Move his pieces up to 3 times, being rotating them right or left (45º in the octaboard, 60º in the hexaboard) or advancing one house in the beam direction (pointing vertix). 


A robot is eliminated when it has 3 robots pointing to it (imagine a beam coming out of its pointy vertix). Also, a robot is "locked" (hence can't be moved and its "beam" are disabled) when 2 robots are facing it.

The goal is to be the player who achieves the target score (2 - 6 players) or to eliminate all but 2 of the opponent's robots (2 players). Points are scored when eliminating enemy's robots.

Kingdom Builder: Nomads provides more options and more variety for Donald X. Vaccarino's Kingdom Builder, with four new game boards - and consequently four new powers associated with their respective game boards, such as the power to place one or two stones from a quarry on empty spaces to block them. Three additional Kingdom Builder scoring cards are included to provide new scoring opportunities during game play, and red houses are included to allow for up to five to play.

The new game boards replace the castle spaces from the base game (which score three points for anyone adjacent to them) with nomad spaces. During set-up, in which four game boards are chosen at random from all available boards, a randomly chosen nomad tile is placed on each nomad space. The first player to build a settlement adjacent to a nomad space takes this tile and must use it during her next turn; the tile is removed from the game after her next turn, whether she used it or not. Nomad tiles allow a player to build three settlements on the specified terrain, move settlements, build without following the adjacency rule, remove another player's settlement, or score points.

The new scoring cards give points as you place settlements during your turn, specifically for placing settlements in a straight line, for placing settlements next to those of other players, and for placing settlements on terrain that's not adjacent to empty terrain of the same type. (Replacement scoring cards for "Workers" and "Merchants" are included in Kingdom Builder: Nomads and should be used in place of those in the base game. These cards merely reflect the fact that the expansion boards have Nomad spaces which also count for scoring for these cards.)

In addition to the quarry power – which both helps block opponents and fill spaces in which you don't want to place settlements – the new location powers allow you to place a settlement in the flower fields, to place a settlement adjacent to at least three of your own settlements, and to move a settlement in a straight line on the board until it hits an obstacle.

Kingdom Builder: Nomads FAQ
