    Dinofarm Buildgame: Let's Build the Mezoic Creature Farm / Dinofarm Buildgame: Let's Build the Mezoic Creature Farm

boardgame: Le Grand Jeu du Peuple Migrateur
description:
"Winged Migration" in English
The game uses the principle of migration, you are a bird and you try to make your trip the best conditions, avoiding the pitfalls Racing game like Chutes and Ladders with cards events, issues, etc.

Each player is a migratory bird. The goal is to achieve complete its migration and reconstruct the puzzle bird as quickly as possible.
Once a player has met the six pieces of his puzzle, he must go to the finish to win the game having completed its "migration".

Player rolls the dice and move forward or backward

Each player has 40 cards that make up its game cards are either characters (heroes, village ...), magic, creatures, objects or places, all from the world of Lanfeust of Troy.
Each player represents a wise man who manages to start a group of villagers. Group that, throughout the game, grew in size and weapon, gets help from creatures, use of magic, visits to weaken the enemy wise.

The goal is to be the strongest or least injured at the end of the game.

In turn, each player performs in succession all the following phases:
1. managing the presence of heroes
2. care management of the characters and creatures
3. draws 2 cards
4. completion of 6 shares of its choice (ask a character, a creature, make use of magic, give an object to a character, use an action (the power) of a character placed in his camp ...
During these six stocks, the adversary can achieve in one and only.
5. launch an attack (either a general attack with all his forces, an attack with all characters flying and flying creatures, an attack against creature creature).
6. managing the presence of heroes
7. return of the hero in the neutral zone
8. discard any cards from his hand.

Once the player has completed all its phases, it is his opponent to do the same. And so on until the end of the game that occurs when a player received 10 wounds or one player has more cards in his deck.

Contents in the starter decks:

     2 sets of 40 cards (each set includes 28 common cards, 9 cards unusual and rare 3)
     40 chips (20 "P", 20 "Axes")
     A 6-sided dice
     1 Rulebook 23-page

Anzio: Operation Shingle covers the initial Allied landings on the 22nd January to the failure of the second German offensive, Operation Seitensprung, to eliminate the beachhead on the 1st March.  The whole campaign can be replayed as well as several scenarios covering key parts of the battle. The game also allows alternative plans to be explored such as the original plan for the US 504th parachute regiment to be airdropped just outside Carroceto on the Via Anziate.

Extract from draft design notes...

…In order to calculate the hex grid needed I looked into the area required to deploy regimental sized units. German, American and British regimental units varied in size and composition, and by 1944 most units were suffering some level of depletion of manpower. Couple this with documented doctrinal frontages rarely being achieved on the ground and the calculation of accurate frontages becomes very problematic. A German infantry division in 1944 had three infantry regiments as well as an assortment of artillery, support and HQ companies and battalions. Each German infantry regiment consisted of two battalions. Bull (2005) states a German battalion in an attack frontage would occupy 400-1000m, and Rottman (2004) has their defensive frontage as 800-2000m. Disregarding any particular formations if these two battalions were placed in line the regiment would need:

German Regimental Frontages 
Attack 800-2000m ½ mile to 1¼ miles 
Defense 1600-4000m 1 mile to 2½ miles

British brigades (equivalent to regiments) and US regiments were somewhat larger than their German counterparts, having an additional infantry battalion. Doctrine would seldom recommend placing all of a regiments units in line, with some elements held back in reserve, so using with the above German calculation offers a useful yardstick.

The next design problem then emerged, how to simulate the different frontages of units and the particular advantages and disadvantages of each? Units in a defensive frontage should be able to defend a longer front and have some defensive advantage. Units in an attack frontage should be able to concentrate their strength and have the advantage of easier movement. The game simulates different frontages by allowing units to be within a single hex (attack frontage) or two hexes (defensive frontage) - additional aspects of the differing frontages are discussed later.

So using the German regimental frontage above I calculated that a single hex needed to be able to contain a regiment in an attack frontage of ½ to 1¼ miles. Taking into account that the allied formations were a little larger a scale of 1½ miles across each hex seems suitable. A regiment in a defensive frontage would cover around 3 miles. Each hex contains around 3½ square miles.

This hex grid was then placed over a map of the campaign area and adjustments made in order for the grid to fit…

Shooting game featuring a highly collectible Golliwog as the target. The idea is to shoot off his buttons and his eyes with your Air Pop-Gun.

Each player takes equal turns and the one with the most successes, wins.

A dexterity game in the mould of later games like Jenga. In this game players must build sticks on to a structure. As the sticks get higher, it becomes increasingly more difficult. The sticks are rounded like a straw which increases the difficulty level even higher.

Three dice decide which colours you need to take. Red sticks are the longest, blue sticks are medium length and green are the smallest.

Your turn is against the clock so you need to be pretty fast. If you knock down the stick tower, you lose that game. Play as many games as you want and the winner is the one with fewest losses.

Straightforward target game but with some flair for relating to the title. In the 18th century, coach travellers were often accosted by highwaymen and the Dover Road was a popular haunt, lying as it does between London and the port of Dover.

The targets, in period dress, are the coach itself, one alarmed passenger, one passenger who has just been shot and the highwayman.

The gun is operated by a rubber band but just look at the appearance, as near as they could get to an 18th century pistol.

The winner is the one with the most hits after the game ends.

El Bazar junior version.

Simple game in which players are trying to make something that they have on drawn tile (an animal, an object, a thing) using wooden elements. When the opponent guesses what you are building you both score a point. If not, next players is trying to make others to guess.

Text on the backside of the box:
How can a plane, a watering can or even a doll be presented using wooden shapes? Guaranteed giggles for the whole family...

Contents:

     120 tiles (toys, animals and objects)
     17 wooden shapes
     sand timer

boardgame: Squad Seven II: Le spectre d'Imhotek
description:
Second game in the Squad Seven saga.

This time the adventure takes place in the great desert of northern Sudan. The Squads explore the mysterious ruins of a temple buried under the sands in order to find the fabulous treasures, but also something else ... may be evidence that we are not alone in the universe and it may already be among us. Clues are hidden on the game cards.

Players choose their squad and enter the desert and take the turns to reveal cards, which may show treasure or various dangers. Many cards require the player to shoot a target or run round the table before a scream is heard on the soundtrack CD. If they fail, they lose a member of their squad.

The player who recovers the most treasure wins the game.

One of the type of game that the British, in particular, liked to play.

Each horse is attached to a length of string. By 'jiggling' the string, your horse can be made to move forward and, when a jump is reached, a different use of the string will allow your horse to 'jump' the fence. A fair amount of dexterity and finesse is needed with the aforementioned actions. The horses have two bases you might say, one flat and one rounded (it works exactly the same as a wheel would).

The first to finish the course, wins.

Speedway was so popular in Britain in the 1930's, many games reflected this.

This is a simple roll and move game. If your bike lands on one of many illustrated spaces, you must follow the instructions.

First to reach the finish-line, wins.

The game consists of a board with 6 holes which stands on two wooden blocks and in each of the holes hangs a bell. There are 2 large and 6 small tiddleywinks. The idea of the game is to propel your counters, Tiddleywinks fashion, and you score points for each tiddleywink that you fire through the holes with bonus scores for ringing the bells on the way through.

The rules are on a sheet attached to the inside of the box.

Highest score wins.

An action game for two football fans.

The board features several indentations which are long and thin. Each indentation has a player attached, and by moving levers at your end of the table, you can slide your footballer forward or back in order to 'kick' the ball. The ball is magnetic so that it is attracted to the nearest indentation once it has been kicked.

The winner is the one with the most goals after a pre-agreed time limit has expired.
Monopoly set in Buxtehude, Germany. All the traditional elements of rolling, moving, purchasing, earning, and spending.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements of Erlangen, in Germany.

A spin and move game. The object of the game is to be the first to reach the jump space by exact spin and then descend by parachute to the landing finish area. Players spin the indicator in turn and then move along the "Take off Run" the number of spaces indicated. Any number of pieces can be on the same space. You must follow the instructions on any space that you land on. You must reach the space marked JUMP by exact spin, and when you do, you must remain there until you spin a 6 on a following turn to descend by parachute to the landing field and win the game

A simple trivia game where players need to know the countries to flags they see and be able to locate the country on the world map.

Players take turns naming a country of which they are shown a flag on a card. If they name the country correctly and can point to it on the world map, they can choose to claim the card as a point and pass the turn to the next player. Alternatively, they can choose to play for a second point by answering 3 of 5 multiple-choice questions concerning this country correctly. If they answer fewer than 3 questions correctly, they get 0 points this round.

Publisher's description:

Earth is being attacked by alien invaders.  Can you defend it or will your Battle Cruisers be pulverised by the Invaders' Star Ships?  Try this exciting 2000AD game and see just how ready you are for combat in the vast depths of outer space.

Abstract Strategy game given away in prog 233 of 2000AD.  Players move their pieces around the triangular board in an effort to surround an opponents piece on all three sides, thereby eliminating that piece.  The game is won when an opponent is reduced to two pieces.

User Summary

Each turn, a player can combine a tile in his hand with some more in the table, being the sum 15. If he can do that, he take all the tiles for himself. If he can't make 15, he discards one tile.
After all the players have used his tiles, the scoring phase begins, adding point for haver certain tiles (yellow 7 by example), having three cards of the same color, most red tiles, or other combinations. The first player that reaches 60 points, wins.

The game consists of a board with holes in. Each hole scores a certain number of points. At the back of the board, a three-dimensional 'slide' is erected. Into the top of this slide, a player in his turn, drops a ball. The ball will bounce around the circus clowns at either side of the slide and, eventually, come out at the bottom and score points when it settles into one of the holes.

The points available range from 5 to 100, the latter hole being right in the middle of the board.

The one who earns the most points, wins.

BATTLEMAGE is a fantasy wargame for two or more players. Each person controls a small warband of warriors, each led by a powerful Battlemage. You are fighting for power and glory, but more than that you are fighting for control of the world of Ra'al.

A game of BATTLEMAGE is fought between two warbands, each led by a powerful spellcaster called a Battlemage. The game is played on a tabletop with miniature figurines.

The game uses six-sided dice to resolve the conflict. Each figure (or 'warrior' as the game calls them) has it's own set of statistics that define how good it is at fighting: MV (move), ATT (attack), DEF (defence) and W (wounds). Additionaly, they may have special rules that let them do unusual things, such as make ranged attacks, use powers, or fly through the air, to name but a few.

The book contains five different warbands, each led by a different Battlemage. They include the Whiterock Loyalists, the Shadow Academy, the Cult of the Black Wind, The Horde of Seven Peaks and the Ghostwood Raiders.

Campaign rules are also include with the game, so that your warriors can improve with each battle.

Objective: collect as many points (stars) as possible; alternative: be the first reaching target space with a matching number
 - moving by turn; similar to 'Snakes and Ladders' -
• move according die roll - no kick out, meeples can be put on same space
• if your move ends on a rocket space move meeple to the 'top of the rocket' space
• if your move ends on a 'Robocat space' ==> move down & get a 'star' slide (as long as available)
• first one reaching target space (96) gets the big star slide

A very old-fashioned game even in 1936. I mean the rules, not the sparkling illustrations by V.T.Hamlin.

You have to get to the end of the trail. A spinner is used rather than a die. You move as directed by the spinner. If you land on a special space, you must follow the instructions. If you land on the same space as another player, that player must go back 5 spaces.

First to the end of the track (by exact spin, of course), wins.

The object of the game is to control the most number of Space Craft at the end of the game.

On your go you roll 2 dice, add them together, and place your Space Craft on the corresponding space on the board, if it is not already occupied. If you roll a 7 you occupy the Moon with your large Moon Base token, this allows you to control the other Space Craft. While occupying the moon you may replace any of your opponent's pieces with a piece of your own colour, provided that you roll the correct number.

When all the areas numbered 2 to 12 are occupied the game ends and the player with the greater number of his colour markers is the winner.

Follow the road of knowledge with Fred and Jamy and let yourself be guided by the DVD to arrive first at the end of the road! 850 questions on five topics around the Earth and the Universe. Questions and explanations images with almost 350 experts from the program.

The objective of this quiz game is to return to the Studio first while absorbing a load of knowledge! Along the way, the players must answer questions. Each card includes a question, three wrong answers, the right answer and most importantly its explanation!

Board game for teenagers with CD included.

2-5 called fans compete with their knowledge of the superstars.
They are singer, stylist, manager, journalist and actress. Each turns must to collect stars in a bending test players answer questions from the four themes:

In addition to having a perfect knowledge about the lives of celebrities, players demonstrate their artistic talents: one that marks the end of the box titled Graffiti player draws a movie title or song in order to make opponent guessing. That evil spirits do not stir, each wins: if the rival is fulfilling its mission, it will be rewarded with a Fate card.

Play as a pirate, Captain of your own ship! Your goal is to obtain the most gold: the pirating way! In game you will: obtain precious treasures, hold prisoners captive, blast your opponents with cannons, and board enemy ships to steal their booty!

C&C uses a unique cannon combat mechanic where players must find or trade for gunpowder and ammunition in order to fire their cannons.  Players can use cannonballs to sink enemy ships, chain-shot to make players lose turns (to repair their masts!), or grapeshot to clear the opponent's deck of crew.

Crew are used for boarding enemy ships to steal their cargo.  Crew can be equipped with pistols, muskets, rapiers, or cutlasses in order to increase their chance of success.  If victorious, crew are able to steal cards from the opposing player, whether that be supplies or gold.   Treasure cards will trade hands multiple times before the next Port! card is found where the players can take a breather before the next round of raids and cannon bombardments begin.

This is a dice and card game that relies heavily on luck.  In this sense, its best suited for casual gamers who are looking for a medium length game (30-60 minutes) that has excitement in every turn.

The game includes rules, two D6 dice, and 136 poker-size cards.

What Has Come To Pass
It wasn’t long ago that Dr. Zycheck’s experiments for immortality went awry and he was transformed into the undead thing known as The Creator. Further experimentation with creatures both living and dead quickly caused the Isle to become overrun with his Zombi monstrosities. Many tried to escape the Isle and others barricaded themselves in their cellars hoping to ride out the storm, while a few brave heroes did what they could to end the madness. Most didn’t make it. Professor Mortimus Philbin, Dr. Zycheck’s former assistant, rose to challenge The Creator with his self-made army of Exterminators, designed to do battle with Dr. Z’s minions. Unfortunately, the Exterminators were nothing more than mindless Cyber Zombis who slaughtered the living when the walking dead weren’t available as targets.

Just when it seemed things couldn’t get any worse, the Virus began to transform and spread like a plague, holding the Isle hostage within its deathly grip. Zombis began to mutate and hordes of the undead were being driven by powerful new masters. The heroes fought valiantly to save the Isle, but the virus-fueled undead seemed impossible to overcome. The Virus had infected the Heroes as well, however, empowering them to perform amazing feats that could be used to stop the onslaught. But rather than using their new found abilities to save the Isle, they began to work against each other in a desperate struggle for self-preservation.

The Creator stood to reign triumphant as the Isle swiftly fell into the hands of the dead, but the Brood Mother emerged from a vile cesspool of the darkest Virus. A creature with an insatiable appetite for both the living and the dead, she began to even the odds. Now, in the Isle’s darkest hour, The Creator, the Brood Mother, and the survivors of Chaos Isle face off in a final battle to either save the Isle, or doom it forever.

Game Overview
Chaos Isle is a zombie-themed card game that can be played solo or by up to 6 players using just a single deck. The game is played using cards separated into 6 different decks, including Mission, Character, Equipment, Feat, Virus and Enemy cards consisting of Tasks, Events, Mutations and Zombis. Chaos Isle: Zombi Deck places each player in the role of one of the Characters, with each attempting to be the first to complete their mission and all the while fending off 14 different Zombi enemies.

What's Included:
Includes the Base Game and the First 4 Expansions!
8 Zombie-themed Dice and pouch in plastic case!
50 colored Glass Hit Counters and pouch in plastic case!

description:
It is played on a 8x8 checkerboard. Every rank has 24 men, 20 ordinary and 4 with determined characteristics. The board is numbered from a1 to h8.

The pieces are collocated on squares dark and clear.  Pieces on a1 and h1 (a8 and h8 for Black) are called asterisk, because are marked with a green asterisk. They move and capture diagonally and vertically (forwards and backwards) but capture diagonally forwards with a long jump like flying king.  When it reaches the last row and become king, this one moves diagonally, vertically and horizontally with a long jump, but captures diagonally and vertically.

The pieces on d1 (e8 for Black) are marked with a blue exclamation point and are called expoint.  They move and capture diagonally and vertically (forward and backwards), but capture diagonally backwards with a long jump like flying king.  When it reached the last row and become king, it moves and captures diagonally vertically and horizontally.

The pieces collocated on e1 (d8 for Black) are marked with a red across, and are called across. It move and capture diagonally and vertically (backwards and forwards), but capture horizontally with a long jump.  When they reach the last row and become king, this one moves and captures (diagonally, vertically and horizontally),and it can not captured by men.

Simple men when it reach the last row after a capture and have still possibilities to catch, it go on to capture and not become King at the moment.Wins who blocks or captures all enemy pieces.

GAME SYSTEM

    
    
         This entry is to allow for discussion/rating of the game system as a whole. It is not for a specific product or release. Versions will appear on the individual item pages.

    


Japanese Collectible card game.

The game objective is about a player who order the character card to try to knock down the opponent's character and much as possible.

Each side of battlefield in the game have a Character in the front. That zone called as "Ring". And up to 3 supporting character behind. That zone called as "Bench". Only a main Character in the "Ring" can attack the opposing character that also in the "Ring". Each attacking requires to rest a supporting character in the "Bench" (like Magic the Gathering's Tapping). The damage is commutative until the attacked character are knocked down. Knocked-down character is remove to the zone called "Retired zone" and then immediately put the card from the top of the deck to the "Ring" face up, solve its effect before next attacking. The winner is a player who can make the opponent reaches 10 character in the "Retired zone".

Card type in this have only one type, the character card.

Constructed deck consists exactly 50 cards.

Save the Everglades Game is a 2-4 player card game that is not only fun to play, but is educational as well!

Players take turns drawing and playing cards while collecting 15 of some of the Everglades' most interesting animals.

Players can block collection of animals by playing action cards that can pollute another Player's Environments or take away their Animal Cards.

Each animal card reveals a beautifully illustrated and detailed representation of a bird, fish, reptile or mammal.

Rounded doninoes what can place on top of each other forming a domino stack if half of the domino is same. If player can't play he must draw many dominoes he can play.

Player who drops the stack is eliminated from the game and puts his dominoes back to the supply.

The game ends when one player gets rid of all your dominoes. He is the winner.

Characters from the comic strip ask questions about love and sex and make various funny tests for younger audiance 8+.

On certain squares players have a different tasks: ask questions, making mimes, making objects with clay, drawing, truth test, challenges etc.

The object of the game is to be the first player to reach the square "arrived". For this, players move from square to square and make succesfully different tests and answer different questions.

Wouégo is a music quiz, which means that players must recognize musical excerpts.

The game features three CDs containing 99 tracks of original music clips and 600 questions on the cards.
The goal of the game is to find who sings and answer questions.

The game can be played as a team.

Spider-Man clings to the top of a towering brick wall—but the Venom hands are coming! In this nimble-fingered challenge, players take turns using plastic Venom claws to push bricks from the wall without making Spidey fall. The wall teeters, the tension rises, and the first one to send the web-slinger tumbling loses. Simple to set up and fun for all ages, Tiens Bon Spider-Man turns steady hands and superhero reflexes into thrilling gameplay.

Knockout, Hold On Scooby-Doo!, Happy Feet Two: Hold on Erik, and Spider-Man: Spider Sense are all about removing bricks without toppling the structure—but each adds its own flavor. While the “Hold On” trio features perched characters and plastic frameworks, Knockout trades in cartoon mascots for pure demolition thrills with its unique battery-powered jackhammer and iconic “Knockout” brick that could spell your doom. Same tension. More vibration.

boardgame: Cadwallon: City of Thieves – Run for Your Life!
description:
Run for Your Life! is free downloadable content for Cadwallon: City of Thieves that adds a new high-stakes timer to the game. Featuring new map tiles and locations along with the variant rules to use them, Run for Your Life! brings even more variety to nightly illicit activities. This variant requires The Inn, Cry Havoc!, and A Gamble in the Dark, which are each available via the links on each of the respective BGG pages, or on Fantasy Flight's support page.

Run for Your Life! introduces the amazing Seismic Scale of Mage-Geologist Von Riechter, which can accurately predict the total destruction of Cadwallon based on the increasing strength of tremors and aftershocks. Watch the scale closely; the thief brave enough to endure fire and falling rubble might just claim the most wealth, if he isn’t killed first.

An action football (soccer) game.

You must assemble the game before you can start play. The board has four legs and is, in effect, a table. Insert a number of rods on each side. When assembled properly, the rod handles will freely rotate when you twist them. Along any rod there will be a number of football players.

When the ball is in play you can twist a player so that he 'kicks' the ball. Of course, your opponent is trying to do the same thing at the same time so play becomes frenetic from the start. The onject is to kick the ball into your opponent's goal. The player who scores the most goals, wins.

These type of games are most often seen at gaming arcades in Britain and I guess the same applies to the States.

Components:
One pre-painted Gargantuan black dragon (4”x4” base)

A simple track game designed and illustrated by Jessie MacQueen Mackintosh (1892–1958). In the game, Mackintosh used not only Aboriginal motifs, but also illustrations of traditional Aboriginal life and cultural events.

Being a roll and move game, there are plenty of special spaces and you must obey them if you end your move on one. Special spaces include No. 5 Dig for honey ants — miss a turn? No. 13 Throw boomerang — run to the emu space; No. 45 Pointing bone — you are knocked out of the game; No. 87 Lost churinga (shield), go back to No. 71’. And so on.

The explanatory notes that accompany the game are an attempt to broaden the knowledge of the players about indigenous culture.

I cannot be absolutely certain but it would appear the game was never boxed (highly plausible in the 40's after the war).

Siege Master is a two-player abstract strategy game of continental conquest. To set up, players create a map-like shape on a hexagonal grid, then they alternate placing an odd number of neutral fortresses on hexes within this map such that most fortresses are at least three spaces from all other fortresses. The variability of map shapes makes every game a new challenge.

Each player takes a matching set of army tokens and fortress tokens; an army token covers four empty hexes in a row (1x4). Players are trying to capture neutral fortresses, and they do so if the fortress is in an area of zero or more empty hexes that is surrounded by army tokens, the edge of the grid, or both and if no armies can fit into this area. When this happens, the capturing player replaces the neutral fortress with one of his own color.

On a player's turn, if the player's opponent did not capture a fortress on his previous turn and it is possible to play an army adjacent to the opponent's previously placed army, then the moving player must do so. Otherwise, the active player can play an army token anywhere on the map. (A swap rule applies after the first turn; when swapping, all tokens played by the first player on his first turn are replaced by the second player's tokens.)

The player who captures more than half of the fortresses on the map wins.

A game that simulates the sport of curling.  Players are rated for their shooting percentages and then those ratings are used, along with dice and charts, to determine the outcome of each "end" of a match.  Game comes with the rating formula so that owners with access to the right stats can rate any competition for which they have stats.

This is a game of women's collegiate volleyball (Division I).  Players are rated for their ability to kill, set, block, serve, and dig.  Three, ten-sided dice are used to resolve Actions from the Action Chart.  All Actions require the comparison of two ratings. All 329 Division I teams are rated in 32 separate conferences.  The game includes all charts and tables, a rule book, and the Final 32 from the 2011 NCAA Division I tournament.

description:
Cosmic ConneXion is an entertaining and educational word search card game created to familiarize players with the Greek alphabet and objects of the cosmos. The deck consists of 2 sets of cards, letter cards and celestial point value cards. The letter cards consist of all 24 letters of the Greek alphabet from Alpha to Omega. Each letter card displays the name, uppercase & lowercase Greek letter, along with English equivalents and variants. The letter cards are played in a diamond arrangement to create words. The celestial point value cards consist of 28 objects of the cosmos from our local solar system to the constellations of the zodiac along with wild cards of the cosmos. For each word formed, a player acquires a celestial point value card from the draw pile. The object of the game is to aquire as many celestial point value cards as possible. However, after all the letter cards have been played, the player who has acquired the sun card during regular play has a final opportunity to score additional points during the bonus round by forming a grammatically correct sentence from the words formed during regular play. The player with the highest point value wins. 
Age Range: 8 and up

Develop, upgrade, and recycle electronic gadgets by laying tiles representing components on a circuit board.

Designed during 48-hour 2012 Global Game Jam at NYU Game Center.  (Minimal playtesting, rules and tile mix require further fine tuning and balancing.)

Tile Placement game in which players develop, upgrade, and recycle successive generations of Apple gadgets by laying tiles representing components on a circuit board, connector edge to connector edge in order to form closed loops consisting of newly laid and previously laid components.

Component Tiles can only be placed adjacent to a tile with a shared connector edge (border) OR on top of one another to create Gadgets. Each successive generation of gadget is built on top of the prior generation's components. Components on the stack level corresponding to the current device generation are added to the builder's score, components hidden by others on higher stack levels are subtracted from the score. A completed gadget-- a closed loop of component tiles also referred to as a device-- is defined by it's most numerous component type, with each component corresponding to a different device, and scores a bonus upon completion. Gadget Cards depict their majority component requirement and keep track of descending bonus values for each device generation of that type built by all players (the first one built is worth more).

boardgame: Order of the Stick Adventure Game: Sticky Shticks
description:
Designer Description:

Sticky Shticks is a mini-expansion for the Order of the Stick Adventure Game that centers around a single mechanic: Stuff sticking to you. The set features two shticks for each of the six characters that can be "stuck" to another character under the right circumstances, either giving them a penalty of some sort or allowing you to flip it later to just mess with them. It also includes seven new Monster cards that can get stuck to you if they win, forcing you to drag their lazy asses around the dungeon until you (or one of your allies) can defeat it. A Screw This! card that interacts with any sticky card rounds out the 20-card set.

description:
Spanic (SP+Panic) is a Series: Shibumi game for 3 to 6 players. It uses 10 balls on each colour (white, black, red), a 4x4 Shibumi board and one bowl for each player.

RULES

Setup

Empty board. In 4 player games, 2 balls are picked up randomly from the supply and placed randomly on the board.

Each player then picks random balls from the supply (bag):

2 players: 15 balls each
3 players: 10 balls each
4 players: 7 balls each
5 players: 6 balls each
6 players: 5 balls each

... and places them in his bowl so the other players cannot see them during the game (variant: balls are visible).

Aim

To get rid of your balls before your opponents.

Rules

Players in turn either:

- place one of their balls on any empty hole or on top of a platform that has at least 2 balls of the same colour as the ball being placed or...
- pass.

End

The game ends when one of the players places his last ball, thus reaching the victory condition. If all players pass in a single round (rare), the player with less balls in his bowl wins. In case of a tie, play again.

Included in the Shibumi Rule Book

Soft cover:

http://www.lulu.com/shop/cameron-browne-and-nestor-romeral-andres/shibumi-rule-book/paperback/product-18940123.html

Downloadable PDF:

http://www.nestorgames.com/rulebooks/SHIBUMI_EN.pdf

IntroductionLoose is a finite territory game for two players: Black and White. It's played on the intersections (points) of an odd-sized, initially empty square board. Both players must have access to a sufficient number of stones colored black on one side and white on the other.

The goal of the game is having the most stones on the board when both players have passed. Loose features surround capture, like Go, but surrounded stones are flipped instead of removed, and a natural, game-defining mechanism is used to favour (rather than ensure) eye creation upon captures. Since no stones are ever removed from the board, cycles are not possible.

				
				
					DefinitionsIn this rules, adjacent always means orthogonally adjacent.

A group is a set of like-colored, adjacent stones.

A liberty is an empty point adjacent to a group. A false liberty is an empty point which is adjacent to at least one stone of each color and no empty points. Liberties which are not false are true.

				
				
					PlayStarting with Black, the players take turns placing stones with their color showing on empty points of the board, one stone per turn.

After a placement, all enemy groups with no true liberties are flipped, i.e. all the stones in them are turned round to show the player's color, after which the turn ends.

At the end of a turn, every group on the board must have at least one true liberty. Otherwise, the move is illegal and the player must choose another one.

Players can pass their turn at any moment. When both players pass in succession, the game ends, and the one with the highest score wins.

A player's score is the number of stones with his color showing that there are on the board, plus a komi (see below) in the case of White. In the event of a tie, whoever made the last move loses.

				
				
					KomiThe komi is the even number of points which are added to White's score at the end of the game as a compensation for playing second. Before the game starts, the first player tells his opponent the even number which will be used as komi, and then the second player chooses which color to play.

				
				
					Author's notesA very interesting, non-obvious feature of Loose is that, assuming that players will only pass their turn when there are no more useful moves available, the game will never end in a tie. In Go, ties can occur if there is an odd number of empty points in seki, but seki patterns in Loose always involve an even number of them. The tie-break rule is just included to make the game theoretically drawless, rather than just in practice.

Empire 4 Viktory II is an adaptation of the classic video game Empire, created in 1971 by Walter Bright.

Empire 4 Viktory II, an unofficial expansion for Viktory II, can be played by 2 to 6 persons. In his turn, each player might generate new resources and units, and give an order to each one of them. Units can move, watch, besiege and build new bases. Bases generate resources capable of creating new units. In Empire 4 Viktory II, the player has 6 kind of units available: Infantry, Tanks, Cargo Ships, Warships, Aircraft Carriers and Bombers. Empire 4 Viktory II uses the fog effect to create an unknown surrounding and hide enemy units for the players.

description:
Spalone is a solitaire puzzle game played with a Series: Shibumi set.

The player puts 10 balls of each colour (red, white, black) in a bag, then randomly draws a ball each turn and places it either:

1. on an empty board hole, or
2. on a 2x2 platform that contains at least two balls of the same colour.

The puzzle ends when the player cannot place the ball just drawn. It is solved if the player can place the last ball on the apex of the pyramid.

The random draw means that not all games will be solvable - some patience may be required! We do not yet know exactly how much patience.

Spalone is the solitaire version of Spanic.

Similar to Crazy Crab, where a mechanical device is attempting to thwart your mission to grab all of your food pieces.  Players try to pick up with the forks seahorses, shells or starfishes. But watch out! The terrible Crab terrible watching You and jump on You if you touch the plate.
Be the first to pick up all of Your food.

In Woe to the Living, you are the sinister spectral force that haunts the night and feeds off the terror you create!  You lurk in the darkest places, making night-time strollers walk just a little faster and double-bolt their doors.  You must carefully cultivate terror and watch those impacted; too much terror and they die of fright becoming useless to you.  Too little terror and you starve.

Sweet delicious terror!

Carefully select your prey across four rounds of game-play.  The goal is to have the second highest total terror at the end of the game.  This is made more difficult by the other players, all vying for that coveted high, but not highest, terror total.  Use a sneaky card swapping mechanic to exchange face down cards throughout each round.  Correctly gauge your opponents’ motives in order to feed them the cards they don’t want, or take from them the cards you need.

boardgame: Original Edition Delta: Book of War
description: A miniatures game that, on a statistical basis, faithfully replicates the results of combat in the Original RPG on a mass scale (with each figure representing 10 troops or 1 hero). To resolve attacks, each figure in a unit that is eligible to attack rolls a D6 against a target number to determine if it inflicts casualties on the opponent. A unit receiving casualties must then succeed in a morale check or rout. Rules are provided to convert troops and heroes from the Original RPG's statistics to Book of War. Magic rules derived from that game are also included.

Educational game where players move around the board drawing cards with the associate questions and their answers or to make small tests.

Junior version of Wetten Dass ...?

The game offers many simplifications, so just come the youngest in the family and for the course when it comes to the most successful European television show on ZDF.

In five different categories have challenging tasks to be solved to the end to win the game as a competition king.

Players must answer the questions, explain something in 60 seconds with a camera, doing pantomine etc.

The Vampire Diaries is a board game based on the Warner Brothers television show of the same name. Players take on the role of one of four characters from the show, Elena, Stefan, Damon or Bonnie, and begin the game in possession of a photo-frame portraying their character as a human. A switch in the back of the photo-frame can be used to transform the photo into that of a vampire, and the object of the game is to avoid receiving three bite marks, thereby being the last remaining human.

Game play occurs in turns with players moving a dry erase vampire marker/mover around a simple game board. In turn, each player rolls three dice marked with crows and moves the marker/mover zero to three spaces clockwise, depending on the number of crows rolled. The space the marker lands on allows the player to use the top portion of the vampire mover to apply a dry erase vampire bite stamp to another player's photo-frame, erase one of his or her own vampire bites with the supplied dry erase pad, swap photos with another player, or gain control of the Family Crest Ring which protects that player from vampire bites. A roll of zero crows does not move the marker, instead automatically gaining control of the Family Crest Ring for the player who rolled the zero.

When a player receives the third vampire bite on his photo-frame, he can no longer win the game, erase bites, swap photos or possess the Family Crest Ring. However, he can remain in the game, taking turns as normal, but only dealing out vampire bites as allowed. The last player without three vampire bites wins the game.

Game description from the publisher:

The Sino-Soviet Union enters Dust Warfare in Campaign Book "Zverograd". This supplement includes a Platoon List with stats and costs for all of the SSU units, background information regarding this faction, and rules for the first Dust Warfare campaign. Moreover, Campaign Book "Zverograd" contains new unit entries for both the Axis and Allies to combat the SSU threat. Field an overwhelming SSU army in Dust Warfare with this tantalizing supplement.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: MCW M3  Walker – "Rattler / Rattler-Amp / Cobra / Cobra-Amp"
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The Cobra and Rattler Medium Walkers – otherwise known as the MCW M3 Expansion – offer the Allies more options for armored support. With its anti-aircraft gun, the Rattler is capable of grounding airborne units. When it is not punishing aircraft, the Rattler unloads a wall of machine gun fire that shreds infantry to pieces. Meanwhile, the Cobra uses its Phaser gun to electrify vehicles and troops alike, causing massive amounts of damage to both. Additionally, this expansion includes parts to give your walker the Amphibious Unit skill.

Game description from the publisher:

Many of the soldiers of the SSU are experts with rifles and specialize in harassing enemies from concealed locations. Unlike the Axis and Allies, the SSU prefers to field these snipers in pairs rather than in tandem with a spotter. The SSU Observer Team allows SSU commanders to use the Artillery Strike ability to activate indirect fire weapons. Containing both the SSU Sniper Team and the SSU Observer Team, the SSU Specialists expansion makes a potent addition to your army.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: SSU Hero Pack – "Nikolaï / Yakov / Red Yana"
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The SSU, like the other factions, goes to battle under the leadership of inspiring and skilled commanders. Heroes like Nikolaï, Yakov, and Red Yana give the SSU forces an edge in combat. Red Yana has an extraordinary Fighting Spirit, and Nikolaï is a superb Pilot. Meanwhile, Yakov is a Legendary Tactician who can adeptly command any strike force. With the SSU Hero Pack, your army has access to new skills and strategies to win any battle.

Game description from the publisher:

The SSU Rifle Squad is unique in the fact that its members make use of a mix of Sniper Rifles and machine guns to support their Comrades. The soldiers of the SSU are renowned for their skills with rifles, and they prefer to wield these weapons instead of more traditional assault weapons like flamethrowers or heavy machine guns. In fact, Rifle Squads are so skilled with their weapons that it is not uncommon for them to take down enemy walkers!

Players can win a trip with a private jet to South America, ride a bike, train, taxi or stay for a visit at the North Pole. Everybody is active the whole time and anything can happen.

The player who is the first one to get six continent cards (one for each continent) wins the game. Players roll two dice and move accordingly on the map. War of Ashes: Shieldwall is the latest offering from the designers of This Quar’s War and Songs of our Ancestors. Concentrating on command and position instead of unit statistics and abilities, WoA: Shieldwall provides simple rules that bring with them difficult decisions.

Taking the gritty tactics and concepts of the Dark Ages and setting them in the whimsical world of Agaptus, WoA: Shieldwall begins with the Roar Phase in which both sides rally their line and taunt their opponent. The goal of which is generating Froth, a resource used throughout the game to bend the rules and adjust position in the line.

To represent the flowing nature and chaos of the battlefield, WoA: Shieldwall utilizes a blind random activation system in which the commander never quite knows how far he can push his fighters before the momentum of the battle passes to his opponent.

Once two lines have met, the fighting begins. Unlike most ranked unit games, casualties are removed from the point of contact and the lines begin to merge. Each slain opponent brings with him a decision – advance and break their line, or hold and preserve your Shieldwall!

'Veils of Verdandi' is a fantasy board game in which players work together to complete a heroic quest.The game has been created with the aim of translating the excitement of interactive storytelling of role-playing games to board games, while simplifying existing game concepts as well as adding new ones.

In the game, players must rescue the nephew of the king, prince Ture, from the dangers and mysteries of the Dark Forest. To do this, the players move across the game board as an adventuring party and visit strange and magical places while battling vicious enemies. But the players must move quickly, for when day changes to night, the Dark Forest becomes much more deadly indeed.

Used in this game are 4, 6 and 8 sided dice for combat and movement across the board, as well as for specific interactive story challenges the game has to offer. Players may buy new equipment and magical items during the game, and train their special abilities to a higher level.

The game is the first in a saga, the next installment of the game, Veils of Verdandi Book II, will carry the story beyond the Dark Forest and into a vast and deadly mountain range, and Slavic mythology. Players will be able to carry over any equipment and skills acquired in the first installment, and use them to defeat such dire foes as the Vampire, the Werewolf, and other dark and dreadful creatures born of mist and moonlight, that will challenge the might and magic of even the hardiest of adventurers.

In each round, two teams compete and the third is the referee! Then the roles change. Mime, design, action, speed, memory, address: 150 simple and fun challenges in every genre: Find 10 objects of specific color in the room as soon as possible! Make more sports guessing mimicking! and many more.

Quickest team scores. Team who has most points and the end of the game is the winner.

A simple roll and move game. Try to land on the green squares and avoid the red squares. If either colour is landed on, you must follow the instructions, good or bad.

First to get the hero from his home to being rescued, wins.

K2: Lawina (K2: The Avalanche), a small expansion for K2, contains four new weather tiles describing bad weather conditions and there is an additional icon showing when the avalanche is coming down. All climbers who are not hidden in their tents that day have to move one space further from the mountain peak.

The game is played in 5 rounds for which the number of cards per player changes each time (7,8,9,8,7).

Each player bets on the number of tricks that he hoped to get.
There are 5 colors with the numbers 1 to 9 and knight, king,  executioner (0 or 10 depending on the player's choice), dragon and the witch who are are stronger (except in the last 3 turn or they are worthless).

The first player commits a color, the others should play the same color and bigger card to take the trick.
At the end of the game everyone counts the number of tricks performed. if the bet is won, the player multiplies the "tricks" by two and adds it to his 100 points. If the bet is not won it calculates and subtracts the difference to his score.
Player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

roll the die (1d6) & move forward; ending on an occupied space: meeple is going to be replaced; ending on a marked space: follow instructions glued on cover inside
remarks: published ~ early 1950's in the GDR

Defifoo purple is a trick taking game that takes place in five innings. The object of the game and scoring conditions vary in each round by drawing card "challenges". The goal is to lose the fewest possible points after five rounds.

There are cards with five colors numbered 1 to 10 corresponding to five planets, some joker cards with two-colors that can change the planet and a Sun card.

At the beginning of the round the challenge-card is drawn what shows the scoring for that round.
Players play the cards following a suit. At the end of the turn, one that played the highest card wins the trick.

Each player starts the game with 1000 points. At the end of each round, we proceed to the number of points depending on the challenge of the round. The player who lost the least points after five rounds is the winner!

Contents: 

     64 cards, 
     a score book, 
     a little pencil, 
     a velvet bag

The game is a sort of fusion between hold'em and scrabble. In each round, players must form the longest words possible by combining the cards in their hand and those on the table. Card system "challenges" imposes additional conditions on each spin: requirement to use a letter, the fastest wins, the longest word wins the cards etc.

If the deck is exhausted, the game ends when a player has got rid of all your cards, or that nobody can play anymore.

The scoring:
• Each card is worth 1 point earned.
• The one who got rid of all your cards double their points.
• The other players take off their total as many points as cards remaining in their hand. The player with the most points is declared the "winner!"

Like in Mille Bornes the goal is to be the first to reach the 1000 miles with some new attack cards included.

This game includes also electronique game console who decides attacking.

players compete to drive 1000 km, dealing with hazards along the way. Draw a card to your hand, play or discard. You must lay a green traffic light to start, play cards showing mileage, dump hazards (flat tire, speed limit) on the other players, remedy hazards (spare tire, end of limit) from yourself, play safety cards (puncture proof), and try to be the first to clock up the distance.

The goal is to photograph wildlife in safari earning 250 points.

In his turn player may play the cards with animals to photograph them, attack the opponents playing the cards with floods, swarm of mosquitoes etc. or defence against attacks by playing cards like canoe, a mosquito net etc.

Player who scores 250 points first is the winner.

learn the foods needed in the kitchen

roll the die & move forward; ending on a card space collect it if available; game ends if all cards are taken.

published ~ mid 50's in the GDR

There are 2 teams in this card game.
Players exchange the cards with each other trying to be the first to match 4 indetical cards in hand and make the secret, pre-agreed sign with their partner, who must quickly ring the bell and say "Kem's!" to score a point. But if opponent team rings the bell first and shouts "Counter-Kem's!" they score a point instead.

Team who has the most points at the end of the game wins the game.

Mapple is a unique abstract board game for two players, and you can play it with pocket change.

The rules and the board are all contained in the same one-page PDF file. Print it and enjoy it. And if you like this game, please make a donation to Cheapass Games, and keep checking back constantly for new and wonderful stuff!

Each player uses eleven coins: One quarter, two dimes, three nickels, and five pennies. One player is “heads” and  the other is “tails.” Players take turns placing coins in empty areas. When you play a coin, it flips over enemy coins in neighboring areas, if those coins are of lesser value. Areas that touch only on the diagonal are not considered connected. The game ends when both players are out of coins. Each area is worth one point. You will play twice, with each player starting once.

Source: The Publisher's Website

boardgame: The Gate: A Game of Myth and Magic
description:
The Gate is a role-playing board game that incorporates a wide range of natural, mythological, and symbolic elements. The game board has a unique, abstract style: images of keys, suns, and moons are set alongside pictures of dragons and other fantastical monsters.

The Gate is essentially a race to the center of the board. Players compete with each other as they advance through three levels of spaces arranged in concentric rings. The first player to the Gate wins. At the start of the game, each player chooses an identity from five different player types: Creature, Dwarf, Elf, Sorcerer, and Warrior. Players roll dice and play cards as they move clockwise around the board. In order to advance to a higher level, one must circle the level, acquire a key card, and defeat a monster. Throughout the game, players encounter a variety of enemies and use fighting dice and weapon cards to combat them. Players also battle each other using dice and cards. The advanced game employs additional cards as well as special fighting and moving dice.

The game box includes both the beginner and advanced versions. It contains one 28" x 24" game board, 333 cards (beginner and advanced decks), 8 metal pieces, 10 custom dice (beginner and advanced), 82 glass pebbles (used as currency), several rule cards, and a "scroll" card with the story of The Gate.

Paint the Line is an abstract for two players that uses Looney Pyramids. It is played on two boards - a 2x6 movement board and a 3x6 scoring board. Three trees each of four different colors are set up on the movement board. The second and fifth squares in the middle row of the scoring board are blocked off.

At the beginning of the game, each player takes two small pyramids of two different colors from the movement board. Players will score points for the two colors they chose at the end of the game. After choosing colors, players take turns moving pyramids on the movement board in order to capture other pyramids. A pyramid is captured when another pyramid moves on top of it.

Pyramids are placed in any empty square on the scoring board when captured. When no more squares are empty, captured pyramids are stacked on top of the other pyramids, but no more than two pyramids can be in a stack.

Players continue to capture and place pyramids until no further moves are possible. At the end of the game, points are scored for groups of pyramids on the scoring board, with larger groups and larger pyramids being worth more points.

Krump is an abstract strategy game for two players that is played on a 6x6 board. Each player starts with twelve units placed on the back two rows. The goal is to capture eight of the opponent's units. One player is black and the other is red.

Units are moved with a combination of movement cards and movement pyramids. Players choose movement cards each round and then place movement pyramids on the cards. A card can have any number of pyramids placed on it, but the pip count of the pyramids can't exceed the number on the card.

Players take turns during each round placing movement pyramids and moving units. When a pyramid is placed on a card, any one unit or stack that contains a unit of that size is able to move. Up to two units can be in a stack at one time, but a stack can not be broken once it is formed. Each unit and unit stack type moves differently, for a total of six different movement patterns.

Movement options early in the game are limited. As more enemy units are captured, however, players gain access to additional movement cards and can use the captured enemy units as movement pyramids.

Due to budget cuts,the entire police force in your city has been laid off and now every villain in town is running wild!
 The crime wave is raging out of control and it's up to you and your fellow costumed heroes to take matters into your own hands!
 Each player begins as an inexperienced vigilante with barely enough crime fighting skill to take on even the most inept villain. On each turn, players divide their time between training their hero, fighting crimes, and reverse-engineering villain technology to use as their own. 
By the end of the game each Hero is stronger, faster and equipped with the re-purposed Villain Technology he needs to take on even the toughest Arch Villain! The player who brings the most notorious villains to justice wins.

Explore the vast American wilderness and preserve the natural features you discover in order to create the greatest collection of national parks. A quick, simple deck building card game for two players.

The object of the game is to establish national parks, each of which requires a certain combination of natural features--wildlife, trees, geology, geothermal, mountains, water and desert. To discover these features, players explore (purchase) natural feature cards from a central row of five, which go into their deck. The feature icons on these cards can be used to establish parks, or to explore additional feature cards.

Truth or dare game with hellish bottle the game machine what is placed at the center of the table, the mechanism is triggered, and the bottle spins itself. When it stops, the player pointed by the bottle: must respond questions truthfully or make an action previously agreed to score a point.

Player with the most points is the winner.

ESPAÑOL

"MAFIA: Nueva York" es un juego de cartas altamente estratégico, en el que cada jugador guía los designios de una de las cinco familias mafiosas de Nueva York. Ambientado en los años 40, las Familias deberán pugnar por el control de los negocios (tráfico de armas, prostitución, apuestas ilegales, ...) que se encuentran repartidos en los dominios de los cinco barrios de Nueva York (Queens, Manhattan, Bronx, Staten Island y Brooklyn). Cada Familia tendrá un objetivo principal a cumplir durante su partida, y varios secundarios que le darán la posibilidad de mejorar su familia.

Por medio del dinero y los Puntos de Respeto podremos ir haciendo crecer nuestra familia (capos, soldados), que a su vez controlarán los dominios y negocios que su familia tenga en su poder.

El equilibrio estratégico entre la defensa de nuestras posesiones y la conquista y asalto de nuevos dominios será la clave del juego, que no se ve libre de un cierto efecto "suerte", ya que los ataques, defensas y ciertas acciones requeridas por las variadísimas cartas de Evento nos llevarán a realizar tiradas de dados.

El objetivo final del juego es cumplir los requisitos establecidos por una carta de objetivo principal que se nos entregará al comienzo de la partida, pudiendo cumplir además tantos objetivos secundarios como seamos capaces y que nos aportarán Puntos de Respeto adicionales.”

ENGLISH

"MAFIA: New York" is a highly strategic card game, in which each player leads one of the five mafia families from NYC. Set in the 40s, the families have to fight for the control of their businesses (arms trade, prostitution, illegal gambling...), spread around NYC's five neighborhoods (Queens, Manhattan, Bronx, Staten Island and Brooklyn). Each family has a main goal to achieve during the game, and several secondary objectives that can make the family improve.

By means of money and respect points, each family can increase its members (gangsters, soldiers), who will help control the domains and businesses their family owns.

Strategic balance between defense of one's possessions and the conquest of new domains will be key to the game, but chance will also take part in it, as attacks, defense movements and several other actions required by many event cards will make us throw the dice.

The final goal of the game is to fulfill the requirements set in a main goal card given out at the beginning of the game, and achieving in addition as many secondary objectives as possible, which will bring us additional respect points.

description:
A crack up is a memory game based on the bolts to be manipulated.
Once a bolt has been screwed more than 8 times, the foot begins to cry.

The first player spins the arrow at the top of the foot. He discovers Hits (number of screws) he must perform. The player may screw his hits to one bolt or multiple bolts.

Player who screw one bolt the 8th turn is eliminated from the game.

The last player in the game is the winner!

User summary

A home-made Japanese card game, based on the so-called "dating simulators". Players compete for the attention of six girls, each with their own events related to them, and the player with the most events scored at the end wins. The game consists of general events, special events and activity cards. Players start with a deck of activity cards and some general events, though none are kept as a hand, but instead drawn one at a time and played. Each turn, a player can choose if he wants to gain a certain girl's special event card to add to his deck, or if he wants to gain as points the events he'll play. It's a matter of keeping in mind what you have in your deck and relying on luck enough to get them in the right order.

Translated box back
A game distilling the sweet-and-sour (or so 'tis written) romantic memories from fictitious school days. Players try to impress the different heorines, and gradually build their own decks with the memories they prefer!

Love or dislike… put into words there is no end to it.
My radical days keep going on and on (pun from a famour dating sim).

description:
From publisher's website.

In the years leading up to 1917 the ground conflict of the Great War was proving costly to both sides, and the battles fought in the air were equally as intense as technological advances and better tactics were developed.

The air war reached its peak between 1917 and 1918 as aircraft became fast, highly manoeuvrable machines; each pilot determined to attain a superior position for the fleeting seconds in which a target might pass through a gun sight.

Richthofen's Flying Circus, written by Owen Branham, is a detailed rules set that allows players to recreate this fast-paced aerial combat with one or more miniature aircraft. Each game turn represents a snapshot of the action as the machines move across the aerial battlefield and fire upon their opponent.

This full-colour, 80-page soft back rulebook contains rules for playing aerial combat games with 1/72 scale model aircraft, and can also be used with 1/144 to 1/32 scale models. Scenarios are provided for such missions as Dogfights, Balloon Bursting and Bombing, as well as rules for playing both narrative campaigns or 'top gun'-style leagues. Also included is a technical gallery and aircraft background information.

description:
Carcassonne: Die Kornkreise II (The Corn Circles II/The Crop Circles II), is the seventh official mini-expansion for Carcassonne.

A corn circle tile allows a player to place a second follower on a feature that they have already claimed, or to return an already-placed follower to his supply.

One of the six tiles of Carcassonne: Die Kornkreise II comes packaged with each of the other 6 mini expansions. Each of the mini expansions contains one part for the corn circles.

The rules are the same as Carcassonne: Die Kornkreise, but the tiles have different layouts. Part of the Carcassonne series.

Each player has a single game board which includes an area for letters in use, an area for the formation of a sentence and an area of ​​points account.
The game features consonants, vowels and Jokers replacing any letter of the alphabet.
At the start of each handle 6 letters are drawn at random: three consonants and three vowels, each letter has a point value (3 to 8) also corresponding to the number of copies used during the game.
Wildcards are 6 in number per player and have no point value.

Each player attempts to write a sentence with the highest number of points with the letters available. Once a sentence exceeds 150 points, the player can if they wish to stop the game. If the sentence is considered correct, the player scores points over its 100 bonus points if they used one or more wildcards, and 300 points if no wildcards have been used (and 500 bonus points if the value of sentence exceeds 199 points even with wildcard!).
Opponents mark only the value of their sentence. Of course an incorrect sentence does not relate points.

Then new round begins with new letters by drawing new letters.

The winner is the player who scores the first 1000 points.
Note: a sentence written without wildcard totaling 199 points and is called Super Volapük and allows the player to win the tournament immediately.

Color Claim is a two player game played on a 5x6 board using Looney Pyramids. At the start of the game, two multicolored stashes are placed on the board, one pyramid per space. Players then alternate turns moving pyramids or stacks of pyramids.

No colors are owned by the players at the start of the game. Whenever a player moves onto single pyramid, that player can choose to capture the pyramid instead of stacking on it. When a pyramid is captured, any other captured pyramid belonging to that player is removed from the game. The player now owns the captured color and will score points for that color at the end of the game.

Players continue to move pyramids and stacks until no more moves are possible. Scores are then calculated, with stacks scoring points according to the number of pyramids they contain and the size of the pyramid on top. The player who owns the highest scoring color wins.

boardgame: Death Ride Kursk: Advanced CSS Logistics
description:
[From introduction of enhancement rules and from company website.]
This model enhancement was made to provide more value to the Death Ride game series. For those who want to feel like you are really part of something bigger, these enhancements now allow a team of players to play much more like a real command staff. there are 4 primary staff sections in a military unit at Battalion and higher. They are the S1 which handles all aspects of personnel and replacements, the S2 which handles all the aspects of battlefield intelligence, the S3 who deals with the operations of the unit, and the S4 who is responsible to provide for all the supply, repair, and provisions of the fighting force.

This enhancement features the need for players to provide for the supplies that keep the battle running smoothly.  This will provide some additional critical thinking to decide how to plan for moving supplies from the back to the front where they are needed and used.  It will also allow you to perform operations at full strength even when cut off, at least until your reserve supplies run out.

This package provides for more detailed supply operations for the Death Ride Kursk series.  Supplies are represented as points and are stored at the supply units already in DRK and then moved around via additional supply convoys (supply trucks) as needed to support combat.  Units now can move out of supply at will and can stay in supply until the on hand stocks fall short.  This module will add a whole new dimension to the game and again has only a page of rules to digest.
 It comes with:
(2) counter sheets
2 page rules supplement
Example Card
Logistics Tables
(2) Logistics Charts

boardgame: Death Ride Kursk: Advanced CSS Maintenance
description:
For those playes of the Death Ride Kursk series who want a bit more detail in the play of the game and a feel that puts a player on the team in the seat of the S4, this enhancement package will provide you with a simple, uncomplicated, inexpensive way to make the play of the game more interesting, more realistic, and more thoughtful.  This package includes:
 a 1 page DRK rules supplement for the enhancement of maintenance operations
a Maintenance Track to hold the wrecks as they are being repaired
a Maintenance Chart that determines how long damage will take to repair and at what level (bn, Regt/Bde, Div, etc)
(3) unit counter sheets with some Recovery untis and wreck markers
 This package will be good for all 3 Death Ride Kursk games and there will be no need to purchase a separate one for each game.  This package serves all.  The package comes in a photo envelope and is meant to be added to one of the other games for storage.

It features the need for players to go and recover the destroyed units and bring them back to either their HQ, or maintenance units for repair.  Those repairs will take various times to be complete.  This will provide some additional critical thinking to decide how to recover these wrecks so that they don't get destroyed themselves and where to place them so that they can recover the wrecks in the most efficient manner possible.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Ravensburg, in Germany.

Spore is an abstract strategy game for two players that is played on a 5x5 board. Two multicolored stashes are required to play. All six pyramids of one color are the invaders. One of them is placed in any square on the board. All other pyramids are villagers. All villagers are placed randomly on the board.

Players alternate turns. Each turn, a player must activate every invader on the board. Invaders can either move and capture a villager or launch a new invader, though only one new invader can be launched each turn. If an invader is unable to perform an action when activated, it is removed from the board.

Players continue capturing villagers until every no further villagers can be captured. Players then score points for each villager captured, with a bonus for completed trees and an additional bonus for completed trees of a single color.

Poptrix is an abstract strategy game for two players that is played on a 5x6 board. Each player is given one monocolored stash of pyramids. Players start on opposite short sides of the board. On the first turn of the game, each player places a pyramid of any size in his or her starting row.

Players then take turns either launching a new pyramid or attacking. New pyramids are launched from existing pyramids and land up to two spaces away. Launched pyramids must be a different size than the launcher.

If it is possible for one of a player's pyramids to attack, that player must make an attack. Pyramids of different sizes attack differently and have different strengths. Pyramids are removed from the game when defeated. Victorious pyramids have a cap placed on them that gives them a bonus is combat. Each player only has one cap. The cap is moved around as pyramids win or are defeated in combat.

Players continue to launch pyramids and attack until all pyramids have been launched and no further attacks are possible. The player with the most pyramids on the board at this time wins the game.

La Bataille de Nancy: 5 Janvier 1477 covers the battle of Nancy that saw the victory of the young Duke of Lorraine René II over the Burgundian army and the death of Charles Le Téméraire, causing the end of the Duchy of Burgundy.

Forces of Lorraine have to rout the Burgaundian army that is besieging Nancy, the capital city of Lorraine.

Game mechanisms are classic (I go - U go). Turn sequence is:
- Lorraine: move
- Lorraine: combat
- Burgundy: move
- Burgundy: combat

Count density is low (Lorraine and allies 20, Burgundy and allies 10). Combat use a CRT (from 1/6 to 6/1) yielding results from attacker eliminated to defender eliminated (exchange is always possible on a 6 for 4/1 to 6/1).
Artillery bombardment is also possible.

At the end of the 11th round, players compare victory points. VPs are obtained through elimination of ennemy units. Players score additional VPs if they have enough units close to Nancy.

description: Monopoly using Texas themed properties such as The Alamo, the Astrodome and the LBJ Presidential Library.

Game uses 6 dice (3x3d6 + joker dice) and a game board with triangles, squares, pentas and hexes.

Object of the game is to perform the first all contracts with the dice. It may be possible to carry out two contracts simultaneously.

The contracts are: 
6 SUITES with different figures.
Fill the Triangle or roll 3 dice with the number 3
Fill the QUADRA or roll 4 dice with the number 4
Fill the PENTA or roll 5 dice with the number 5
Fill the HEX or roll 6 dice with the number 6

Player who perform all contracts first is the winner

The game about fractions.
Play with fractions through the two dice. 
e.g. The dice show a 3 and 5. Will you choose 3/5 or 5/3?

Object of the game is to roll the dice and choose the most appropriate fraction to bring all his chips from the first box to box DEPARTURE ARRIVAL crossing the proposed route.

Also player can opposing his opponents to block him or slow its progression.

Each player or team has 18 pieces on a circular chess board.
The pieces move at angles corresponding to their nature:
1/2 turn, 1/4 turn, 1/8 of a turn, check box, etc.
The game has richness and variety of movement of each piece result in many combinations.

Object of the game is make a contract making himself master of an area agreed in advance.
