Strategy comes into play as you make plans for your next move to defeat your favorite opponent(s).  Hide your tiles using the Player Tray.
     If you are lucky maybe you can “Shoot the Moon”, it is a play where you complete any shape and clear your hand by playing two (2) or more tiles all at once. Astromino rules include many options so you can choose from them and play the game as you like it.


Game Options

     There are six diferent games each one can incorporate these options to some degree.  Play options include Rule of Seven, Blank is a wild to play, Add a Shape,  Shoot the Moon, and scoring sevens or just scoring tile values with extra points for completing a shape.


Winning the Game

     Although optional scoring rules are included, the main goal of the game is to use all the Astromino tiles in your hand. When you are down to your last Astromino tile yell “ASTEROID!” Fair warning to the opponents you are about to defeat. You win the hand when all your tiles are played. You win the game when you win two hands.  Maybe you will be lucky enough to “Shoot the Moon” for the win.


Six Unique Games in one!

     Basic Line of Play Game
     Moons and Stars Game
     Sun, Earth and Moon Game
     Sumerian Sevens and Twelves Game
     Freeform Penrose Tiling Game
     Four-Stage-Star Game

Pitch It! - Who's Got the Million Dollar Idea? is a dynamic and social game requiring blissfully creative nonsense, but also a keen eye and sharp ear for what’s marketable. It is both fun and challenging! You are charged with the task of selling conventional products to unconventional clientele.

HOW TO PLAY
A combination of ‘What?’ card (product) and ‘Who?’ card (target audience) is picked. The goal for you (or your team) is to sell the ‘what’ to the ‘who’. For example, ‘potting soil’ needs to be sold to ‘police officers’, or ‘bath toys’ to ‘bankers’, or ‘sunscreen lotion’ to ‘NASCAR fans’…you get the idea. Summon your creative mojo to come up with a catchy idea for product title, slogan, and logo. Then pitch your best ideas to your friends. After that, sit back and listen to what others have done. Vote for the best idea presented – it may be yours, but nobody can vote for themselves. The winner of the most votes gets the $1 Million prize for that round. Play more rounds until someone reaches the ultimate goal of $5 Million.

boardgame: Live It Up: A Game About Drugs and Consequences
description:
Live It Up: A Game About Drugs and Consequences is a fun family game which educates about the serious consequences of drug use while reinforcing the positive aspects of a drug-free life. The primary purpose of Live It Up is to be used as an educational tool, but it is an enjoyable game that can be played casually by adults and young people as well.

Live It Up is a useful visual, audio, and kinetic learning tool. Parents and Educators can play Live It Up with their preteens and teens, while Therapists also will find Live It Up useful for rehab patients.

Live It Up is played with five kinds of cards: Live, Activity, Arrest, Overdose, and Sober cards. The cards are laid out face down on the table. Each player chooses cards and stacks them until they receive a Live card, in which case their turn is up. If a player pulls an Arrest card, their stack of cards go into Jail, only to be redeemed by a player who pulls a Sober card. If a player pulls an Overdose, all their cards go to The Grave, never to be played again. Players can choose to redeem their Sober cards whenever on their turn they wish, but the longer they wait, the more they risk having their own cards sent to Jail or the Grave. The object of the game is to build the most fulfilling life, and the player with the greatest stack of Live, Activity, and/or Sober cards wins.

Promo card given out in 2012 at various conventions and most likely will be available at Essen 2012.

Copa Motordead is an Event using the autoduel skill and awards 10 VP, 25 money, and can be rotated to give 2 Major Projects points.

The object of this game is to move your pawn along "love tracks" and reach the finish square. Rolling a die determines how far along the "love track" you may move on your turn. In so doing, you win time to kiss the person of the opposite sex closest to your pawn. When you land on a square with a heart sign, cupid sign, male sign, or female sign, you draw a card with the matching sign and follow the instructions. The first person to complete all of their card-driven tasks and reach their finish square first, wins the game.

From the game box: "Seven Minutes in Heaven is an entertaining game that will liven any party. The object is to strategically race along love tracks and reach the finish squares. You will find the journey sensuously exciting, intellectually stimulating, and emotionally thrilling. This game is a unique "ice breaker", because the fun experienced along the love track makes everyone a winner."

In an alternate history on the brink of global war, the rare telonite ore has been identified as the key to the revolutionary superweapon that could end hostilities before they begin. Unfortunately this ore only exists in meaningful quantities in a small, blighted archipelago in a remote area. Your superiors cannot spare many men or resources - it would be strategic suicide to move important units from their existing positions - but you've been given the vital task of securing enough telonite to ensure the victory of your nation. Unfortunately, the other world superpowers have sent task forces of their own...

Conquest of the Skies is a light 4X in which 2-3 players compete to gather sufficient quantities of ore before anyone else. Players will explore the abandoned archipelago, research and develop new technologies, manoeuvre airships and giant warships into combat, harvest the meagre supplies of food to keep their people alive and try to be the first to obtain enough telonite ore, by fair means or foul.

Play proceeds via an action point allowance, with each of a player's settlements granting a number of action points which can be used for harvesting resources, researching technologies and acting with units; however, food is almost as scarce as ore, and players must be sure to harvest enough to feed each settlement every turn.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Promotional game made by Waddingtons for Blue Label Fyffes Brand of bananas. Fyffes were the most famous supplier of bananas in Britain, mainly thanks to a catchy TV advert featuring the slogan "Unzip a Banana".

Top Banana is based on the Banana Industry, its pitfally and its successes. You travel around the banana-shaped board, buying plantations and then shipping your produce to the marketplace in Britain.

Although it is a familiar type of game, suggested by Monopoly, income is dependent on that which you can raise by making and selling (there is no "Collect 200 when you pass go").

The richest player wins if 'time' is called or if that player is the only one still in business.

The goal is to be the first to succeed three humanitarian missions in 3 different countries.
The board represents two circuits: an external circuit to collect the necessities of the mission, and an internal loop to achieve the mission.

In each round, the player rolls the dice and advance in the direction he wants and if his pawn lands on a square of an element it needs to collect, it takes that element, otherwise it says "I give" and can assign this element against a Solidarity card to the player who needs it for his own mission.

First player to succeed his/her three missions is the winner and is named the best chef.

boardgame: Order of the Stick Adventure Game: Zombie Gamers card
description: This is the Order of the Stick Adventure Game promo card released at GenCon 2006. It may also have been available for those who pre-ordered the original Order of the Stick Adventure Game. A single-card expansion, this promo card adds the "Zombie Gamers" monster to the Battle Card deck, allowing you to inflict a horde of foul-smelling yet oddly familiar creatures on another unsuspecting player.

DeepWars is a 28mm scale tabletop wargame set in an alternate history of Earth in the late 17th century, where fortune seekers and conquerors have discovered a lost land with an underground sea miles beneath the frozen surface of the southernmost continent.

DeepWars is set in the world of ShadowSea, but is meant to be a stand-alone product and is focused on adventure and battle scenarios on the bottom of the Sunless Sea. The game will have multiple types of scenarios, including exploration of wrecks on the seafloor, adventures in ancient cities of cyclopean structures, battles among fields of geothermal chimneys, submarine boardings among others. Adventures within flooded tunnels inside wrecked ships or crumbing cities may be played with “tiles” to represent stretches of passage and chambers, while games in open environments are played with scenic terrain pieces on the tabletop. This shadowy sea holds powerful artifacts from a long disappeared civilization, and the

Fortune Hunters have staked their claim to these treasures, modifying some of the technology they have already recovered to design advanced diving suits and weapons for underwater defense. They are not alone in their quest though, as other forces compete for the same artifacts. The enigmatic Dark Mariners, remnants of the noble lost civilization, have completely devolved, mixing their bloodline with the foulest of undersea life and twisted ether technology to create vicious biomechanical creatures. These forces cruise the depths, carried within monstrous vessels that resemble gigantic sea creatures, gathering artifacts to help them prepare for an assault on the surface lands. The Ancients of Atalán are the most advanced of all of the forces, for they are the last of the pure relatives of the lost civilization of the Ancients of the Shadow Sea. They are a small group, with a hidden undersea city many leagues away to the North, and have traveled under the ice to recover the technology of their lost kin follow them into the dimensional void.

Other forces will be included in the game, but the three main forces will be the focus of this Kickstarter project and will be the first ones to get miniatures.

The game is a journey on a world map that indicates the important places in the production of Cocoa.

In his turn player roll the dice, move around the board and will be asked questions about Cocoa!

The first player to collect enough bean-cards for correct answers is the game.

Introduction

"Gods of Fantasy" is a card game that puts you in control of fantasy creatures. You act like a God and you lead a tactical fight. Each player plays cards from their own hand. You can have Creatures, Items and Victory cards. All cards are drawn from a central draw pile. The game is simple enough that it can be learned and played in a short time, and the battles are different each time you play a new game. This is a game for 2-4 players and pairs. Experienced players should be able to finish one game in 50 minutes.

Object of the Game

To win, you must have more Victory points than other players.

Contents

When you open a game box, you'll find the deck of cards which contains 54 cards. Each deck represents the unique Creatures, Items and Victory cards. The cards are well-balanced and competitive straight out of the box. This guarantees that you will have a satisfyingly unique game experience every time.
Each game box contains: 
54 card deck designed by professional artist, and a Manual in full color with rules and lots of examples of play.

Setting up the Game

This game can play: 
2 - 4 players 
2 vs. 2 (playing in pairs)

Playing the Game

Please for further instructions read the PDF manual.

Important

There is only one deck and that's it! You don't have to buy special cards to make better decks!

The goal is to bring the most of cups of Nesquik at locations of its own pawns, in the area called "Safety Zone". Whoever has the most cups won.

Each player roll the dice in his turn and moves either his checkers or cups (yellow pins) on the ground the number shown on the die. 
It is forbidden to cross a pond, a hedge, a pawn or a cup or, to be with two checkers on the same box. A pawn of the opponent can be eliminated when you're passing with one of his own pieces.

Player roll the dice and move on the board similar to Game of Goose and try to find advertising slogans as original as possible according to the boxes they landed.
Several boxes have known brand products containing bizarre ideas. You need to find the best slogan for this product.

examples:
"Terraillon Launches scales for two-seater twin brothers"
"Pepsodent Launches Toothpaste with polka dots."
etc.

You're exobilogist!
Space university of unknown life forms (SUULF) gave you task to find new, unique animals, plants and humanoids from outer space.

During game players collect cards (which reflect information about some species) from 4 decks (1deck=1planet) for making up encyclopedies, articles and guides or they just can deliver some exponates to museum of SUULF :)

During each turn players can do 1 action, which are:
Explore planet- (take a card from planet decks)
Write text- place 2 cards from your hand facedown
Publish text- get VP for text you've written
Give exponates to SUULF museum- discard cards which are not useful (for VP)

Game ends when there is no unrevealed cards in the planet decks.

The one who manages the best sets of cards will get more VP and become GREATEST EXOBIOLOGIST.

Rock, Roadies and Roll is a game for 3 to 5 players where one must guide a band from the humble beginning playing in garages to the heights of fame! Bands are defined by four attributes which can be increased throughout the game: Confidence, Talent and Guitars, and Amps.

In Phase 1, players hire up to 3 Roadies and add these to their band of 3. They will place these 6 'workers' on action spaces such as: Drop off Demo tapes, Hang up Concert Posters, Record Album, Buy/Sell Gear, etc...

Phase 2 is a cash auction for Booking Agents which will lend their precious 'Leverage' to the band to get them gigs for the weekend.

Phase 3 is an auction using the Booking Agents' leverage to try to secure the best gigs for the weekend. Players can pick up gigs for Friday and/or Saturday.

Once the bidding is over, or all players have 2 gigs, players will 'play' their gigs. Add the band's confidence to 1D6 and compare to the difficulty of each gig to see if the Band bombed or blew the roof off! Increase your fame accordingly, get paid and cash in on royalties.

When the last gig card is drawn, complete that round. Whichever player has the most Fame, is the winner!

"An educational activity to teach and reinforce vocabulary skills" says the cover of the rulebook. There are four decks of cards, ranked by difficulty, that each contain three words, and their definitions. The basic game is for somebody to read all three words, and one of the definitions. The person or team whose turn it is has to identify which of the words goes with the definition read. A spinner decides what level of word (1 through 4) is used, and if the guess is correct, that team earns 100 to 400 points. First time to 5000 wins.

Choose a rum barrel as your game piece. Highest roll goes first, then play continues clockwise. Roll the die move that many spaces.

Landing on sea snake spaces: take the sea snake, to cancel out all negative playing fields

Landing on Captain of the Ghost Pirate: roll a second time during your turn.

Landing on creepy skull: a player misses one turn.

End of Game: The player who reaches the Ghost Pirates' ship with the sea snake wins the game. If a player reaches the goal without the sea snake, he has to turn back to the  blue field and continue from there.

components:
6 various colored barrels
1 Ghost Pirate
1 sea serpent
1 playing mat
1 die

Welcome to the world of Zulu 6: the world's deadliest covert special missions force.

Assembled from the ranks of the most elite special forces units from around the world, the 6 warriors of Zulu Team are the guardians of humanity against the evil faction known as S.C.A.R. (Soldiers of Chaos, Anarchy & Rebellion).

Six of the world's top ten elite fighting forces are represented among the members of the Team: the Russian Spetsnaz, the French Naval Commandos, Israel's Shayetet 13, the U.S. Army Airborne Rangers, U.S. Marine Special Operations Command and the U.S. Navy SEALs.

Choose your side: one player controls Zulu 6 while the other controls S.C.A.R. The objects of the game: During each mission, Zulu 6 must use their elite combat skills to complete 2 mission objectives. S.C.A.R. must stop them with the use of overwhelming forces.

The game begins with the Zulu player choosing 3 of the 6 team members as his/her first assault team while S.C.A.R. player builds the game board by laying out 4 inch map tiles printed with a dirt path cutting through a jungle, placing each tile so that the path is continuous until the line of sight ends in a curve or a 3-way "T". As the Zulu 6 team moves along the path, the S.C.A.R. player continues to build it. Some of the map tiles contain hazards designed to slow the Zulu 6 team members while others contain mission objectives such as a prison camp, a weapons facility and a downed spy plane. There are also map tiles that contain symbols that allows the S.C.A.R. player to place a number of his troops in that tile to keep Zulu 6 from completing its mission. The S.C.A.R. player also has the ability to spawn more troops to keep the danger moving.

Combat between the 2 armies is resolved using 6-sided dice (D6). Each Zulu 6 team member has a stat card that explains his special skill and lists how many attack dice he gets during combat as well as areas to keep track of ammunition spent during combat (10 slots) and wounds taken (3 slots). Each time a Zulu 6 team member attacks, a marker is moved 1 space along his ammo track. The same happens when a team member takes a hit. Every roll of 5 or 6 on a Zulu 6 die roll is a kill. Each S.C.A.R. trooper only gets 1D6 and a roll of 6 on the die is a wound on the target Zulu 6 team member.

As the game progresses, Zulu 6 races to achieve mission objectives as S.C.A.R. sends wave after wave of troops at them. All the while, Zulu 6 receives 5 mission points for each objective achieved and for each S.C.A.R. troop killed and may use these points in trade for special actions such as healing a wound, an extra attack die, an ammo drop to reload weapons or even to call in reinforcements if a Zulu team member is lost in combat.

There are 3 missions to complete that make up the core game, each with 2 objectives.

With expansions coming soon featuring different mapped environments, daring missions, and new team members, you can take Zulu 6 around the world battling the evil forces of S.C.A.R.

boardgame: The Game of Safety and Danger
description: Promotional game from Coca-Cola. The board represents the walk to home by any child. It follows the traditional formula of 'Goose' type games. Move along and if you land on a coloured space, obey the instructions. The first player to reach 'Home', wins.

Salty Captain (Set of 8 cards)

The Salty Captain is a unique card that can be purchased during any Hire Captains phase by any player. It is the only card in Fleet that works that way and it is a great way to maximize your boats. The Salty Captains can be purchased instead of using a boat card as a captain. Salty Captains allow a boat to catch TWO crates of fish per Fishing Phase AND to hold EIGHT fish instead of just four.

Salvage Yard License (Set of 4 license cards)

Salvage Yard integrates into the license deck and adds a new way to generate cash for players that choose to use it. Salvage Yard allows players to salvage a boat card from their hand for its cash value in new boat cards. It is a powerful way to generate cash, but must be used wisely as it counts as a launch!

boardgame: Call of Cthulhu: The Card Game – The Unspeakable Pages Asylum Pack
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Some secrets should never be brought to light... Murder, madness, and terrible realities haunt the pages of Abdul Alhazred's horrifying tome, the dread Necronomicon.

The "Revelations" cycle for Call of Cthulhu: The Card Game moves to its penultimate Asylum Pack, The Unspeakable Pages. The game's rival factions delve into four versions of the Necronomicon, and a host of new characters seek to gain power from its secrets. Meanwhile, the ignorant masses continue their mundane routines, blissfully unaware of the Ancient Ones, monsters, and magicians who seek this dark knowledge to manipulate reality itself. While the key to the fate of the world may lie in Abdul Alhazred's dread tome, the "Revelations" cycle moves toward its climactic finale. Sixty new cards (three copies each of twenty different cards) deepen the game's eerie Lovecraftian horror as cultists, lunatics, and investigators all delve into The Unspeakable Pages!
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Call of Cthulhu – The Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
(From the product cover)

STAR FLEET BATTLES
REINFORCEMENTS #2


     216 ADDITIONAL PLAYING PIECES


Additional starships, seeking weapons, and markers to handle large battles.  Completely new playing pieces for all races, including the new ships from SSD Books #7, #8, and #9, plus web anchors, cast web, and drone swarms.  Also included are additional markers to record special events and locations.


     14 NEW PLAY-AID FORMS


Ready Reference Cards for electronic warfare and terrain; record forms for drones, plasma torpedoes, minefields, shuttles, and special events; special Energy Allocation Forms for carriers, Andromedans, and large ships; a small-scale map to record your battles; and a new Master Weapons Chart.


     3 NEW FULL-COLOR PLANETS

A strategic American football action game with hidden movable defenders and a fold-out stadium. Gameboard inserts are available for conversion to Basketball, Soccer, & Ice Hockey.

Before each play, the Defensive player (D) secretly sets his Linebackers -- 3 blue INDEPENDENTLY MOVABLE static-cling dots -- on a hinged, clear plastic, overlay.  He then swings the overlay against side of stadium and hides the Linebackers with a large black card.

The Offensive player then studies the tacklers printed on the field, HIDES HIS EYES WITH HIS PLAYBOOK, and either marks a line on the field with an erasable pen on a run, or lifts the pen and tries to land on a pass receiver (and then runs from there).  D tells him when he runs into a tackler, and then both players look at the field while D flips the overlay onto the field to see if a Linebacker tackled him sooner, caused a fumble, or batted down/intercepted a pass.

From the website:
"With an erasable pen and your EYES COVERED by a Playbook, how well can you evade tacklers, pass to receivers, or execute kicking plays?  That’s the challenge of Out of Sight Football! But beware your opponent’s hidden Linebackers! They can Stuff Runs, Intercept Passes, Cause Fumbles, or Blitz when you least expect it! Includes Kickoffs, Punts, Field Goals, Sacks, Safeties, Blocked Kicks, Fake Kicks, Laterals, Run Blocking, Injuries, Clock Management, Slo-Mo Replays, and a Playbook with fifteen offensive plays plus variations (left, right, middle) for OVER 40 total play choices."

A great way to learn American football.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Expansion to the Swedish version of Brainstorm, published by Casper. Contains 360 new categories of 10 words each. Also contains a new rules version introducing the opportunity to challenge an opponent, tweaking step 3, 4, 6 and 7 in the turn.

Despite what the name indicates this Swedish game is a draw-and-guess game. The game consists of a number of cards with words or phrases on them in different categories. The players take turn trying to make a fast sketch illustrating one of the words on a card. All the other participants try to guess and points ar distributed if you guess correct and to the sketching player if someone manages to guess right. You only count the points, there are no pieces on a board.

It is in many ways similar to Pictionary but without a game board and not played in teams. The categories are (translated from Swedish):
Objects
Verbs
Abstract terms
Names oc Titles
Technical terms
Proverbs

Full sysselsättning ("Full employment") is a game about managing a borough / local council and how to handle the tax payer's money in order to best put them into use.

The game consists of a large plastic circular board on which the players get a 6th. The board is separated into squares on which the players can build. By doing so they manage differend aspects of a township, like a political administration in a small town. The game has a path around the edges on which the players move. Those spaces give them different opportunities and makes the players dra cards containing various events, much like the cards in Monopoly. One interesting thing in this game from 1975 is that the players mark their buildings on the plastic board using whiteboard markers. This must have been a rather early use of that in a boardgame.

Includes land development, unemployment issues, building of inustries, malls, water cleaning facilities, summer house areas and family homes. Four different variants of the rules provide different levels of difficulty. There is also squares for "?"-cards which is a variant where the players are meant to construct cards of there own to tweak the game.

Swedish game about running a lokal athlete club, where you as a manager will have to deal with player salaries, national review boards, tax administration, sponsorships etc.

The game has borrowed a lot of mechanics from Monopoly, but instead of streets the board consists of a number of local business, local to different areas i Sweden. The game exists in a number of variants, similar as a game but with different local business on the board. The players make deals with the business (instead of buying the street) and build arinas and VIP-booths (instead of houses and hotels). There are random events on cards and you walk around the board using 2 dices. There are also charade cards (!) where you are awarded money if you manage to get the co-players to understand the charad. The one who has the larges fortune after a set time is declared the winner.

Object of the game is to discover different characters through puzzles.

There are many prominent historical figures: politicians, entertainment, literature, art, real characters, mythical or imaginary, but always with a certain reputation.
Clue to four per person, are arranged on cards, in descending order of difficulty.

If player discovers a character he advances his pawn up to 4 squares on the board. The winner is the player who first reaches the fifth level of the pyramid (in the top easier).

English:

TOGETHER WE ARE STRONG!

A game about problems and their solutions for 2 - 4 players.

In our daily lives we are often faced with various problems, which may harm our contentedness and feelings of happiness. These problems may be of bodily, social and/or mental nature. To solve these issues, there are numerous solutions (or at least approaches) and helpful people covering all aspects – bodily, social and mental.

This easy to pick up, educational yet fun-to-play card game tries to recreate these aspects of our living reality in a more or less abstract manner. The game should be a cooperative experience, in which not one is declared winner and the others losers in the end. Not selfishness and only striving for one’s own benefit, but rather helping each other should pave the road to victory. All players win TOGETHER. Nobody should be left behind. Thus the name of the game!

In "Together we are strong!" players cooperate to solve their problems or problems of other players by playing "solution" cards which can be boosted by "helper" cards. At the beginning of his or her turn, the active player draws cards from a shared 60 card deck containing problems, solutions and helpers. For each problem a player solved on his or her own, he or she receives two smiley-chips (Victory Points). If players work together to solve a problem, each player involved gets one smiley-chip. When the draw deck is depleted, all players must have reached a minimum of smiley-chips / Victory Points (depending on the number of players) in order to win the game. If one or more players have not reached that goal, everyone loses.

Developed by a studied social worker, this game is ideal for social workers and other people working in the social sector as well as for people/families who have experience with psychosocial problems, while still providing a pleasant gaming experience for other interested gamers as well.

Français :

L’UNION FAIT LA FORCE !

Un jeu de problèmes et de solutions, pour 2 à 4 joueurs.

Nos vies quotidiennes regorgent de problèmes de toutes natures, psychologiques, relationnels ou physiques, de sorte qu’il n’est pas toujours facile de conserver notre équilibre et notre confiance en nous. Heureusement, nous pouvons recourir à des gens compétents et à des recettes éprouvées pour les surmonter.

Dans ce jeu facile d’accès, éducatif, mais qui n’oublie pas le plaisir, le but est de recréer ces aspects de la vie de façon quelque peu abstraite, et de les combattre tous ensemble, d’où le titre ! Il n’y aura ni vainqueur ni vaincu dans ce défi avant tout coopératif ; l’entraide, et non l’égoïsme, sera la clé de la victoire. Nul ne doit être laissé pour compte !

Les joueurs doivent utiliser et échanger des cartes de Solutions et de Soutiens pour résoudre leurs Soucis et ceux des autres. Chaque problème surmonté rapporte deux pions Sourire, que l’on partage si l’on s’est fait aider par un autre. À la fin de la pioche, chacun doit avoir gagné un minimum de Sourires qui dépend du nombre de joueurs, sinon tout le monde est perdant.

Développé par un professionnel du secteur social, ce jeu sera idéal pour vos collègues, votre famille, ainsi que pour quiconque connaît, ou a connu, des expériences difficiles. Mais tout le monde peut aussi y jouer rien que pour le plaisir.

Scenario expansion for the Cheer Boys Cheer ruleset for the American Civil War.  Four battles, all fought in 1861, with orders of battle, historical background, maps, rules and historical outcomes.  Scenarios consist of:

Blackburn's Ford
Buck Hill
Greenbrier River
Belmont

description:
Scenario expansion for the Cheer Boys Cheer ruleset for the American Civil War.  Four battles, all fought in 1864, with orders of battle, historical background, maps, rules and historical outcomes.  Scenarios consist of:

Olustee
New Market
Mine Creek
Morton's Ford

boardgame: Adventure 1: The Mountain Keep (fan expansion for HeroQuest)
description:
The Game of Hero Quest lives on in these new adventure series of games. Each adventure is a single story that are 14 quests long. The Heroes start out with their beginning status and build up their strength as they fight their way through each castle. In the end they must have a fight with Zargon to survive. Will they succeed? 
These games' pdf files are available for downloading. They are made in a way that will allow you to make the books and cards. The pages are available at "Dragonkingdoms.co.uk" and yahoo's group site "Hero Quest Game board" for free. You do need to join the group to access the file's section where they are stored. The photo section of this site will allow you to read the story and look at the maps to see if this game is something you would like to play. Your adventure awaits!

boardgame: Total Strategy: Z, Arena of Combat
description:
Total Strategy is a 2 player strategy game.

Both players take control of an army of 32 units in the arena. The armies are made up of 12 different types of unit including infantry, cavalry, chariots and war elephants. Each unit has its own strengths and weaknesses so a careful balance of strategy is required to be a successful general.

The object of the game is to out maneuver your opponent and capture their general.

The game-play is historically realistic, players can use maneuvers from classic battles directly in the game to improve their chances of victory. This level of realism makes the game both easy to learn yet deceptively difficult to master.

Players take it in turns to move all their pieces, moving and attacking. Play then passes to their opponent. The winner of each attack is decided by the combat pot. The game finishes when one player captures the opponents general.

As with all Total Strategy Games the game is designed to be customisable by the players once they become proficient.

Grab a spatula!  In this childrens' dexterity game, players race to flip burger patties in order to collect toppings shown on the underside of the patties.  Once the four burger/topping combinations have been collected, players gather buns to top their burgers.  The first player to complete their four burgers wins!

In this zany expansion, Cartoona creatures can become even stranger!

It includes six neck tiles that can be placed in-between a head tile and a front body tile. These neck tiles can be placed even when the head and body have already been connected! They can also be stacked on top of one another for some seriously long necks!

Neck tiles can also be placed on the creatures of your opponents in an attempt to disrupt single-color creature scoring, or give negative points with the "crick in the neck" tile.

Below are the neck tiles included in the expansion:

     One yellow and purple dual-color neck, worth six points
     One yellow crick in the neck, worth -3 points
     One magenta neck with Adam's apple, worth 4 points
     One blue neck with a collar, worth 5 points
     One wavy magenta neck worth 3 points
     One straight magenta neck, worth 3 points


Four new cards will come with the expansion including:

     One "remove neck" tile card
     One "steal neck" tile card
     One "-4 pain in the neck" card and
     One "use a neck as any leg or tail" tile card

The best face wins the game!

In Funny Faces, players take turns drawing a card and making a face that matches the illustration on that card.  The other players then race to place their token on the tile on the gameboard that best matches the first player's expression.  The player who correctly guesses the match collects the card.  First player to collect three cards wins!

Glory to Rome: Republic Expansion has been in the making since 2007 or thereabouts, and with the publication of the Black Box edition of Glory to Rome in 2012, it has finally become an official variant/expansion. (The Republic Expansion is included with GtR Black Box; this card set makes the expansion available to those who own older editions of the game.)

Playing with the Republic rules, players will find Glory to Rome a bit more forgiving (especially to new players) and more balanced in regard to the Craftsman/Architect roles. The Republic Expansion includes 15 cards:


     Crane (x6), which replaces the Circus and allows you to use a card to lead or follow Architect.
     Domus Aurea (x3), which replaces the Coliseum and lets you demolish incomplete buildings with no materials when performing the Legionary role.
     Forum Romanum (x3), which replaces the Forum and allows for an instant win if you have one client of each type and one material of each type in the stockpile.
     Tribunal (x3), which replaces the Senate and allows you to draw one Jack when performing the Thinker role.


When using the Republic Expansion, players start with five order cards in hand (instead of four order cards and a Jack). Players may also Petition with two cards of the same role instead of three.

description:
A travel-sized version of Monopoly, with all of the pieces of the standard game (including Chance and Community Chest cards), just smaller, unlike Monopoly: The Portable Property Trading Game, which has some game play differences, such as using three dice and a chart rather than Chance and Community Chest cards.

Gameplay

On his turn, a player rolls two dice and moves that number of spaces around the board. If the player lands on an as-yet-unowned property, he has the opportunity to buy it and add it to his portfolio or allow the bank to auction it to the highest bidder. If a player owns all the spaces within a color group, he may then build houses and hotels on these spaces, generating even more income from opponents who land there. If he lands on a property owned by another player, he must pay that player rent according to the value of the land and any buildings on it. There are other places on the board which can not be bought, but instead require the player to draw a card and perform the action on the card, pay taxes, collect income, or even go to jail.

Goal

The goal of the game is to be the last player remaining with any money.
family: Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

description:
Seven-a-side miniature version of the bar-football games you see in pubs or clubs.

Each player stands at halfway, on opposite sides of the board. Once the ball is in play, both try to swivel their players (using levered handles on their side), to kick the ball into their opponent's goal. This game tends to get more frantic as the game progresses.

A home version of the arcade game where you use flippers to send the ball to the opposite end of the pitch. This is a reduced size so that it can easily be played on a table.

Both players stand at opposite ends of the pitch. Put the ball in play and frantic flipping becomes the order of the day. Whoever scores the most goals, wins.

The Game of the Sixties is the game of questions, mimes, songs or drawings.

The box comes in the form of a jukebox: the outcome of the dice indicates the category with which we will play. Mostly, this is to answer questions, but sometimes you need to make small fun events. We can then find themselves dancing the twist, to mime or sketch a profile of a star.

Yards is a two-player football-themed strategy board game. The game begins where the most exciting football games end: it is sudden-death overtime, and the first team to score a touchdown or field goal wins. The game is played in drives in which players alternate driving the ball towards opposite ends of the field.

Players are dealt cards with simple patterns on them, and then set up their pieces in formations of their choice on opposite sides of a line of scrimmage. Each player, on their turn, may then choose to move one piece one space, or move several pieces at a time--but only if the pieces on the field match the patterns on the card. The pattern cards may be rotated and combined to set up dramatic breakaway plays, but only if they can outwit the defense.

Playtime is approximately 30 minutes. Ages 10 and up. Solitaire variant also available.

6 violins, 4 violas, 3 cellos, 2 basses, 5 Wind Instruments, 5 Percussion cards, 4 Brass, and even a harp and a piano are gathering for a harmonious concert.

You draw a card and put in front of you. You replay if you draw an instrument of a family You already have. 
There are also 3 special cards in this game:
The conductor can take a card from another player. 
Phone and alarm clock are not good for the ears and You lose a card!

Try to collect similar families and take pleasure in having the full Philharmonic Orchestra to win the game.

Contents:

     31 large cards representing the instruments,
     3 special cards: conductor / alarm clock / mobile

Cardgame based on Kemps

There are 4 different type of cards with Italian characters, and two cards for each player: Meals served and Bad food. You must collect 4 cards of the same family of Italians by changing the cards with opponents.  You need to define a sign with Your partner and when this sign is done, your partner will then play the card "Meals served". But if opponent's team play the card "Bad food" before this they score a point instead Your team.

boardgame: Talisman (Revised 4th Edition): The Blood Moon Expansion
description:
Game description from the publisher:

With over 100 Adventure Cards, plus new spells, characters, and alternative endings, Talisman: The Blood Moon Expansion adds a gripping layer of gothic horror to your quest for the Crown of Command.

In addition to a host of new challenges, including a bloodthirsty Werewolf that prowls the land, time alternates between "Day" and "Night" under The Blood Moon. The new Time Card flips between its Day and Night sides to indicate the inexorable passage of time. Whenever a player draws one or more Events on his turn, he flips the Time Card, a seemingly minor change with a range of game-altering consequences. Whereas creatures are slightly weaker in the cold light of day, they grow bolder at nightfall.

What's more, a number of new Adventure Cards have varied effects depending on the time of day; the night can usher in the Witching Hour, during which spells are more prevalent...or a thick Moon Mist can descend on the land, inhibiting safe travel until dawn.

description:
In Matchpoint, the first title in The Game Master's "Smart & Fun" game line, players take turns placing one of two tiles in hand onto the playing area, turning the tile this way and that to determine the best way to earn the most points.

More specifically, each tile is comprised of four colored quadrants, with each quadrant having a number of pips: purple has five pips, blue has four, and so on. One tile is placed face-up on the table at the start of the game. On a turn, you place a tile next to tiles already in play, then score the sum of all pips in the colored areas that you add to with the placed tile. (In the advanced version of the game, you multiply the sum of all pips by the number of colors that you matched on that turn.) You then draw a replacement tile, and the next player takes her turn. whoever has the most points when the tiles run out wins!

The rules also include a solo variant.

In Sharks with Lasers, you are a special agent trainee trying to pass an extremely dangerous field exam in order to claim your rightful place in the Mysterious Intelligence Agency (MIA). In this field exam, you are dropped into the fearsome depths of cold, dark waters and your goal is to reach a nearby island… alive. To obstruct any special agent trainee foolish enough to try and pass the exam an army of sharks equipped with lasers has been unleashed in the waters. You and your comrades are all in for a hell of a swim, as you dash for the island and your safety. Are you skilful enough to avoid getting blasted or worse, shredded by one of the fishy monstrosities? Fortunately, you brought along something to help you achieve your ridiculous mission: N’s little science project, a shark-with-laser-dazer. N’s device is capable of controlling the utterly dangerous sharks with lasers and it even comes with a built-in laser shield. That N, isn’t such a nerd after all.

Sharks with lasers is a fast paced boardgame, where players secretly play movement cards to move their agent. In addition, they jointly control the sharks, by secretly playing movement cards on the sharks. The game is played in rounds of which each consists of playing cards and subsequently resolving agent and shark movement. The first player who reaches the island alive, passes the special agent field exam, wins the game and becomes part of the special agent elite of the MIA.

boardgame: Le petit jeu des Princesses et Chevaliers
description:
"Princesses and Knights" in English
Card game on the evolutionary theme of princesses and knights. By assembling the cards by 4, we can reconstruct various skits. The cards are laid on the table. Whoever gets the first card of a sketch can begin. The player may now find next card of this sketch etc. and finish the 4-cards sketch first to win the game.
Contents: 32 cards for eight sketches of four maps (castle courtyard of the castle, tower, dining room - the illustrations are duplicated, once with the characters, once without character), rules.

The "Formula Fun" management card game.

Adopt the best strategy in order to earn maximum points on the different tracks in the Formula Fun championship.

You'll have to hire pilots depending on market offer and your income, give the best offer for engine and chassis contracts, while managing the unexpected: disqualified pilot, sponsor offer, financial issues, ...

In order to win, you have to use strategy, common sense, bluff and a bit of luck.

During the game, player will put pilots, chassis and motors in a public market and bid their money (20 million dollars in the beginning) depending on the value of the card.
Sponsors will be put into play without bidding, but no more than 2 sponsors per player.

Each turn, a player may play a circuit in order to start a race.
The race is only a comparison between the cards' value of each player, with the biggest total gaining the first place in the race and thus the best score.

Buy the best cards in order to win the races and earn points to win the game!

Similar with Memory

All photos are by Francois Koch. They represent different animals. Children must not find images that look alike, but those that show the same animal.

"The Lure of This Game Will Hook You on the First Cast!"

Players take turns rolling, moving around the board, and answering trivia questions related to fishing.  The winner gets a prize.  The players, however, are instructed to determine what that prize will be before game play begins. (The game does not include any prizes itself.)

The monks go to the gathering around the monastery.

The game board represents a path consisting of multiple branches in boxes. For these cells will spread face down cards representing different plants.

The first player chooses how many spaces it will move. The other players will move the same number of squares in the direction of their choice.
Once on a box, the player becomes aware of the nature of the plant in secret.
- If it appears on his card objective (each player has different objective), he takes it in front of him.
- If it appears on the card of opponent, he places it before him face down.
- If the plant appears nowhere, it stays in place face up.

The first player return to the monastery with its five plants is the winner.

Publisher Blurb:

Travel Edition

Take your facts and your crap on the road!  Imagination Fact Or Crap Card Game, the famous Hollywood sign once read ‘Hollywoodland’. Fact or Crap? From the day-to-day to the truly bizarre, you just need to just answer: Is it FACT or CRAP? Earn tokens from the bank if you’re right; lose them if you’re wrong. If luck is with you and you’re fast enough, you can earn double tokens in the madcap Rush Hour. The player with the most tokens at the end of the game wins. So, take the challenge, put your thinking cap on and remember it comes down to two words: FACT or CRAP? All new questions, same old fun.

From the rules sheet: "Tension triumphs as two to six take turns tossing chips into the can...  When you think the target number is reached, CALL IT!  Dump out the chips and count 'em up - if you're below, you challenged too soon and you'll lose points - wait too long to challenge, and an opponent may take the round!

Object: To guess when the sum of the numbered chips in the can equals or exceeds the target number announced by the first player in the round"

contents: Can, 6 sets of chips numbered 1-10, score pad, pencil, rules.

boardgame: Who's Got Game? The Game with Benefits by Neil Strauss
description:
SUMMARY:

Neil Strauss, seven-time New York Times bestselling author who brought you the international bestselling seduction books The Game and Rules of the Game is back with one question in mind: “Who’s Got Game?”

“Who’s Got Game?” adds a new twist to being social and can be played anywhere. Whether or not you’re currently a player or playette, there is more game in this box than in most people’s brains. Play it for fun, for training, or for a better social lubricator than alcohol. Like The Game itself, these cards can be used to break the ice and get to know people—whether strangers or your own partner—in minutes.

“Who’s Got Game?” is engineered to bring out the fun and attractive qualities in every player. From physical challenges to personality tests to secret conspiracies, “Who’s Got Game?” has it all. So if you’re looking to turn an ordinary night into a memorable adventure, this is the game for you.

OBJECTIVE:

Players take turns running game (script cards) on each other. The player with the most points after 12 script cards + The Final Challenge wins!

HOW TO PLAY:

Each round, one player is THE LEADER. THE LEADER reads a script card aloud to all the players. The script card, for example, can consist of personality tests, fun loaded questions, charade-like questions, handwriting analysis, palm reading, physical challenges, secret conspiracy missions, and more. The players respond to the script card accordingly. The player(s) who win the round receives points. (The script card states how many points the card is worth.) After each script card is completed, the next player going clockwise becomes THE LEADER. The player with the most points after 12 script cards + The Final Challenge wins.

The Farmageddon: Livestocked and Loaded expansion introduces animals (one per player) to add ranching to the farming side of your operations. Whereas Crops are relatively flimsy and easy to destroy, Animals are permanent. Each Animal will provide many points to your farming operation, assuming you're willing to pay!

The goal for this expansion is to create a balance between tactical Crop management and strategic Animals. It also broadens the strategic choices of the game and adds depth without too much complexity. Players will need to make a choice with their Action cards to lean into animals, crops, or both, to squeak out ahead. Along with the animals will come new Action cards, special animal abilities, and a little bidding to top it off.

In addition to animals, Livestocked and Loaded will introduce Weather Events. These will shake things up so that no game is the same.

The best part is that you won't need to relearn the game. Livestocked and Loaded is built upon the simple framework of the original Farmageddon game. On each turn you're going to Draw Crop cards, Farm and Play Action Cards, then Draw Action cards.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Rules for a grand tactical Napoleonic miniatures game.  Infantry are mounted three figures to 1"x2" base representing about 400 men.  Calvary are mounted two figures to a 2"x2" stand representing about 300 men and horses.  Artillery are mounted two men and a gun on a 2"x3" stand and represent about 200 men and 8 to 12 guns.  Each turn represents one hour of game time.

On his a turn, a player moves any and all units up to their movement allowance, then declares attacks.  An attack is resolved based on the combat factors of the units, the unit characteristics, and terrain.

The rulebook contains no information on how to design scenarios.  Players sometimes recreated historical battles or historical what-ifs.  Sometimes they played miscellaneous forces with no historical context.  Many games were played as part of campaigns.

Bestiary is a simple card driven two player game in which players create chimeras by adding different creature parts.

Drawing creature part cards from a common deck, players take turns to put them into play. When a card is played, a control marker is placed on it. Creature parts can be tails, middle parts or heads. A chimera is completed when it has a tail and a head. Several chimeras may be incomplete at the same time. A player may choose to discard a card and draw a new one instead of playing a card, or may choose to pass. When two players pass consecutively or the deck is depleted, the game ends. Players then earn score according to the sum of values printed on the creature part cards they played which form completed chimeras.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The set of Inuit Fisherman Cards for the Fleet game is a fun and strategic expansion.  (8 Cards)

Inuit Fisherman - Inuit are the native people of Nunavut, Canada. With the Nunavut Land Claims Agreement Act in place, Inuit fisherman can fish in Ridback Bay without a license, but still reap all of its benefits!

The power of the Inuit Fisherman is that he does not require a license to launch and NO captain needed! The best part is he still catches fish and counts towards all the applicable bonuses (launch, captain, and VP). Inuit Fisherman is a great card set that can be added right to Fleet and provide additional strategy and flexibility.

This is a one card mini expansion which was included in the White Wind game Rainbows to promote their games Tricks and Phantoms of the Ice.

The expansion consists of a new goalie named Tricks and a rules card. Tricks has a value of "0" but can foretell the defeat of his team. If he is right, the losing team wins the game, if he is wrong, he must be discarded.

This is a one card mini expansion which was included in the White Wind game Tricks to promote the games Rainbows and Phantoms of the Ice.

The expansion consists of a new foward player named Rainbows and a rules card. Rainbows has only a value of "1", but before a match, you may select one player from your opponent's hand and switch it with one of your players.

This is a one card mini expansion which was given out by the German online game store "Adam Spielt".

The expansion consists of a new defense named Elfenwizard and a rules card. Elfenwizard has only a value of "1" but if you have Elfenwizard in your team, you may perform two actions each turn.

Dominate is a two-player game of area domination with deceptively simple rules.

The game is played on a square 9 x 9 game board with 3 x 3 sub-regions enclosed in boxes – a Sudoku grid without numbers. For game purposes, the board can be broken down into 27 overlapping regions each containing nine squares. There are nine rows, nine columns and nine 3x3 boxes.

The board is empty at the start of the game.

Players take turns placing one, two or three stones as an orthogonally connected group on empty squares. Swap rules apply. Players may not pass a turn.

A player dominates a region by occupying any 5 or more squares in the region. The game is won by dominating 14 or more regions on the board.

The Eggsclusive is a set of limited edition Rooster cards you can use in the Chicken Caesar game!  Players can replace some of their own roosters with these eager chickens during game setup.  The following roosters are included in this expansion:


     Abus I - A somewhat familiar looking rooster for you history buffs.
     Baro I - This is another one for the history buffs and Peanuts. The most famous fighter pilot of all time.
     Plusho I - Plusho isn't exactly what you would expect from a Roman chicken politician, but sometimes just the "appearance" of leadership will do the trick.
     Cucullo I - "Hood" in Latin; this particular chap might remind you of a certain vigilante who steals from the rich to give to the poor.
     Magus I - This Rooster resembles a certain magic peddler who was once a little grey-er.


Note:  Stevus I was removed from The Eggsclusive due to legal concerns.

Sometimes you have to fight dirty to win.

The Fowl Play Expansion for the Chicken Caesar game comes with 8 new Rooster cards, each with unique art and abilities.  Players can replace some of their own roosters with these eager chickens during game setup to amp up the nastiness.

Springe (Two-Fisted Bluff) is a two-player game designed for play with a Shibumi set.  It uses a 4×4-holed board, and balls in three colors (white, black, and red).

Aim: Make a full line (orthogonal or diagonal) of your color on a level, or get 9 of your balls on the board, or place the top piece of the pyramid, or (if both players run out of balls to play) have more of your balls in play than your opponent and more of yours than neutral balls.

Rules: The 4×4 board starts empty. Each player (White and Black) starts with his 16 balls, and 8 of the red neutral balls. Keep this supply of 24 balls hidden from your opponent at all times during the game. Use an opaque container (e.g. the Shibumi bag) to receive rejected balls over the course of the game. It should be able to hold all 48 balls, just in case. Never look in this reject bag during the game.
On your turn, hide any one or two balls total from your supply into your two hands. Each hand now has zero or one ball in it. Now present your closed fists to your opponent. Your opponent must sit still (no leaning and peeking), then touch one of your fists. Put the ball in this hand (if any) on the growing pyramid. Put the other hand (without looking!) into the reject bag, and release the ball (if any). Always do this, even if the other hand is empty, in order to keep your opponent guessing as to which balls you have left. If you run out of balls before your opponent, simply pass each turn until the game is over: You will still select which hand your opponent gets to play each turn.

Spandrel is derived from Spaiji, and is a three-player game designed for play with a Shibumi set.  It uses a 4×4-holed board, and balls in three colors (white, black, and red).

Rules: The 4×4 board starts empty. The players, White, Black, and Red, take turns placing one ball of each color (yes, all three colors) in a contiguous, touching chain on the growing pyramid. The balls can be placed on board holes and/or on top of 2×2 platforms. The three balls can go on the growing pyramid in any orientation, as long as one of the three balls touches the two others.

Aim: The player with the largest group of connected balls of her color when the pyramid is full wins. In case of a tie, the last player in turn order wins (i.e. Red beats Black beats White in a tie).

Spurn is a three-player game (inspired by Néstor Romeral Andrés’ Spuzzle) designed for play with a Shibumi set.  It uses a 4×4-holed board, and balls in three colors (white, black, and red).

Aim: Be the last player to create a group of four or more balls of your color.

Rules: The 4×4 board starts empty. The players, White, Black, and Red, take turns placing one ball of their own color on the growing pyramid.  If you create a group of four or more of your balls, you are eliminated, and will add no more balls to the pyramid.

Spruit is a solitaire game designed for play with a Shibumi set.  It uses a 4×4-holed board, and balls in three colors (white, black, and red).

Aim: Score the most points you can, without losing the game by disconnecting a white or a black the others of its kind.

Setup: The 4×4 board starts with the 16 base holes filled as shown to the right.
The game is played from the 3×3 level up.  Empty the bag, and put 5 balls of each color (white, black, and red) into the bag.

Rules: Draw a ball from the bag, and add it to the pyramid according to these rules:  When there are no free spaces on a level, you may place the ball on any edge point of the new level; otherwise, you must push a ball from the side with a new ball.  White and black balls must end up touching others of their kind; red needn’t do this.  If a white or black becomes stranded, you lose.  You may not push balls off the pyramid.

Once the last ball is on the pyramid, you score according to the following:
7 points for a visibly connected path of red from corner to corner; otherwise, red is worthless.
A color (black or white) that is split into separate visible groups is worth the product of the number of balls in each visible group of that color.
A color (black or white) that is in one contiguous visible group is worth negative the number of balls in that visible group.
 Random Setup: Start with the two red balls in the corner, then put 11 white and 11 black in the bag.  Spiral clockwise inward from one of the reds, drawing a ball from the bag and adding it to the board.  Now spiral clockwise inward again from the same red, and each time you reach a white or black that is not touching any others of its kind, replace it with the other kind from the bag.  (Apologies for the difficulty in replacing the center four!)  Note:  The base may thus contain more groups than just one white and one black.
After setup, dump the bag, and put the usual 5 white, 5 black, 5 red in the bag to start the game.
When playing with random setup, you should play several games in a row with the same setup, so you can really experience the setup.

Multi-player Solitaire: Any number of people can compete, as long as each person has a Shibumi set.  If you do a random setup, everyone should copy one random setup, so everyone starts with the same base.  If players can see each others’ pyramids, each player should draw randomly from her own bag during the game; if players cannot see each others’ pyramids, you can have one person draw from her bag the whole game, announcing the color drawn, and the other players simply pull out that color and place it.  The latter allows for more pure competition, but the former is often more practical.

Sprenge is a three-player game designed for play with a Shibumi set.  It uses a 4×4-holed board, and balls in three colors (white, black, and red).

Rules: The 4×4 board starts empty. The players, White, Black, and Red, take turns placing one ball of each of their opponent’s colors (e.g. White plays a black and a red) anywhere on the growing pyramid — they don’t need to be touching one another. The balls can be placed on board holes and/or on top of 2×2 platforms.

Aim: The player with the largest group of connected balls of her color when the pyramid is full wins. In case of a tie, the first player in turn order wins (i.e. White beats Black beats Red in a tie).

Sia Doble appears to be a two-player version of Oliver Schaudt's Sia Solo, a solitaire puzzle game in which the solver tries to swap the positions of two large stones on the game board. In Sia Doble, each player has three large stones and – I'm saying this based totally on gut-feeling – needs to place those stones in the starting spaces of the opponent's stones in order to win.

David Parlett's Puzzle of Oz was initially designed as a solitaire puzzle played as follows: Five stones in each of five colors are placed in a bag. Draw four stones and place them in the four outer squares of the playing area, which resembles a fat plus sign. Then draw the remaining stones one at a time, placing each stone next to a stone already in position. If no two orthogonally or diagonally adjacent stones are of the same color, you win.

For this release, Parlett has included rules so that two players can compete against one another.

Players take turns adding wooden pieces of different shapes to a fragile tower. They also need to get their two playing pieces onto the tower. One piece is lighter the other heavier. Once in a while (the length of this while is determined by a random element), there are scoring breaks. The players then check the positions of their pieces and multiply the number of levels one piece is up with the number of levels the other piece is hanging over the edge of the board. When all pieces have been built, the game ends and the player with most points wins. If a player topples the tower before that, that player is the sole loser.

The game consists of three tracks, laid out by tiles: An inner one that both players use and two outer ones, used by only one player each. At the outset of the game, each player places three pieces on each tile in the two lanes s/he can use. 
Afterwards, the players take turns picking up all the pieces from one tile, then moving the pieces onward in the designated direction (the tracks are one-way and form a "round" track for each player, with the middle track being part of both players' tracks). At each tile they pass, they have to drop one piece. In the middle, they can choose which color to drop (if they have both colors in their hand), but when they reach the end of the middle track, the pieces are split up and continue their path in the different outer tracks. 
If a tile on the outer tracks ever contains more than six pieces, that tile is removed together with all the pieces on it.
During the game, there are scorings at partially random moments. Each player counts the number of pieces on his or her outer track and gets points accordingly.
The game ends when one player has less than three tiles in his or her outer track. Then a final scoring is done, with the other player scoring double. The player with most points wins.

Each player has a set of pieces consisting of different numbers of assembled cubes (resulting in different shapes). Beginning with a random start player, they take turns placing a piece on the base board. The following rules apply: 
- A player maynot play the same piece as the other player immediately afterwards
- The piece may not touch the last piece played by the other player
- The piece may not have any parts hanging in the air, reaching over the edge of the board or reach a height of more than 5 cubes.
When a player has managed to get rid of all his/her pieces (or neither player can legally play another piece), the game ends, and the player(s) who still hold pieces count the number of cubes they have left. This is the negative score. After two plays, the player with the lowest overall negative score is the winner.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Shadow and Flame
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Throughout the "Dwarrowdelf" cycle for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game, the heroes of Middle-earth have been tasked by Elrond to find the root of the increased Orc activity in the Misty Mountains. Through treacherous mountain passes, goblin ambushes, dark tunnels, and underground waterways, their journey leads them at last to the depths of Moria...and the terrible discovery that awaits them!

Shadow and Flame is the climactic final Adventure Pack in the "Dwarrowdelf" cycle. Its sixty cards introduce a new hero, three copies of each player card, and conclude the epic saga's narrative arc with an all-new scenario that pits the heroes of Middle-earth against a demon from the times of Morgoth in the fight of their lives!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
[GreekGuardians.com]
Theme:
Greece of yesterday was a different place than it is today. The age of Ancient Greece was frought with peril, sacrifice and prayer. To get anything done, people would pray to the gods for favor. To complicate matters, the right prayer had to reach the right deity in order to have any chance at success. It was very confusing and complicated life. To simplify this vast set of choices a Greek everyman or everywoman would face, I have chosen two paths that you and your opponents must set your sights towards. Order and Chaos, both are neutral, they are not bad or good in the classical sense, but rather a point of view. By the end of the first players turn, each player must align with Order or Chaos and build their strategies and play their cards towards that means.

Goal:
Each player draws cards that move players towards your final goal of 21 culture points. Each action, each king, queen, god, monster, philosopher, literally each card can either add or subtract culture points, cause mayhem, deny abilities, outright kill foes, or otherwise meddle towards your cause or to take an opponent away from their cause, for your chosen faction (Order or Chaos). There is one initial turn to set up and identify which faction, Chaos or Order, you will be gaining points towards and then there are 4 turns, one per season: Spring, Summer, Fall, Winter. If a player does not reach 21 culture points, at the final play of Winter, the player with the highest amount of culture points WINS!

Game-Play:
Players will determine who goes first and will draw (and play) cards in the correct slots (Gods, Titans, Stars, etc.) and each card will have passive and active effects that resolve at the end of each player's turn. There is a (normal sheet of paper size) game board that shows where different cards "live" Passive effects will usually be "Add one culture point towards either Order or Chaos" (depending on the Card's Alignment). Active effects (or action cards) will require using action points (each player gets 5 action points per turn; the game has 4 turns total, so each play and card management is very important) that can alter the landscape of the game and change fates FAST.

Unique Feature (Stacking):
Say a certain God card is played (let's say Zeus) and he is Chaos aligned. And say there is a chaos aligned star already in its slot and another chaos aligned Titan in her slot. There is a bonus, or stacking, effect of having a bunch of "like-minded" cards, so this combination of three cards (each say producing one chaos token for this turn, 1+1+1=3) there would be a +2 chaos bonus. So if the player can navigate the correct stacking of "like-minded" or similarly aligned cards, there are bonus effects that can speed up your plan (OR slow down opponents).

RoleChess 
is a combination of Chess and a role game that is played with pieces similar to those used in Chess. The movements of pieces differ a bit from those of the pieces used in Chess. Nevertheless, the biggest difference from Chess is in the progress of the game. 
Different types of pieces possess an energy amount of their own, and additionally, they have certain attack and defense coefficients, which when multiplied by the number of spots of a dice, determine the attack or defense score of the piece in question in a battle. The difference between the attack and defense score affects the energy amount to be lost by the pieces.

The aim of the game is to reduce the opponent’s Emperor’s energy amount to zero, while at the same time protecting one’s own Emperor from the same fate. The player who is the first to beat the opponent’s Emperor will win the game.

Product materials
Board and pieces are fully made of real wood. Game board is made of pine tree and game pieces are made of basswood. Game box is made of cardboard.
Product is designed in Finland and is manufactured in China. All components used in this product are environmentally friendly and information is found of all components. Product has a CE marking.

This game was published in 1904, and predates the Game of the North Pole which were both published by McLoughlin Bros.

The object is to be the first player to win a race with his "new air ship."  The first player to reach or pass the balloon in the sky wins.

boardgame: Phantoms of the Ice: Double Trouble & Magician
description:
This is a two card mini expansion consisting of two special players for Phantoms of the Ice which was included in the White Wind newsletter #3 ("Colours of the Wind #3").

Double Trouble is a value "4" forward player. If he scores a goal, it counts as two goals. Although if the other team scores, it also counts double.

Magician is a value "3" forward player. After he is played, you may dictate your opponent the card he has to play for the rest of the match (goalie, defense or forward).

Operational level wargame for fictional invasion to Kansai region in Japan at final stage of WW2. The game is inspired by the Race to Tokyo. However, this game is not race by two Allied Armies. Standardly one player control the US Army and another player control the Japanese Army.

The game play is repeat alternately movement and combat. The rule mostly same with Race to Tokyo.

The U.S. purpose is threatening the Kyoto, to lure out the Japanese Tokyo garrison.
The Japanese purpose is to have depleted the US units, to defense the Kansai region.
The actual winner determined by the control of the important city by U.S. Army and loss of U.S. units.

The game counters is not die-cut.

In this two player deduction, trick-taking card game players take it in turns to play Detective Graves and the Mysterious Mastermind who is planning to commit the crime of the century. Detective Graves must uncover the clues that will lead to the elimination of suspects and reveal the identity of the mastermind behind the plot.

Game play takes place over five locations, players take it in turns to play cards from five different suits and after a certain number of turns in a location the player with the highest score wins, either providing a clue towards the identity of the Mastermind (points for the Detective Player) or resulting in that particular piece of evidence being lost forever (Points for the Mastermind player).  Some cards may also be used for special abilities, but they often must be discarded to achieve this.

The game is played over two sets, each player taking one turn as Detective Graves and one as The Mastermind.  Points are scored for collecting/protecting evidence and correctly deducing who the Mastermind is. The player with the highest score at the end of the two sets is the winner!

All files are available in the file section.  There are tokens that can be printed out in the Gallery.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Dust Tactics: Building Accessory Pack – "Warzone Tenement"
description:
Easy to assemble into a host of different shapes and sizes, the Building Accessory Pack will have you fighting in an urban warzone in no time. Strike down your foes from the rooftops. Blast away at troops hiding behind protective brick walls. Lead risky hand-to-hand assaults in the dilapidated hallways of once great buildings. The Building Accessory Pack allows you to do all of this and more!

This expansion contains two more structure levels that can be used to build an urban battlefield for Dust Tactics. Get into cover and clash with your opponents at point blank range with this new expansion.

This expansion includes a 2 story building similar to the one included in the Operation "Cerberus" expansion. This will allow for building to building combat or as expanding the size and dimensions of the building included in Operation "Cerberus" if available to you. The rules for movement and combat in buildings is outlined in the Operation "Cerberus" rules available on the Fantasy Flight Games website, however this expansion does not require Operation "Cerberus" to be used.

UNRELEASED GAME

    
    
         Note that all details for this game are preliminary. If you have information about this game, you are invited to edit this section or submit corrections.

    


The animals are ready to dance, with the elephant wearing its grass skirt and ready to sway as the music swells. Dice drive the dancing in Limbo Party, and as the animals circle the limbo tree, they'll need to watch for the snake that winds its way around the tree. Ladders and vines can keep the animals safe if you know how to use them!

Information supplied by kind permission of Matthew Turner of Warwick University:

Spoof is a game of chance (but not without strategy) often played in a pub to establish who buys the next round. It has the quirky character that it is a game without a winner, just a loser. Basically it is a guessing game involving coins. Each player draws some number of coins between zero and three from their pocket and holds them concealed within a clenched hand. If more than three are held this is deemed to count as three. The denomination of the coin is irrelevant, its just the number that counts. The idea is then to take turns to guess the combined total number contained in all the players' hands. Its that easy ! One player starts by making a guess ('call' or 'shout') and then the guesses proceed clockwise until all have guessed a number that hasn't previously been taken. This is a single round of the game. All players then reveal their coins, a total is calculated (and checked!) and someone may be 'out' if they chose the exact number (there are no prizes for nearly). If this happens the not-loser will then sit out the remaining rounds of this particular game and the others will play more rounds in the same way until only two players are left. The player who loses this last round buys the drinks. If nobody guessed the correct number in any given round then the round is played again. Every time a round is played the first person to call rotates clockwise to the next available player.

Trivia game that tests people's hearing rather than brain-power.

A CD provides the sounds as players compete to recognise audio clips or sound bites. Played in teams, everybody takes part at the same time, recalling classic lines from the movies, deciding who said that in quotes, guessing the year from the news headlines, solving sound effects, identifying catch phrases and much more.

The team with the most points after 10 rounds is the winner.

The board has a clear central area and around the edge are 16 'slots'. Four Peanuts characters have four slots each. The characters are Charlie Brown, Woodstock, Linus and Lucy. Each player is assigned one of the characters.

Snoopy is in his Super Snoopy Dodgem Car which is friction driven. Rev him up and let him go in the middle of the board. The wheels are designed so that the car will chunter back and forth and eventually it should go into one of the slots, awarding that player points. Points are recorded on a score chart. First to complete their line in the chart, wins.

Contents
1 Playing Board
1 Score Board
1 tin plate Snoopy Dodgem Car
4 Coloured Scoring Pegs

boardgame: Which Way to the Lodge... or I Would Have Been Here Sooner But...
description:
Which Way to the Lodge is a fast moving, fun moving after ski game for 2 to 6 players... drinking, loving and laughing!

Players roll dice to move around the board. If a player lands on a on a question card space another player draws a card and asks the question on the card.  If the answer is correct the player moves ahead 3 spaces.  If the answer is incorrect the player moves back 3 spaces.  If a player lands on a Snow Bunny space the player must draw a card and perform the feat on the card.  Some of the feats involve drinking. The winner is the first player to reach the lodge.

SABEL, a adventure game

Became a hero, fight soldiers and wolfs. Enter to the castle and find the the book that will help on your destiny. Fight the boss and then escape before everything blow up.

This is a role and dice game. Each time that you fight an enemy, you can pick a card to get a power or the key to enter to the castle.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Published by Bandai in IF series.

The game covers the European theater of WW2 from German invasion into Poland to the end of the war.
One game map, 2 counter sheets, play aids, rulebook, 2 dice and counter tray.

This is  Shunichi Takanashi's second attempt to cover the whole European thatre. The first trial was Deutschland unter Geld. But, this is not a reimplementation.
This game has a bigger map and more counters, resulting in more details.
