This is your chance to forge the destiny of an age...
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Series: War of the Ring (Nexus/Ares Games)

description:
Publishers description from the game box backside translated in English: 
A safe way to school. Children are the weakest participants in traffic and are - justified - protected. But it is important, that the children themselves know traffic rules, so they can use their knowledge in real life. The basics for this are the traffic signs, one must know the signs and what they mean. In this game children learn the most important traffic signs just by playing. Each player that knows what a traffic sign mean can move his pawn on a symbolic pedestrians crossing one "move" ahead. Who knows the most traffic signs and is first to reach the goal?

Verkehrszeichen kennen is a quiz game. One player draws a card from the tile, shows the sign and asks the question. Usually along the lines of "what does this traffic sign mean". He hides the answer on the bottom of the card. For a correct answer the player who answered can go one step ahead of the playing board, which is made to look like a pedestrian crossing.

In MEGAcquire, a re-envisioning of Sid Sackson's classic game Acquire, players create and grow companies by playing tiles from their hand on the hexagonal grid on the board.

MEGAcquire is an exciting journey of strategy and luck, for three to six players, through the corporate world. Live in the world of fun and fortune as you maneuver to outwit your opponents. Build and merge companies while shrewdly buying, trading, and selling stocks to become the wealthiest player.

Each player builds companies by adding tiles as they increase wealth by purchasing stocks in these companies. Cash flow is obtained by selling majority and minority shares through mergers. It is not always prudent to have the most stock in the biggest company, if you can build and sell enough smaller companies to generate a large income. Many different strategies can be used to reach the pinnacle of the corporate world, but you will always need the “luck of the draw”.

MEGAcquire includes 182 tiles and 350 stock certifications in 14 colors (seven hotel chains and seven companies).

You are a gladiator fighting for Glory in the Roman arena. Savage beasts and vicious warriors are all that stand between you and eternal fame. Defeating your foes is not enough; you must win the crowd’s favor. In Gladiators, you can play as you want: from lightning-fast matches with simple rules to epic tournaments with extra components for added depth.

Bravely attacking face-to-face earns the cheers of the crowd while attacking from behind is harder to block. To make things more interesting for the bloodthirsty mob, the arena is strewn with dangerous traps and helpful objects. The crowd’s mood changes throughout the match, and by entertaining them you can win their favor and eternal Glory. Even your glorious death can win you victory!

The core of Gladiators is simple symbol-based play: 
Swords are your attack power 
Shields are your defense
Feet are for movement 
Stars are for special skills

When attacking and blocking, you compare swords to shields. If you have more swords than your foe has shields, you win and cause wounds. You spend dice symbols to move, use combat abilities, and play (optional) action cards – trips, head-butts and more that gladiators can learn above and beyond their core skills.

Think up a great name for your restaurant, then answer true/false and multiple choice questions about food, wine, cooking and related trivia, and earn Duckats.

Use your Duckats, skill and luck to improve your restaurant, by hiring Excellent dining room and kitchen staff, even if you have to steal them away from other players' restaurants. Be the first player or team to have ALL Excellent staff and the best restaurant and you win the game.

Sounds easy, but when you have to pay for renovations, plumbing problems or deal with a chef who wants to quit and work for one of your competitors, things start to heat up.

And remember, if you can't stand the heat, stay out of the kitchen.

Throughout the years no three words have captured the essence of "Church-folks" having a good time better than "Let's Have Church!!!" Finally all of your favorite Sunday Morning "faithful phrases" & "funny faux pas" have been made into a hilarious game the whole family can enjoy-"LET'S HAVE CHURCH!!!"

Answer Church surveys & Gospel music trivia, draw outrageous pictures, shake your neighbor's hand, & do your dance. Guess if the phrase, "He's a lawyer in the courtroom" is in the Bible or not? But don't think too long! The Church music timer is playing real "shouting" music in the background. Faster, faster, faster...TIMES UP!!!

With over 500 unique answer choices & challenges, your team can earn up to 7000 points in one action packed game! The team with the most points at the end of three rounds wins the game! So turn off the T.V. and open the box. It's time to play "LET'S HAVE CHURCH!!!"

boardgame: Surrender: Walk Through the Valley of the Shadow of Death
description:
A miraculous experience happened on Earth, JESUS the Christ!

Long-ago Jesus descended from heaven and he is one with the God. Meet and travel with your friend Jesus, he is the same yesterday, today and forever.

Surrender more than just a game it's an experience! a Bible Trivia board game. The game is stimulating, the artwork is captivating but the message is real. Ultimately, Jesus desires for us to get to know him. Travel the Surrender trail of 33 spaces, from Alpha to Omega you will land on circumstances and strengthen your bible knowledge through sentence-completion.

Get excited! Have fun and Surrender all to Jesus, its rewarding!

Finish the game with the highest credit score and most money, while navigating through life's twists and turns, and avoiding foreclosure.

The unique feature of ForeclosureOpoly is that there are 18 Trivia spaces on the board. When you answer the trivia questions correctly, your credit score increases and you earn cash. Challenge your knowledge. When you answer the trivia questions incorrectly, your credit score drops and you must pay a penalty to the bank. If you don't have enough money to pay your yearly mortgage payment (every time you pass Home) YOU LAND IN FORECLOSURE. That's when staying in the game really gets tough!

Only the smart, savvy and educated can stay out of foreclosure...or GET OUT OF FORECLOSURE!

Roll the dice to determine where you will start your hunt.  After reaching your hunting destination, begin your hunt by drawing a card. Each card will tell you what you have to roll to harvest your duck. Ducks move around the board as "shots" are made - hunt where the ducks are! First player to the limit of 10 ducks wins the game!

This is a game about animal behavior.  Learn who is your PREDATOR and which animals are you their PREDATOR. Or who can kill you and who can you kill.

When (2) animals land on the same island as another they are in a challenge because there is enough room on the island and a fight must occur.

Hop from island to island until you reach you pre-selected island from a card selected from the deck of cards.

Publisher Blurb:

As all players play simultaneously let's see who's the fastest to win the fruit...

Unique, quick and fun game that will keep you on your toes.
 Players must try and collect 6 fruit leaves before their fellow monkeys. However there is an additional obstacle lurking in the jungle. If players are not careful, a hungry gorilla may swipe the fruits that they have gathered. A colorful Toucan can also bring you luck and give you a chance to win more fruit.

The Object: 
 The first player to collect 6 fruit leaves is the winner.
 If the gorilla collects 6 fruit leaves first, then it's the winner.

Combination of tactic decisions, speed, memory and fun...

Fill the Barn is a card game in which players earn money by planting and harvesting crops to the barn. The game board is a barn with only enough room for three harvests each of six different crop types. Each harvest of a same crop pays a lower price than the previous harvest of that same crop. Once all three harvests of a crop have been filled, players may not harvest any more of that crop type.

In addition to planting and harvesting, players may fertilize a crop to increase its value, or irrigate to protect against drought. Drought or Bugs cards may be played to wreck your opponents' crops while they are still waiting to be harvested. Mice, Junk, and Clean-up cards add a further wrinkle by allowing players to change the availability of crop spaces within the barn.

As the barn fills up, the competition increases to outmaneuver opponents to fill the last remaining crop spaces, with an extra bonus paid to the player who "fills the barn" by harvesting the last crop to fit in the barn. The game ends when either the barn is filled, or when the 115-card deck has been exhausted, whichever comes first.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Ornitorum is the first game of Riachuelo Ludum labs, a games design workshop class, in Rio de Janeiro, Brasil . Our games course have two classes in year : march and august. And we search for new designs and game designers.

History :
In this Earth discover the power of ornitorrinco´s eggs and use them in spaceship propulsions. The player are space colonists and have to "produce" eggs for the fuel market. The market is savage and dangerous, but you can gain a lot of profit ! Robbery, traition, laughs and fun are present in this game !

Main Goal :
Each player has your "ornitorrinco´s farm", and needs to "plant" eggs for produce new ornittorincos. The players needs to steal ornitorrinco´s  eggs from another players to win the game. But it´s not a easy task, because we can use antimatter traps to protect the eggs.

Winning the game : 
The player with more cards with more points is the winner.

The rules are in Portuguese and English, but the cards are language independent.

Game Designers (1st Student Class - March 2012)

Antonio Marcelo
Carlos Monteiro
Fabio Corato
Fabricio Kolk
Jorge Barbosa
Rodrigo Rego
Thiago Escobar
Wilerson Lucas

Artists

Carlos Cade
Marcelo Bissoli

Note: The game will be published in a free PNP.

Scenario pack for Advanced Squad Leader. Tactical combat at the company level set during the Second World War, specifically the battles at Carpiquet airfield during Operation WINDSOR in July 1944. The scenarios were designed as part of a contest to see how diverse a collection of scenarios would be, given a common set of paramaters to work with. Fourteen entries by 12 authors were judged by well-known players and scenario designers in the ASL community at the gamesquad.com online community.

Scenarios include:

MWT1: Field of Fire        
MWT2: Fourth of July    
MWT3: Fifty Butchers    
MWT4: Stalwart Sons    
MWT5: Carpiquet Ballet     
MWT6: Rifles Standing Where They Fell
MWT7: The Streets of Carpiquet    
MWT8: The Hangers at Carpiquet    
MWT9: Le Lande Leads    
MWT10: Party Boys        
MWT11: A Choice Morsel
MWT11.b: A Choice Morsel
MWT12.1: Tenacious Defense        
MWT13: Prelude to Caen
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Information from the publisher's website.

How to play BOULOMANIA:
First of all, each team throws one dice to see which team starts. The one with the higher number goes first (Team A). Team A throws on dice to determinate the position of the jack (one of the spaces 1-6) and so places the jack. Then, throwing a choice of two or three dice, Team A determines the position of its first bowl - the total number of points indicating which space its first bowl should be placed in. If the total number of points thrown is less than seven, the bowl counts as dead and is removed from the game.

Now it is Team B's turn to place its first bowl. If Team B is better here, in other words, if its bowl is nearer to the jack - then it is Team A's turn to go again. If Team B doesn't succeed on the first go, then it throws again until on of its bowls is nearer to the jack than the opponent's best-placed bowl or until Team B has no more bowls left. Then it's Team A' turn again... and so on and so forth.

There are extra rules for things like trying to remove an opponent's well-placed bowl and other stuff that a Boules player might expect.

SYNOPSIS

"Since the Ascension, the universe is a hard-fought setting of the Technomagi. Besides their schemes and secret agendas, they fight a power struggle for the most valuable resource that is known to sentiment beings: Crystallium. This substance fuels all Magitech, an invention that grants godlike powers by fusing magic and technology. Mighty legions and armadas of spaceships became obsolete within the blink of an eye, as the Technomagi tapped into the possibilities of Magitech. In the far away future, battles are fought and decided by a selected few instead. Accompanied by their Minions, Technomagi strive for nothing less than ultimate rulership over the universe. And Crystallium is the key to victory..."

SUMMARY OF PLAY

In "The Magitech Wars" - players take the roles of mighty Technomagi - competing to claim  Crystallium Sources on the plane, through skirmish battles. Using customized Vaults of cards, a hex-tile gameboard, six sided dice and tokens, they let their minions fight  to become the emperor of a fictitious multiverse. From turn to turn the players summon Minions and move them around the board to let them battle. Players gain crystals by putting some of their Minions into the crystal zone, and win the game through gathering six victory points. Those points are scored by controlling the most Places of Power on the gameboard, or by banishing enemy cards.

OBJECT OF THE GAME

The first player who scores six Victory Points, wins the game. This is done in two different ways:


     Control Places of Power.
     Banish enemy cards.

boardgame: Advanced Squad Leader Starter Kit Scenario Design Contest
description:
The Advanced Squad Leader Starter Kits (ASLSK) depict World War II combat at the tactical (company-sized) level, using squad-sized units in an interactive phased turn sequence based on the original 1977 Squad Leader game. The game was intended as a gateway to the Advanced Squad Leader system created from the original Squad Leader in 1985.

Discussions at the Armchair General/Strategy Zone Online (SZO) forums in 2005 led some to surmise that the ASLSKs in many ways brought back the “feel” of the original Squad Leader, having just a handful of unit types, mapboards, and rules necessary in order to play. A controversial design aspect of that original Squad Leader game was the use of four mapboards, laid end-to-end, in the design of two of the twelve enclosed scenarios. From these discussions, a contest was announced on November 29, 2005, to see if it was possible to recreate the "feel" of those original scenarios using Starter Kit components.

Pete Shelling took the prize from among a talented field of contestants, including veteran gamer Murray McCloskey, Tom Morin, editor for Dispatches from the Bunker who has since seen his popular Valor of the Guards module released by MMP, Ola Nygards, whose Eastern Front scenarios have seen print in ASL Journal, Jean-Pascal Paoli, a regular contributor to French ASL magazine Le Franc Tireur, and veteran scenario designer Pete Shelling, who has had scenarios published in The General and ASL Journal in addition to various third party venues.

Scenarios include:

Scenario A: To the Soldiers of the Ostfront
Scenario B: Exhausting But Productive
Scenario C: Thank Heavens, We Lost
Scenario D: Crimson Snow
Scenario E: Glimmer of Hope
Scenario F: Air Assault on Drvar
Scenario G: Siege Without Shells
Scenario H: Aidez L'Espagne!
family: Series: Advanced Squad Leader Starter Kit

description:
Magtspillet (The Power Game) is a political incorrect game about Danish politic, which can be played by 2-4 players.

You strive to collect the largest team of ministers, to be able to take power in the "Tinget" (Danish Parliament), and become statsminister (Prime Minister). Betrayal, lawbreaking and slander are just three of powers the players can use.

Magtspillet uses a broad selection of politicians from the last 25 years from Folketinget.

All cards are dealt out, and players take turn to select any card from their collection, choose a category, and beat a random card from an opponents hand. Whoever ends up with all cards, or just the most cards after a predetermined time, wins the game.

The game can also be player like Top Trumps, where the categories are: Scandals, Years in power, Party memberships, Minister seats, and Books published.

Objective of the game is the construction of a far branched family dynasty. There is no board; every player builds up his own dynasty by skillfully connecting special pieces. The clan with the most widespread structure wins the game.

The 10 different pieces made of strong cardboard have recessions and connectiong cones similar to a jigsaw puzzle. They stand symbolically for love, night, luck, possession, life, success, youth, mind and power.
The player can can 'purchase' them by rolling a 10-sided die and include them into the existing dynasty.

At the beginning every player receives four figures: Two adult figures, a heart and a cloverleaf which stand for love and luck. A family is founded whith these and can be extended with dice-luck and also with tactical planning. In addition to the dice result you can receive points from chips.

Some examples of dice results:
With a thrown '4' you purchase a house, which stands for possession and security. Mind and enducation (symbolized by a star) are added into the dynasty by rolling an '8'. When a '2' is rolled, the moon signals night, end of luck and a stroke of fate; the player should get rid of it again as soon as possible. With a '7' a child (boy/girl) comes into the dynasty, which stands for future and development. A second child in the dynasty will form the foundation for further generations in the construction of the dynasty.

The numerological and color-psychological background on which the game is based are explained in the appendix of the rules of the game.

boardgame: Director's Cut Survival Horror: The Card Game
description:
The first set of cards is the initial deck, the first movie Zombie Hospital that we wrote for the Director’s Cut Survival Horror role-playing system. There are card sets for two more movies, and plans to release expansions of items and characters at set times during the year. The plan is to write more movies for the Director’s Cut Survival Horror role-playing system and convert them over to the collectible card game. The premise is that anyone can pick up a deck and start playing. The goal of this game is to simply survive the movie, with fewer injuries than your opponents.

Each game consists of a 50-card Scene Deck, required to play the game. The Scene Deck is the movie as a whole. Each player has a deck of 50 cards that they have compiled from the available cards and one archetype card. The archetype is the character they are playing in the movie. Each deck is filled with Items, Actions, and Skills to handle the scenes as well as Wounds and other cards that affect game play and the other players.

While the card game is ideal for two or even eight players, because players are playing against Scenes as well as other players, this game can be played in a solitaire fashion. The whole premise of the game is to recreate surviving a horror movie, without too many rules to learn.

Orkhorde is a card game based around the daily life of an orc horde. Planning their actions, the orcs can decide to favor their own tribe or to cooperate with larger raids. In the end, it's important how many trophies the tribes gather.

Each game turn, each tribe decides for its members how much work they will spend at the tasks, which is limited by the health of the orcs. Afterwards, these tasks are resolved: the shaman runs a ritual assisted by the other orcs to improve the warbands' chances, the male orcs go hunting alone and as the big warband, the female orcs educate the children and gather fruits and herbs, and in the end the orcs have a chance to improve their position in the pecking order by fighting their immediate superiors. Hunting, raiding and fighting for a better place in the pecking order can net an orc not only food – orcs that go hungry, starve and lose health and thus have fewer activity points to spend for tasks – but also trophies (trophy points) which determine which tribe is the most successful in the end. Part of the rule system has been taken from Wolfsspuren and is used with permission.

Due to the planning phase and inter-horde rivalries, there is lots of competition and backstabbing as well.

Players are postmen travelling round Greendale competing to deliver letters. At the start of the game players are given some letters to deliver. Each time one of these letters is delivered by arriving by exact count on a particular square you take a stamp. The stamps have varying values.

The games ends when one player has delivered all their letters. The winner is the player with the highest total value of stamps, not the player who finished first.
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

description:
Game play in Chasse aux Marsupilamis! resembles that of Pounce / Spitz paß auf! in that one player tries to capture one or more animals while everyone else yanks his animal off the table to keep it safe. The animal in question this game is Franquin's marsupilami – the spotted leopardy dog with the incredibly lengthy tail. Each player has his own marsupilami, with the tail being a piece of string.

On a turn, the active player rolls three dice – white, black and yellow – and depending on the roll of the white die, players consult the black or yellow die (or possibly still the white) to see what they have to do. In some cases the active player tries to slam a cage on the marsupilamis, taking a food token from the owner of each animal he captures; in other cases the goal is the same, but the active player must pay everyone he doesn't capture. Sometimes the active player can fake a capture, taking food from anyone who feverishly yanks his marsupilami out of range.

Players start the game with a number of food tokens, and the game ends as soon as one (or more) players has 15 tokens or 0 tokens. In either case, the player with the most tokens wins!

boardgame: Muckraker: Race for the White House
description:
Welcome to the biggest Media Circus in the world! You have chosen to throw your hat in the ring hoping to become the most powerful person in the world, the leader of the free world, the President of the United States. But to get there you must fight all the others who want the same prize. The fight is guaranteed to be dirty with all the Media Frenzy surrounding it.

In this lighthearted dig at American presidential politics players take on the role of candidates like Congressman Daddy’s Boy or Senator Green with attributes like Tree Hugger, Veteran, Fat Cat, Religious, etc. and compete to win the delegates from twenty-one groups of states or Regions like Hurricane Landfall (the Carolinas), Extra Shipping (Hawaii & Alaska), and Second Biggest (Texas).

Who doesn’t know what politics and elections are about? The candidates definitely do… and so they start with a war chest of funds ranging from $4 million to $10 million. Once used, the funds are gone till the next round of seven regions. So candidates must choose where to use how much carefully. They can also choose to stay out of the race in a region and instead use the funds to Dig for Dirt on the competition.

Each of the regions have their preferences for what kind of candidates they like, so if your attributes match that of a region, your funds go further, and vice versa. But once the campaign funds are spent the outcome is not guaranteed for the leader in spending. That’s when the Media Frenzy begins! Candidates can now play to the Media and either bring-out Support or Protest Rallies, they can use the dirt they collected and expose Scandals about the competition. Each media event can make your spending go further or not so much.

Candidates must be careful not to indulge the media too much, because if more than one person is spending at the same level, the fickle crowd tunes them out, and the highest unique spender win the delegates.

We hope you have the support of your base, you have the fortitude to let a few protests roll off your back, and you don’t have too many scandals in your past. If you do… you might just be the next Commander in Chief!

Star Crashers is a free, home made, tabletop, turn based game of space exploration and combat.  It is a "friendly" competitive game, in that players are competing for points, but don't attack each other directly.

Physical components include ships (either models or counters), a starbase, measuring sticks, and encounter blips.  Planets or other "scenery" add visual interest and block line of sight, but are not required to play.

Players start the game with equal number of points to purchase their fleet.  Turns proceed clockwise around the table.  Movement sticks are used to measure movement and weapon ranges.  Each ship has a unique ability, for example bombers get an extra die when attacking, scout ships may examine encounter blips, and freighters get bonuses to repair rolls.

Each ship in a fleet gets two actions per turn, which can include moving 1 stick, making a ranged attack, or collecting a commodity.  Most combat occurs between player ships and Pirate ships or other enemies that appear when an encounter blip is revealed.  Combat is a simple contested die roll, the number of dice based on the size and type of ship.  Player ships don't get destroyed, rather they become crippled and can repair, be repaired, or return to the starport to get repaired.

Unexplored space is represented by encounter blips, cards with different encounters placed face down and revealed when a ship comes in contact with them during its move.  Resolution of the encounter blip can earn that player Credits.  The game ends when all the blips have been resolved, and the player with the most credits wins!

On the frontside of each card has an IQ task, on the backside there is a solution. For each task, you get a certain number of IQ points credited, you will find in the IQ-log of each card.

Shuffle the cards and distribute to each group the same card number. The groups oppose each other alternately, for each task now. What group solve the task first gets the card.
The winner is the group that holds most of the cards at the end of the game.

Classical Music.
The game consists of an audio CD, including 99 musical excerpts, 20 cards and a "Track List".

Before starting the game, one player will be designated DJ. Its role will be to manage the CD and watching the responses on the "Track Liszt". The option of reading the CD will be on "Random" or "Schuffle" so that the extracts are chosen randomly by the machine.

When the game starts, the 20 cards are shuffled. 19 of them have one of three issues of the Blind Test:
- Who wrote the song?
- When the piece was composed?
- What is the song title?
The music is played and the first player to find the correct answer wins the card.

When all 19 cards have been drawn, each player add the value of his cards. The player who scored the most points is the winner.

The game consists of an audio CD, including 97 musical excerpts, 20 cards and a "Track List".

Before starting the game, one player will be designated DJ. Its role will be to manage the CD and watching the responses on the "Track Liszt". The option of reading the CD will be on "Random" or "Schuffle" so that the extracts are chosen randomly by the machine.

When the game starts, the 20 cards are shuffled. 19 of them have one of three issues of the Blind Test:
- Who wrote the song?
- When the piece was composed?
- What is the song title?
The music is played and the first player to find the correct answer wins the card.

When all 19 cards have been drawn, each player add the value of his cards. The player who scored the most points is the winner.

The game consists of an audio CD, including 99 musical excerpts, 20 cards and a "Track List".

Before starting the game, one player will be designated DJ. Its role will be to manage the CD and watching the responses on the "Track Liszt". The option of reading the CD will be on "Random" or "Schuffle" so that the extracts are chosen randomly by the machine.

When the game starts, the 20 cards are shuffled. 19 of them have one of three issues of the Blind Test:
- Who wrote the song?
- When the piece was composed?
- What is the song title?
The music is played and the first player to find the correct answer wins the card.

When all 19 cards have been drawn, each player add the value of his cards. The player who scored the most points is the winner.

"Forbidden" in English is a pirate game.
World map on the game board is drawn by Lucien Boucher (famous illustrator known for his maps and posters in the year 1930/50)

Originally intended to be played in two, The game proposes special rules that allow up to 6 players to participate.
Game board offers land and sea routes how the painted figurines would move.
Each player can win in two ways: Bringing his six crews in the same place or capture two opposing crews by blocking them.

When the game starts, each player places one by one the six units of the other team(s). Some intersections of roads are better than others. In turn, a player may move one of its units from one intersection to another.

A card game for 2 to 6 players ages 4 and up.  The deck consists of 96 cards, 10 each numbered 1 to 8 and showing a picture of Hermie or one of his friends, and 16 wild cards.  Each player starts with a small stack of cards (their 'stock pile') and a hand of 4, played face up. Players take turns playing their cards onto 4 stacks in the center, each central stack starting with a #1 or a wild card, and counting up. First player to get rid of his or her stock pile wins.
Hermie and his friends are from a line of christian children's book by Max Lucado.

Like Connect Four but the game deck rotate on its axis, causing also the game pieces to be falling.

In any time player who gets 4-in-a-row he wins the game.

boardgame: Go Diego Go: Air Soccer Kick Off
description:
Join Diego and his rainforest friends for a friendly game of soccer.

Head-to-head action for two players. Players try to hit the soccer disc into the opposing goal over a bed of air generated by the board. Scores are kept track by slide scorekeeper.

Electronic board has a air flow system that creates a cushion of air that floats the disc. Comes with 2 pucks, and 2 player pieces.

This game is just like air hockey except it has a soccer theme.

The card game Buck Jitters comes a deer hunting theme, yet is quite abstract in nature. Each card has two numeric values and they are compared with the opponent's card played, with the higher number winning and the lower number adding to the victor's point total. The game includes wild cards and trump cards to adjust the card values. There are many scenarios included with partnership play available as well. It is played in rounds and there is bidding involved, providing a Bridge like format.

Each player represents a political party and has 2 characters.
The six political tendencies are represented.
Using the 110 game cards, players must score popularity points possible for them or their allies.

There are multiple cards that allow attack their opponents and send them scandals, insider trading etc. If You will have the cards like lawyers, or bankers You can get out of chaos easier!

Once all the cards are played, each player adds up its popularity and the two winners in the second round goes to elections!
Add the two points of their allies and whoever is the most popular is declared the president.

This is a version of Monopoly, based entirely in the Swedish translation (using Stockholm streets) of the game that was patented in 1935 by Charles Darrow and released by Parker Brothers. 
The gameplay is similar to Monopoly.

In Tjörn-Monopol the streets are instead places/harbours along the cost of the island of Tjörn, one of Sewdens largest islands and a summer holiday paradise. Instead of house upgrades the owner of a harbour upgrade the pier of the place to more and more luxurous versions. Instead of money the economy is handled using cases of fish of differen kinds. The board is designed in a marine manor and the cards texts are rewritten ti fit the theme, as pieces used for movement.

Mummel is a game where the players take turns in the role of the reader. Based on a spin on a wheel the reader do one of six actions.

Read a new card asking "Who said: [quote]?"
Read a new card asking "What did [Celebrity] say about [fact]?"
Read a new card asking "Finish the quote: [uncomplete quote]!"
Steal a collected card from one player
Give a collected card to anoter player
Force another player to exchange one card with you.

The Read actions has about 25% each to occur.
The actions using allready collected card share the remaining 25% chance.

If a new card is read the players write their answers in secret.
The reader collects the answers ans checks if any of them are correct. The player closest in turn to the reader who has the right answer gets the card that was read. If no one has the right answer (usually the case :)  the reader reads all the answers and the correct answer and the player closest in turn that guess which one is correct gets the card. If no one guess correct the reader gets the card.

The back of the cards (seen by the players when the reader reads) are marked with four different synbols. The one who is first at collecting at least one of all four symbols wins.

Each player has a circular playing piece with six pie-shaped holes. The goal of the game is to collect a pie in each color. The colors correspond to different questions in the following categories:

Blue: Places of Europe
Pink: Live in Europe
Yellow: Institutions of Europe
Brown: Culture of Europe
Green: History of Europe
Orange: Personalities of Europe

The board consists of a circular track with spaces in seven different colors. Six of the colors correspond to question categories while the last color gives a new dice roll. Six spaces along the track are "pie spaces", and from these there are "spokes" of track leading to the middle of the board.

Players roll a die and move along the track in any direction they like. When a player stops on a color they get a question of the appropriate category. If the player answers a question correctly while on a pie space, they get a pie of that color (assuming they don't already have it). A correct answer on another square allows the player to roll again.

Once the player has one pie in each color, she can move along the spokes to the middle of the board to win the game.

In this game the players take the roles as antique dealers, trading antiquities on the auction market. 
There are 42 antiquities in the game, (fairly realistic in their representation with photos and descriptions). The 42 antiquities are sorted in 11 series of 3-5 antiquities each.
The players are to collect them and the one who has 2 (short game) or 3 (longer game) complete series with no outstanding money loans is the winner.

The players start with some cash and 5 antiquities each. All antiquities ar kept secret until a complete series is collected.
as the game progress they move around a board where they land on squares that causes auction to occur; new antiques enter the market, opportunity to loan more money, more money (salary) or random event cards.

The central mechanic is the auctions and the players auction antiquities as well as the bank. Asking price and acceptance price is set by the player. What is to be auctioned, asking and acceptance price is kept secret until the auction starts. Players that win objects include them in their hands and keeps them hidden until a series is completed, (but as the game moves along you can get more and mor knowledge about what the other players have, based on what auctions they won, if you remember this)

Objective:
be the first stepping on field 70
 - moving by turn -
roll the die - define starting player with a '6' 
ending on a marked space follow instructions glued on cover inside

remarks:
belongs to series 'Scholz' künstlerische Spiele'
schould be published ~ 1930's
equipped with cute plane meeples

The goal of the game is to build the best buildings in his village like church, school, town hall, fire fighters, homes and businesses.

At the beginning of the game, each player chooses his village and receives a color pawn.
In turn the player draws a card and cards allow you to either acquire a building or to move his pawn. Different buildings scores differently.
The first to reach 100 points wins. Otherwise the game ends when all the buildings are placed and the player who has the most points wins.

A new hero joins the fight, bringing with him new challenges to face and treasures to win!

Quick Quest: Hero Pack 1 adds a Paladin to your roster of heroes.

Fight Fanatics, Seekers, and the dreaded Oathbreakers on your way to face the Cult Leader!

Swap out one of the four heroes using the color coded dots and shuffle the new cards in or use five heroes and continue the adventure to dungeon level 12!

This is an expansion for Quick Quest. It is not a stand alone product.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A new hero joins the fight, bringing with him new challeges to face and treasures to win!

Quick Quest: Hero Pack 2 adds a Ranger to your roster of heroes.

You have creepy Crawlers and the spider-bat Web Wings to fight. Luckily the Ranger's brought his Spider Bane potion to help!

Swap out one of the four heroes using the color coded dots and shuffle the new cards in or use five heroes and continue the adventure to dungeon level 12!

This is an expansion for Quick Quest. It is not a stand alone product.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Logical deduction game. Each player starts with a grid of 7 x 7 squares. Both boards must be hidden from the opponent. One player (the Target) marks his starting point in the centre square of the grid. The other player (the Shadow) starts at any one of the four corner squares.

The objective for the Target is to visit two of the four corner squares without being captured. The objective of the Shadow is to capture the Target by landing on the corresponding square.

The Target moves orthagonally and must keep a record of his moves by tracing a line from his start point through all the squares he travels over during the game. The Shadow moves like a Knight in Chess. Information is given each turn to enable both players to get some idea where the other is.

A pencil-and-paper game for up to 4 players. You need a 6 x 6 board to be placed in the centre of play.  The board should be noted as A to F horizentally and as 1 to 6 vertically.  It is then left in the centre as a quick reference for each player to know where each of their pieces are standing. Each player will have 3 Warriors and 1 King.

Each player takes a player sheet on which they allocate 10 points in total secretly to their three Warriors. Then the four pieces (including the King) are placed on the centre back-line nearest to each player.

Decide a random starter. A player can move and/or have a 'battle'. Warriors move 1 square in any direction. Kings move up to 2 squares orthagonally. Moving a piece is done by crossing out the old grid reference where it was and writing in the new grid reference being the square that that piece has just moved to. No two pieces of the same player can be on the same space and a piece cannot jump over a piece of the same colour.

Battle is done by announcing the square a piece is currently on (so before a move). The Battle takes place if one or more opposing pieces are on that square. The attacker announces the strength of his piece and the defenders announce their strength. The strongest piece wins and there are tie-breakers should their be a tie. After a Battle, the victor receives one point for each of his pieces so he gets the reward of stronger pieces for the rest of the game.

If a King is challenged, it immediately loses and that player is out of the game. The winner is the player with the last surviving king.

In the mid-2000's, something is opening Holes, corridors into other dimensions, which are destroying the Earth's ecological balance. These passages are filled with hostile aliens and mechanical life-forms.

Your objective as a Trained Holebusting Operative is to get to the core and destroy it.

Holes has a wicked sense of humor, with such hostile and funky aliens as Brats, Kagoody, Klowns, Zonies, and Rats. It's a great beer and peanuts game, and lavishly illustrated.

This game is a cross between an RPG and a miniatures game. Players command a squad of 12 humans as they battle their way into a  randomly generated maze of corridors. The game uses a d100 for all combat resolution and lets players choose a few modifiers if they want to expand the No-Frills Combat System.
publishers: Tri Tac Games LLC (Tri Tac Talents)

description:
Object of the game
Superpion is a kind of checkers that can play up to four players. Besides the number of players, with the notable difference is that the ladies are orthogonal movement while catches are diagonal. The goal is to capture the most of his opponents pieces without getting caught hers: at the end of the game, each captured pawn is worth 1 point, 2 points each pin retained. Each player can also try one or more Superpions reaching the bottom line of the opposing camps. The Superpions allow increased catches by scanning the plate with a larger amplitude (equivalent of a Lady).

The game ends when there are only pawns in a single player on the board, or when taken have become impossible.
Each player add up points: 1 point per captured pieces, 2 points per his own pieces on the board.
The player who has scored the most points is the winner!

A roll-and-move game where players answer safety related questions from different categories to earn tokens.  The tokens  can then be cashed in to earn pieces of a brain shaped puzzle. The game comes with a stack of "You're a Safety Brainiac" certificates for the winners.

This game is played with lego pieces.

One player is the Architect. He creates a Rule to which a lego-building must comply. The other players take turns to create small lego-buildings from a few pieces. The Architect reviews these buildings; ‘good’ buildings are free, ‘faulty’ buildings cost money. Players earn Victory Points for guessing the Rule. A round continues until either the Rule is guessed, or until all players are out of money. Then the next player is Architect. After all players haven been Architect once, the player with the most Victory Points wins. Rules below in dutch and english.

The purpose of this game is to test and increase players' knowledge of Africa.  Players travel through different regions of Africa and have to answer a number of questions about the continent along the way.  The progress they make will be partly determined by how well they answer these questions.  Players have to pay a fine when they do not answer questions correctly, but even if they do answer questions correctly, they still have to face a number of obstacles that may slow them down.  Players have to quit the game if they lose all of their money.  The first player to reach "The Heart of Africa" wins the game.

Fest im Sattel is a simple horse-themed roll-and-move game for children. It comes in a tin box whose lid is used as a board. The board shows a racecourse (shaped like an eight placed inside/onto an oval, with an extra circle inside one of the halves of the eight). Each player puts his/her horse token on the start space. Players take turns rolling the six-sided number die. If you roll a 1, 2 or three, you move your horse forward the corresponding number of spaces. Occupied spaces don't count, you just jump over them. If you roll a -1, you have to go back one space. Horses directly behind have to go back too. If you land on one of the special fields, you have to take a detour or lose a turn.

The player whose horse is the first to reach the finish line wins the game.
family: Components: Game Box Used In Play

description:
Elaborate game of chance.

Before play, the game has to be assembled. When finished there will be a three-piece slide. At the top of the slide you place a ball. The ball will now trundle downwards, going from one part of the slide to another until it comes out at the end and on to the board. With luck, it will settle into one of the holes and the player scores points for where it has ended up. The winner would be the first to reach a pre-agreed total.

This doesn't seem to have any skill at all. You cannot influence the speed of the ball so it is sheer luck where it ends up. However, I can see it having novelty value, especially for children. And it is rather beautiful to look at.

CQS or Close Quarter Slaughter is a fast-paced brutal skirmish wargame about gray and black ops in cyberpunk Japan. 
During various scenarios players will be in command of a squad of ex-military rebellions or multinational special forces. Also there is a third side controlled by the game and used for dynamic showdowns in each scenario.
Players will simultaneously give order to their unites and than resolve them with special mechanic Action Drop. Players will not be limited by turns but the more turns they spend the harder it will be to accomplish their objectives and survive in a firefight.

CQS is attempt to create small wargame were a player know about opponent's forces as few as possible during a course of the game.

At each turn, a card "waste" is returned to the center of the table.

If it is a sort recyclable waste and you have the corresponding large bin, seized the card as soon as possible and throw "the waste" inside.

Be careful not to get overtaken by one of your opponents, since one of them has the mini-trash can and then you fight!

The player with the most sorted and discarded waste at the end of the game is the winner.

You have a pack of word cards, and a spinner. The spinner has two windows, one shows a number, take that number of cards, the other shows a sad or happy face.

You have one minute to use all your word cards to make up a happy or a sad story. If you do this you score 1 point. When all the cards have been used, count up who has most points.

Objective: be the first getting most winning points after agreed time
 - moving/acting by turn -
throw the die, move & draw a card due to space ending on

very similar to Bärlin: Das Stadtspiel
content:
230 'sag-mal' cards (trivia)
90 'mach-mal' cards (action)
30 'moment-mal' cards (tactical decisions)
1 board
1 die
1 rule
8 meeples

Role the dice to travel round the board collecting four different segments of Dreamstone Tokens. When you have all four make your way to the to the finish square. Along the way you may land on squares that will hinder your progress. The first person to reach the finish square with four different Dreamstone Tokens wins the game.

The gang bosses have met to divide up the town. Tempers turn and the talk turns nasty... It's a gang war!

The gangs have their instructions on a secret card and so they set off round the streets to take over two of the four city rackets.

Unoccupied property is taken over without a fight, but if a rival gangster has already taken it over and stayed in defense, it means risking a 'hit'.

Who knows the outcome of a duel with this hilarious armory? When a gang member initiates a hit on another player, they each draw a hit card add its number to your dice roll (one dice for each gang member and the Boss gets two dice), add up your score. The losing gang member(s) are 'hit' and no longer participate in the game.

Gangs collect tokens from the food, drink, gambling and money rackets in their headquarters to show the other gangs who is the boss.

The gang that survives to run the two sets of rackets on their objective cards is the winner.

Quick-play introduction to the classic game Monopoly. Be the player with the most money at the end of the game!

It’s all about raking in the rent by putting your tokens on as many properties as you can. But beware, any property you own can be snatched by the next player who lands there. On your turn roll, move your token, and then pay rent, buy, build or auction. When you land on any colored property space, you can build on it – even if someone else owns it. The building tokens have a number on them and they can only be played if they are the next highest token. Look out for the special die that helps build or demolish properties. Properties and utilities cannot be mortgaged in Monopoly Express. If you run out of money, the game is over. The player with the most money wins

Gameboard, 32 colored property tokens (8 per player), 8 Chance cards, 8 Community Chest cards, 2 Utility cards, 4 movers, a money pack, and a die.

This is a trivia game that comes with a book series that deals with facts. With every book you get a pack of "question cards" that expands the deck.

There is also a deck of "animal cards". To solve these cards you have to mime the animal that is pictured.

Unlike most trivia games you move along the path by "moving cards". You get two "moving cards" if you solve one animal or question card. You have to figure witch "moving cards" you have to use to get you faster to the end.

The aim of the game is to race along the ground avoiding all the hazards such as, the Earthquake, the Dinosaur fight, the Marsh etc before you can be the first to cap the erupting volcano.

Each player has its own playing grid to move along, which is three squares wide, you role the dice and move the appropriate number of squares, you can only move straight ahead or diagonally, so keep an eye on what instruction squares you want to miss and which ones you want to land on. Knocking down a tree allows you to move extra spaces (2 squares for a small tree, and 4 for a large tree). Instruction squares may move you forward, may move you back, may allow you to place a tree on your playing grid, or remove a tree from an opponent's playing grid. If you land on a fight square you must fight the player to your left, highest dice role wins. The winner moves forward three, the loser goes back three. The first to cap the volcano wins.

boardgame: Robin Hood & His Merry Men: Sherwood Races
description:
A game for two, three or four players.

On their turn each player rolls the dice and moves their piece that number forward. If you land on a multiple of ten square (10,20,30 etc) your piece is returned to its former position and the player forfeits their turn. This also applies if a piece lands on a log or puddle.

The first player to pass the finishing line wins the race.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Robin Hood

description:
Each player draws an 11 x 10 grid. The columns are numbered and the rows lettered. In each of the ten columns the players distribute four 0's, three 1 's, two 2's, one 4, and an A for appeal in any order. The players then take it in turn to bowl an over. It is advisable for each player to record the outcome of his over on a spare grid.

Object
The player who scores the most runs before all ten of his appeals are located is the winner.

Play
The bowler picks a line of six contiguous squares as the over. They are announced by simply giving the starting square and the direction — e.g. E5/SE. The batsman plots this line on his grid and announces the total number of runs scored (i.e. the sum of the numbers passed through) and the number of appeals.
If a bowler thinks he knows the position of all ten appeals, he cries 'Howzat!' and reads out the positions. If any are wrongly placed the batsman draws up a fresh grid and continues anew. If the bowler is right, the batting side is all out. Obviously, if one side is still batting with more runs scored when the other side is all out, they need not continue. The bowler can then guess at the positions of the ten appeals, to discover the number of wickets down, and so determine the winning margin.

With a little ingenuity, limited-over and two-innings matches can be devised, together with individual batting and bowling figures, etc.

Scratch is a two-player strategy card game where each player constructs a deck of iconic fantasy monsters. Players take turns laying a single card in a 3x3 grid with each successive card sharing at least one side with a card already in play. When cards are first played, numbers on shared sides are compared to determine if the new card defeats the old card, allowing control of cards to be gained and lost throughout play. Victory occurs when one player controls three cards in a row (horizontal or vertical). Scratch has a minimum of rules but deep strategic game play.

Enter the Earth Realm and immerse yourself in the world of karma! Collect good karma as fast as you can while avoiding bad. Acquire the tools you'll need to develop your skills: torches (intuition), swords (reason), and wands (action). Meditate in the Temple of the Torch and break habits in the Temple of the Sword. Maneuver around the Pit and don't relapse when encountering fear or ego! Strategize. Collect and use Free Will Cards to expand your options.

At the end of a physical lifetime, transition to the Spirit Realm where you reflect the past and plan the future. Re-enter the Earth Realm when and where it's most advantageous. The first player to eliminate all negative karma, collect all master tools, and return to Cosmic Consciousness wins the game.

Playable on many levels:
-As a game of chance and strategy--entertaining and absorbing
-As a metaphysical tool--provokes reflection on a variety of cosmologies
-As a self-help tool--stimulates personal insight
-As a spiritual tool-reveals the role of karma in the soul's journey
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
TOP SPEED MINI is a fast and furious racing game for 2 to 8 players (recommended 2 to 5). It’s the simplified version of Top Speed.

TOP SPEED MINI uses one single Top Speed deck shared by all players (instead of one deck per player), one small car for each player and a small board (1 pad instead of 3). The games are shorter than in Top Speed and are based on Kart racing.

All the components come in a single set as opposed to Top Speed, where the components are purchased separately (one team per player).

The rules are quite different from those in the Top Speed rulebook. For example:

- There is no Safety Car, Pit Stop or Drive Through penalty.
- The number of laps for the race is smaller (usually 3-4 instead of 60).
- You can fix your car by just passing a whole round, but you can only discard 1 damage card each time.
- The maximum recommended number of players is 5 (but it accomodates more).

You can play TOP SPEED MINI with a Top Speed set, but not the opposite.

Components:

- A small TOP SPEED board (one pad) depicting the Montmeló circuit (SPAIN).
- A TOP SPEED deck of cards.
- 8 cars in different colors (white, black, red, yellow, green, orange, blue, purple). 
- 8 color markers.
- A carrying case.

The pilots sit in their cockpits, the engines are started and the tension immeasurably rise.  Anakin Skywalker is in the podrace of his life! Help Anakin to win this dangerous race.
 You use your Jedi Reflexes to copy as fast as possible the current order of the Podracer from the covered panels. Whoever accomplishes this first ends the round and receives 6 points. Other players also gain points depending on how many they could get correct.

Who collects most points in 5 racing rounds, wins!


From german rules:

WORUM GEHT’S?
Die Piloten sitzen in ihren Cockpits, die Triebwerke sind gestartet und die Spannung steigt ins Unermessliche.
Gleich beginnt für Anakin Skywalker das Podrace seines Lebens!
Helft Anakin, dieses gefährliche Rennen zu gewinnen.
Stellt eure Jedi-Reflexe unter Beweis und versucht, möglichst schnell die aktuelle Reihenfolge der Podracer aus den verdeckten Plättchen nachzubilden. 
Wer das als Erster schafft, beendet für alle die Runde und erhält 6 Punkte. 
Die anderen gehen aber auch nicht leer aus je nachdem, wie viele Plättchen sie bis zu diesem Zeitpunkt in der richtigen Reihenfolge stapeln konnten, gibt es auch für sie Punkte. 
Wer in 5 spannenden Runden die meisten Punkte sammelt, gewinnt!

boardgame: Star Wars: Kampf gegen Darth Maul
description:
Obi Wan Kenobi and Qui Gon Jinn are in the royal palace on Naboo when the malicious Sith lord Darth Maul attacked. Your task is to support the two Jedi knights in the fight so that Darth Maul has  received at least 8 points of damage after 10 rounds, because only then is he defeated!  Who contributed most points of damage wins.


From German Rules:

WORUM GEHT’S?

Obi-Wan Kenobi und Qui-Gon Jinn werden im königlichen Palast auf Naboo von dem bösartigen Sith-Lord Darth Maul
angegriffen. 
Eure Aufgabe ist es, die beiden Jedi-Ritter im Kampf zu unterstützen, sodass Darth Maul nach 10 Runden mindestens 8 Schadenspunkte erhalten hat.
Denn nur dann habt ihr ihn besiegt! Wer von euch am Ende die meisten Schadenspunkte dazu beigetragen hat, gewinnt.

Each box contains 15 to 17 cards for use with the Lost Worlds combat books.

The cards depict weapons, spells, items, tactics and luck. Some are useable over an entire campaign of battles.

Before a battle, characters can buy some of these cards depending on their skill values. The cards offer special abilites for the characters that can be used during or between battles, depending on the card.
family: Series: Picture Book Games (Nova Game Designs)

description:
The Atlantean Wars depicts the ancient and mythical war between three all powerful civilizations: Atlantis, set in the middle of the Atlantic Ocean, the empire of Mu, set in the middle of the Pacific Ocean, and the Matriarchy of Lemuria, set around the island of Madagascar. The board depicts the entire world, divided in land and maritime zones.

After a long time of peace, and with the oncoming apocalypse, war pits once again those 3 mighty titans, war for the domination of the world, war for the control of the rich zones of Orichalc, this fabulous alloy allowing them to build wondrous machines of war, war for the gaining the alliance of the numerous small nations around the world. The Empires will be able, to win this war once and for all, to expand their territory with their troops, to build new colonies which will grow in power, to build strange and beautiful wonders allowing for teleport movements, to further their knowledge of technology, all the while battling their neighbors and exchanging with them deadly "fire of God" (read nuclear)attacks.

Players proceed through 4 phases, Initiative, Tech, Build, and Activation, leading to combat which is resolved with a die roll on a differential of strength chart, adding a number of "artefact events" according to the tech level of the engaged units. During the end of the Acitvation phase, the players check to see if a sudden death victory occurs, when a player dominates a majority of the orichalc zones, or of the wonders.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: You've Been Sentenced! Add-On Deck: NASA Space Terminology
description:
Publisher Blurb:

Additional cards for You've Been Sentenced.  Turn outer space into inner classroom fun with the officially licensed NASA deck. Packed full of out of this world words that will amuse and inform students learning their planets and points beyond.

boardgame: A.W.A.C.S.: Arm Weapons And Climb Solo – Gulf War
description:
AWACS: Gulf War is loosely based on the events of the First Gulf War, but isn’t a typical, realistic and historically accurate ruleset. Instead, you can expect quick, thrilling and fun B-movie style action!

Within this box you will find nearly everything needed to start playing: 62 unpainted, fine metal miniatures in 1/600 scale, 62 bases (stems for airplanes are not provided), a set of all required dice, counters, an ‘army list’ sheet and a brochure containing rules and six scenarios.

The players take various roles, from leading Allied fighter sweeps, Iraqi counterattacks or stealth bombings of SAM batteries, to destroying SCUDs hidden at the end of a long, well defended valley! The scenarios may be played on 3 difficulty levels, and the players may easily create their own missions. Additionally, they may share them with others on the official Assault Publishing forum!

(from the publisher's website)

This game abstractly represents the struggle of an activist group to spread its message and convert the world.  It is a single player game played on a 6 by 6 grid, involving the use of two dice and also a number of checker pieces and one piece representing a core group.  Players struggle with trying to recruit, and losing support due to attrition and dissension.

Plays similar like Perudo but uses poker dice instead.

In turn, each player plays his dice and makes a bid which must be superior to previous one, and this for the next player. This one can then accept or refuse the bid. In case of refusal, he wins if the game is lower than the bid.

Very unusual item. It plays like a regular picture Bingo game but with the difference that the children must play it whilst they are watching television. If an image appears on screen, if you call out that it is on your Bingo card, then you can mark it as seen. First to get all the images on their card, wins.

From the description:

"Telektonon is the sword of light, the technology of telepathy, revealed as the day-by-day unfolding of the Thirteen Moon 28 Day Calendar. In the form of a game, Telektonon consists of a playing board with thirteen moving pieces and an accompanying set of 52 playing cards."

"The playing board and deck of 52 cards are the tools used on a daily basis for achieving the goal of putting you back in time with telepathy."

Includes the instructions, a "Sarcophagus Box" to hold the cards, a text describing the "Talking Stone of Prophecy", 4 time calibrator stones, three turtle tokens, 5 oracle pyramids, 1 crystal, a poster, a colorful board, and a cloth storage bag.

A player will move around the board and assemble a card layout; this pattern is cross-referenced with special charts to determine fourth dimension power.

Although the game can be enjoyed as a group activity, each player conducts his or her own metaphysical journey. The rules appear to reward the player who completes a session with greater cosmic power with a higher "score" which would then be compared to the "scores" of the other players during their own effort to obtain enlightenment. A complex set of charts is used to determine a players fourth dimension power.

British version of an American Football related game.

The game is in two stages. First you have to get round a track (like Game of Goose games) until you get to the centre of the board. On the track you will get various things happen to you, it is just a game of chance.

Once you reach the centre, it becomes an American Football trivia game.

The winner is the first to score a touchdown by asking questions correctly and advancing to the end-zone.

Players can buy racehorses sold at auctions and also bet horses if the race begins.

Money is won by good bets or by owning the winning horses.

For racing each horse moving is determined by rolling two dice.

Players with the most money at the end of the game is the winner.

Object of the game is to earn maximum points by getting rid of his letters. To do this, form new words from words already installed.

Each player draws a number of letters: 12 letters each with 2 players, nine letters of 2 to 4 players.
Based on number of players, each laying one or two 3-letter words on the board. These are the starting points they placed horizontally: 2 bases each with 2 players and one each in base 3 and 4 players.

In his turn player must add one of his letters to one of the words posed by changing it to form a new word and the player gets the points corresponding to the number of letters in the word and receives these points in chips.
- If the player can't reconstruct any word, it can exchange a letter from his game with a letter from the deck. In this case, it passes.
The game ends when a player places his last letter.
The player with the most points in chips is the winner.

Some more game-variants included.

boardgame: Toners: Let Us Sing Into The Mouths Of One Another
description:
Using a paper towel tube, two players position the tube so it covers each of their mouths.  
On the third player's signal, the two players adjoined by a tube intone a tone.  Their goal is to stop and start the tone as many times as possible before the get out of sync.  If one of them stops the tone for longer than a second before the other stops, then their turn is over.  
The non-intoning player is responsible for counting the number of stops and for judging when the turn should end.  Players are awarded one point for each successful start and stop.  Play rotates so that the non-intoning player becomes one of the two intoning players in a new round of play.  
The player with the most points after a predetermined number of rounds wins.

Object of the game is to answer the questions and recognize the objects with Your senses: with Your hand, Your mouth, Your nose, Your ears and Your eyes to score points.
Gym in brain, you need to answer all your senses. You will need to reconstruct such a figure (Tangram-style) with a blindfold, to recognize a tree among several wafers fragrant, count the colors in a literary text, or find a taste on the tip of language.

Players who is the best to recognize and score most points is the winner,

Objective: score winning points by achieving most accordance;
learn more about your teammates 
 - acting by turn -
draw a card and ask questions about life-style, fashion, love, partnership... (according to the coloured die roll) out of the world of 'Daniela Katzenberger' 
the better you can judge the opinion of your teammates the more points you are going to score ==> judging questions like:
Who trusts somebody to do something?
Who is willing to do delicate things?

A module for Lambourne's "World of Motor Racing".

This statistical replay game recreates the Indy 500 race. It uses the same basic rules as the base game, but adds specific rules unique to the Indy 500 race, such as different starting grids, Yellow Lights Phases, start procedure and pit stops.

As in the basic game, everything you do is handled by a roll of two dice and different tables for each driver, car and track section. The outcome of this roll and a number of modifiers tell you how many spaces you are allowed to move.
family: Series: World of Motor Racing (Lambourne / Owzat)

boardgame: World of Motor Racing: The British Touring Car Championship
description:
A module for Lambourne's "World of Motor Racing".

This statistical replay game recreates the British Touring Car Championship. It uses the same basic rules as the base game, but adds specific rules unique to touring car races.

As in the basic game, everything you do is handled by a roll of two dice and different tables for each driver, car and track section. The outcome of this roll and a number of modifiers tell you how many spaces you are allowed to move.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Series: World of Motor Racing (Lambourne / Owzat)

description:
A competitive or cooperative, dice rolling, Zombie survival experience.
In the game, each player rolls dice to acquire different resources. These resources are used to build and maintain a group of survivors who are working to eliminate the Zombie horde while trying to survive. Each roll of the dice presents difficult choices as players must decide which of these valuable resources they will keep, spend, or chance in order to best equip their survivors!

As the game begins players take turns rolling dice. Players must work to feed their survivors, buy items such as weapons, or special skills that allow players to manipulate their dice rolls to best benefit their survivors. Players will work their way through Town, battling Zombies and gaining Victory Points for each Zombie eliminated and each survivor that remains alive. But players must beware of opponents who can draw Zombies to their location as they try to move in and steal Victory Points and other valuable rewards that are specific to each location in Town. The pace is frantic, the Zombies are hungry and survivors can easily turn on one another!

Can you survive?

A little game of chance about the adventures of Odysseus on his return to Greece.

In his turn player roll the dice, moves his counter and if it stands on one of the events, inspired by the adventures of Odysseus during his Odyssey, must act accordingly.

The first arrived at the destination wins.

Creekos: The Card Game is a trick-taking card game with a customized deck that pits two partnerships of two players against one another, first in an auction to determine the power of their cards, then in the card play itself.

At the start of the game two auctions take place, one for the number scale – that is, which value on the cards they hold will be used by the partnership – and a second for the trump color. After this, the players then compete in a trick-taking game that uses customized cards in different categories that relate to one another; in the packet "Hercules' Labors", for example, the categories are Events, Gods, People, Creatures, Lifeless. For each trick, players lay one card in the center of the table – with some cards having special abilities that activate thunderbolts or block other cards – and the most powerful card wins the trick. Once all the tricks are finished, players tally their points (subtracting however many points they bid at the start of the game), and the partnership with the high score wins.

Creekos: The Card Game can also be played by only two players, with each player competing on her own.

Alteration is a post apocalyptic card game set in a science fantasy futuristic world that has been destroyed by the cataclysm known as The Great War. In Alteration players take on the roles of mutants, plants, animals, and even pure strain humans in a struggle to claim powerful technology left from before the great war! players take turns acquiring new mutations and researching technology to defeat the opponents.

In Alteration each player receives a random character card that they will use to get their stats, hit points, and special abilities. Each turn players will chose different actions to get more mutation cards, try to research technology, Attack an opponent, or use a power on one of there cards. many of these actions use dice to determine if the attack or power hits or if you can repair the tech you are trying to use and gain its card for use next turn. play continues until only one player has hit points left. The last mutant standing is the winner!

Do you have what it takes to traverse the irradiated wastes of Alteration?
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Object of the game is to be ​​the first to go home with the full pie, the party favors, sandwiches, drinks and masks.

Setup: The players put up their houses on the corner of the game and choos the characters.
In his turn player spins the wheel. Either the arrow indicates a number to advance his charakter to field with something he doesn't have (party favors, sandwich, drink, masks) and he takes it.
If the arrow indicates "pie" in which case the player chooses a pie face down and shows it to everyone. if the share belongs to his pie he keeps it.

When a player has all the elements of pie he returns to home. First player to reach his house is the winner

boardgame: A More Perfect Union: The Struggle to Ratify the Constitution
description:
This 2-player game simulates the great public debate over the ratification of the Constitution that took place across the United States from 1787-1789.  The game employs the popular card-driven mechanics of such well-known games as Twilight Struggle and 1960: The Making of the President. Representing the Federalists and Anti-Federalists, the players pursue strategies to ratify or defeat the Constitution.  The Federalists win if nine states ratifiy the Constitution.  The Anti-Federalists win if five states refuse to ratify.

The game places a premium on hand management and strategic planning by providing players with the tools to build powerful hands of cards over consecutive turns.  After players become familiar with the deck of cards, playing time should be around 2 hours.

2 Player game with fantasy theme that combines chess with worker placement.  Minions of 8 types are used as both chess pieces, some with unique moves, and also as workers. Each round players take turns placing their minions into the 16 available Tower Chambers. Each chamber holds a distinct Mage that grants a special power, such as adding a minion to the board or moving a specific type of minion on the board. Players then take turns removing their minions from the tower to activate these powers. 12 chaos mages are available to mix things up. Win by capturing your opponents greater minions or by wiping him off the board.

Diamino Competition is a crossword game for up to 2-8 players, according to the rules of the game chosen.

The game includes 112 square, 110 carry a letter with a number indicating its point value. The last 2 squares representing a devil and can replace any letter. These devils have no point value.
The black boxes and the devils of different colors on the game board, create barriers or benefits.

1. The first player forms a word of eight letters long either horizontally or vertically with the first letter placed on the green box.
The player on the left continue and raises the number of squares they want to form, with the squares already in play, a new word. 
2. The game should at all times, consist of complete words separated from each other. One or more voids must be present between two consecutive words
3. Exchanges. Each player may at any time redeem one or more squares of his hand, against an equal number of squares of the reserve. This exchange account for a ride. The square to the reserve (in reverse) are mixed again.
4. Benefits and barriers.
Black boxes found on the shelf boxes are prohibited. During the game, it is impossible to place a square. 
Boxes with Diable Vert are in principle also prohibited. all-time, a player who needs to use them to form a word, may place a tile. In this case, as a penalty, he must take an extra tile to the reserve.

The winner is the player who succeeds in placing all his tiles first. The number of points acquired by him is the total of the numbers on squares that remain in the hands of other players.
If the winner ends the game by recovering a black box, the number of points is doubled.

This is, at first glance, a puzzle but it was always intended to be a game when two want to play.

Inside are 48 plastic shapes and two trays. The puzzle element is that one set of these plastic shapes, 24 in all, must be placed into the tray in such a way that they all fit.

The game is why there are two trays and 48 pieces. Each player has a tray and 24 pieces and it is a race against time to complete the problem the fastest. That player wins.

The box promises that there are 48 puzzles to solve so that should keep a pair of solvers pleased.

The object of the game is to be the first to get 20 points by taking on challenges and being the fastest to find characters on the game board

In his turn player spins the arrow and draws a card of the color indicated by the arrow.

Cards represent one of the characters. The first one who puts his finger on the image of this character (exactly the same image!) on the gameboard, wins the card. He places it face up in front of him.

Some cards are "clues": these represent either a close-up, or the silhouette of a character. The first one who puts his finger on the image of the character wins the card
Ps! The character on the image must be exactly the same position!

Some cards are "challenges": only the player who drawn this card challenges with all other players to score point or not.  If the award is made, the player keeps the card.

The end of the game:
The first to get a total of 20 points wins. Points (1 to 3, depending on the difficulty) are indicated on each card.

Issue game of Wargame Japanese History #13.

Seinan Senso is a campaign level wargame of the Satsuma Rebellion (1877).
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Satsuma_Rebellion
Designer's note writes that the game was designed with reference to basic engine of Paths of Glory and deck building concept from Dominion.

Players control either imperial Japanese Army or Rebels of Satsuma Domain. Players manage military operation and resources represented by the card.

The winner is determined by the collapse of morale. Morale will be reduced by the difference of the holding number of important spaces. Satsuma Domain player will also win that morale will not collapse until the final turn.
family: History: Boshin War & Meiji Era Rebellions

description:
Objective:
give correct answers on questions abouut Cologne -
 - acting by turn -
A small trivia game about Cologne with 100 questions and answers. The game itself is like a notebook (with spiral binding). Each page contains 2 questions and 4 answers, the back side contains the correct answers with additional information.
Questions like: Whats the name of a non-Cologne-citizen?
How long was Adenauer senior mayor?
What is a 'Bangendresser', what's a 'Pittermännchen*?

A traditional card game where the aim of the game is to score the minimum score possible over 11 rounds of play.

using 2 standard decks of cards shuffled together, each player is dealt a number of cards depending on the round, starting with 3 cards, then 4, 5, 6 etc until the players have 13 cards in hand for the final round.

Players attempt to "go out" by playing their cards out of their hand following the rules for each round, all other players add up the scores of their cards in hand in a running total. After 11 rounds, the player with the lowest score wins. As an extra, you can score individual points for who went out each round as a bonus competition.

Cards are scored based on their number, with face cards counting as 10 points, and aces as 25

Aces are wild cards, additionally, for each round, the card matching the number of cards in hand for that round (so, 3 in the first round, then 4, 5, 6 etc) is also wild (up to J, Q, K)

Jokers can be used additionally as wild cards, and also score 25 points if left in hand when someone goes out

On a player turn, they may draw a card from the face down draw pile, or lay any number of allowed hands to the table
