So, if no-one is correct after one clue, then the second clue to the same answer is tried. Then a third and a fourth, if needed. With only 50 or so cards and only one question per card, I guess replayability is not so hot.

If a player gets it right, he is given the cards and the player or team with the most cards at the end, wins.

boardgame: The Curious Case of the Disappeared Decktet card
description:
HELP! One of the cards from the original decktet has been stolen. You are on the case, like the good detective you are. The rich reward and eternal glory certainly are good motivations. Unfortunately you are not the only detective who's after the card. This makes the investigation difficult at least. 
The answers you get from your fellow detectives are ambiguous and enigmatic. So... it's up to you to find the missing card first.

This is the first Decktet deduction game. All 2-9 cards but one have been dealt out. The cards in this game all show at least 4 characteristics: one rank, two suits and one type (location, personality or event). On your turn choose two characteristics you would like to know more about. Ask the other players how many times these characteristics appear on their cards. As a detective, you will find it's just a matter of elimination. It's all quite logical...

Pirates of Gold Cove is a trick-taking card game for the whole family. New players will find the game easy to play with waves of fun for everyone. Weathered card players will enjoy the strategy of play, as vast as the deep blue sea.

The player that is dealt the ship starts the game, and each other player matches the color if they can, trying to take the trick with the highest numbered card of that color. There are cards that you want to capture for more points like treasure maps, chests, and gold coins. There is even one card that you want to avoid capturing, the dreaded Magistrate Burns. Once all the cards have been played and all tricks taken, the player that captured the Duchess Carmen receives the cards that were removed from the game, and perhaps even more points. Players total their points and are paid in gold and silver coins. Once all the coins are given out, the player with the most treasure is the winner.

The game includes high-quality plastic coated playing cards and a sack full of gold and silver coins. Whimsical characters illustrated by Monte Moore in bright colors make the game as attractive as it is fun.

boardgame: San Guo Sha: Tomb of Cao Cao
description:
UNAUTHORIZED PRODUCTION

    
    
         The information that BGG has on this item indicates that San Guo Sha is an unauthorized reproduction of BANG! with just a few differences, published in China and distributed in several coutries of the world.

    


Scattered artifacts of the Legends of the Three Kingdoms lay hidden in Cao Cao's tomb.  Find and reassemble the artifact fragments to unlock the power stored within for a millennium!  
What secret is hidden in the tomb?  Who can master the hidden power in the artifacts?

After Cao Cao's tomb was discovered, the explorers delve into the tomb to unlock Cao Cao's secret .  The players will look for artifacts in the tomb through exploration of the debris, excavation or exchange with other explorers.  The artifacts wield special power, and will aid in further exploration of the tomb.  The first player to complete five artifacts wins the game.

Though this takes place in the San Guo Sha game setting, this is a stand-alone game of drawing tiles.  Treasure and junk tiles will be placed face down in the middle of the table.  Players will draw two tiles, and keep one.  Then players will trade one of their own face-down tiles with the face-down tile of another player.  The object is to be the first to collect the right tiles that will assemble the lost treasures.

The game comes with:
   60 artifact tiles
   4 character tiles
   1 drawstring bag
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

boardgame: Ascension: Storm of Souls – Cetra, Guide of Ogo Promo
description:
Cetra, Guide of Ogo is a special "Road to GenCon" promotional card. This card will be made available as part of Gary Games' "Road to GenCon" tournaments.

From the Card:

Cetra, Guide of Ogo
Cost: 8 Runes to aquire
Lifebound Hero
Acquire all heroes in the center row without paying their costs.  Put them on top of your deck in any order.

Teachatot is designed to help children learn simple spelling and arithmetic. Select a picture card and asking the child, whose turn it is, to find and identify the letters making up the word for the picture. Similarly, you could select a number and the child must find the numbers needed to make that total. Get the most right after an equal number of turns, and you win

All the 144 pieces interlock with each other to provide lots of word combinations and arithmetic sums.

The game was first produced in Britain in the 1950's but sank without trace. However, it has now resurfaced in Australia/New Zealand, and has been available there for some time.

Players draw cards where (German) first names have been creatively transformed into some weird word conglomeration.

Actual examples from the German game: Weichangst for Hartmut, Hirschweitkaffee for Renate (Reh-nah-Tee)

An English example off the top of my head (so non-German speakers get the idea):  Buddhaout for Christin

The goal is to correctly decipher as many of the first names behind them as possible. Turns are timed with a sand timer.

Quiz game who pinched the name from a different American game related to $64,000. the names are the same but the games are completely different. The 64,000 Question is a party game where guests are asked quiz questions. No board, no playing pieces, just question cards. This game has a board and playing pieces.

Up to four players attempt to get 10 questions correct and thus reach the spinner in the middle of the box. The spinner itself is used to determine the 'colour' of the question you have to face.

The questions are intended for children to answer, not adults.

Statistical ice hockey game. A few dice rolls and the result is known, at that speed you can play a whole season within a couple of days.

All the Stanley Cup teams are rated, both as teams and as individual players.

It comes in the usual Valgames' pouch.

Football game with dice.
There are 7 dice in this game and player rolls these on his turn.
Different dice have different faces like: passing, defencing, corner kick, fault, shot a goal, penalty and players do the actions according to dice.

A game consists of two half-time of three minutes. Players with the most goals at the end of the game is the winner

Descriptions from Kickstarter:

A Las Barricadas is a boardgame about conflict between two opposing social forces, namely that of the state and of anti-authoritarian demonstrators. It is a two-player game with each player representing one of these social forces, the theatre of this conflict being that of the street demonstration. It is being developed and designed by the Black Flag Games collective, committed to the idea that games and interactive media can have an impact in the struggle for a free and cooperative world. We are also committed to the ideals of free culture and aim to deliver professional play experiences that enrich a participatory entertainment culture.

'A Las Barricadas' is a two-player, asymmetric boardgame. This means it is a contest between two players whose ability to affect the game is different. Play takes place on a board which represents a real city or historic location, and small chips are placed on building squares (buildings can be occupied, blockaded or vandalized). The Radical player chooses randomly from a small deck of objective cards and both players choose their tactics for the game. Victory is achieved by the Radical player if they complete their objectives in an allotted number of rounds. The Police player only gets a small piece of flavor text as a hint to the objectives of the radical player, and their objective is to keep the radical player from gaining victory points and completing their goals. Many of the objectives relate to acquiring Strike chips (by causing economic loss through blockades, pickets, occupations etc.) or by gaining Media chips (representing media coverage).

boardgame: The Scrolls of Power (fan expansion for Dominion)
description:
This is a fan created expansion for the card game Dominion. This print and play expansion contains 23 new kingdom cards, 13 actions (2 of which are durations), 4 treasures, 2 action/reactions, 1 treasure/reaction, 2 victories, and one strange but cool action/reaction/attack/victory card.

You need Dominion to play. You can get Dominion from your friendly local gaming store. It's well worth it.

The Scrolls of Power is designed to play alongside the designer's first print and play fan expansion for Dominion, The Books of Magic.

The game of Bird Cage is a connection game in which players attempt to connect their sides of a square by establishing an unbroken connection in a rectangular (but staggered) grid.

The game's original incarnation was as a machine built by the game's designer, noted engineer Claude Shannon.  The machine would choose its' move by measuring electrical resistance between its' sides of the square and would reportedly win "almost always" when making the first move.

The game was later re-discovered independently by mathematician David Gale, who got it published under the name of Bridg-It.

Bird Cage has since been defined as an abstract game called the Shannon Switching Game, playable on any finite graph.  In the abstract game, two players, Short and Cut, will either attempt to connect two predetermined points on the graph (short) by claiming edges, or remove graph edges (Cut) such that the two points cannot be linked.

boardgame: Angry Birds: Spring is in the Air
description:
This game one of the many games in the Angry Birds Series.

"Spring is popping and the birds will have their revenge!"  The third in the "build and destroy" series of hands-on Angry Birds games, this stand-alone game includes 1 red bird, 1 white bird, 3 pigs, 2 sets of bunny ears, 2 crackable eggs, mission cards, a "slingshot" (catapult), and an assortment of girders.

Players score points by using the birds and the slingshot to destroy the structure designated by the mission card.

This game was launched as a Target exclusive.

Australian promotional game, published by a bank.

The game is a simple roll-and-move game. Obey instructions when you land on a coloured square (Green is good but red is bad).

First player to get from Home to the Bank, wins.

boardgame: Ma Boîte à réactions: Monsieur Madame
description:
A game of reaction. Players turn the cards on the board, if they recognize a character from the card, all players must make the appropriate response: laughter, falling, tickle etc.

Whenever you're the first to give the right answer, you will receive a rubber stamp on your scoresheet. 10 shots and You win the game.

Each player has a spaceship marker that comes on the board by one edge.

In his turn player places a tile that will advance his marker on the board, creating a path. Also players must avoid asteroids flying on the board.

The goal is to remain on the board last and therefore push his opponents off the set by forcing their path out of bounds.

This game is an adaptation of Tsuro with a new twist: Asteroids will disrupt things.

A battery operated British game of chance, no skill required at all.

The board has a wheel with nine different coloured horses with jockeys on board. Players are invited to place their bets. You could play for real money or buttons. When all the bets are placed, you flick a switch and the wheel will spin. As each horse goes past a connection, their colour light flashes on, so with a fast spinning wheel, the lights all flash, adding to the excitement. When the wheel stops, there will be one light on and that is the race winner. Winning punters collect their bets and another race can start.

The winner is the one player who has the most money when the game ends.

Three dimensional abstract game. The board is plastic and stands between the players (as the Connect 4 board does). In a turn, a player may either:
Drop a disc into the board; or...
Move the current discs by operating a slide at the side.

Thus, you may add to the board or manipulte discs already in the board.

The winner is the first player to get three discs of their colour in a row.

Action game. The board is three dimensional and involves chutes to take a ball from one chute to another. A ball is dropped in the top and will work itself down to the bottom where, hopefully, it will score points depending in which hole it eventually settles.

It seems to me that there is very little skill in this. You cannot decide to throw it down the chutes with gusto, nor can you try to make it a very gentle roll. The different direction chutes take out any influence a player might have, and the end result is purely a matter of chance. Children will enjoy it though, at least until the novelty wears off.

The winner is the player with the most points at the end of the game.

The box has this slogan....
'Roll - a - ball
Down - a - hill
Try your luck
Test your skill'

Fatum is a fast-moving civilization game set in the year 600 BC in the world of ancient regions around the Mediterranean Sea. Each player is in the role of a king whose task is to bring prosperity to his lands: alleviate famine and plague, establish trade routes, build alliances, found new cities and improve culture, defend or expand his territory and secure peace. But first he must win favour and support of capricious divinities…

Players choose different actions (building, fighting, trading etc.) which, directly or indirectly, bring them victory points. Their starting positions are different, but well balanced. Strategic planning and a bit of divine benevolence could turn each of them into one of the most glorious rulers of ancient times!

From the publisher:

Field of Glory Napoleonic is written in an approachable and easy-to-learn manner to allow players to concentrate on realistic deployments and battlefield tactics of the early modern era. FOGN will cater for both types of player, with a full point system allowing theoretical battles between balanced armies to be played out, as well as accommodating factual scenarios. The rulebook will maintain the same production values as previous FOG and FOGR releases and will be designed both to explain the game and be a reference guide when playing. To make the rules easier to follow, we will include examples, detailed descriptions and explanations of unusual situations in a similar way to our Ancients and Renaissance rulebooks.

Based on the core mechanics of its predecessors, Field of Glory: Napoleonic is a tabletop miniatures game that allows players to command the armies of any of the nations involved in the Napoleonic and French Revolutionary Wars, emphasizing the combined arms tactics that dominated the battlefields of the period. Players will deploy cavalry, infantry, and artillery units to move, fire and assault their way to victory.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Enchanters of Legamoria is a free print-and-play fantasy game that combines the strategic aspects of chess with the dynamics of magic the gathering.  Designed for 2, 4 or 6 players, the game is played on a grid where opponents take turns summoning pieces, modifiers, lands and spells to the battleground.  Pieces have various attacks, abilities and effects that, when used in concert with each other, produce infinite variations of duels.  Each player may summon up to 4 lands in his/her summoning ground.  The game ends when a player or team has no more remaining lands on the board.

The game files are located at legamoria.com.  Enjoy!

Players take turns saying positive integers less than or equal to 8, but not equal to the number the previous player said, or 9 minus that number, and adding or subtracting that number to a running total. The goal is to be the first player to pass through all ten "hoops" (multiples of 10) up to and including 100. There are two ways to pass through a hoop (a) by saying a number that will reach the opposite side by an equal amount (e.g., if you are at 28, saying 4 will take you through hoop 30 to 32) or (b) by saying a number that lands you in the hoop, and then exiting by the same number on the following turn. If you miss, or are blocked, go back and try again, but you're only allowed to miss the final goal of 100 one time, or else you lose.

One variant allows players an extra move immediately after passing a hoop.

The two maps included in Power Grid: Québec & Baden-Württemberg were previously released in two separate Power Grid base games: Québec is part of Mégawatts, the French edition of the game released by Filosofia, while Baden-Württemberg was included in Funkenschlag: EnBW, a German edition of the game from EnBW.

In Québec, the Canadian province Québec places great emphasis on energy production via ecological hydro power plants. Once an ecological power plant has been added to the power plant market, it will not be put on the bottom of the deck even if it is the highest plant - the highest non-green plant is put on the bottom instead. The two cities of Montreal and Québec City offer several cheap connections, making this map challenging with several layers of decisions for building the best networks.

In Baden-Württemberg, the clocks move differently: The main change focuses on the order of phases of a game round. First, you buy power plants, then you rearrange the player order. Furthermore, as this province is small, several transregional locations may only be connected once Phase 2 begins.

Only a single printing of Power Grid: Québec & Baden-Württemberg will be produced, although the Québec map will remain available in Mégawatts.

Incorporates maps from:

    Mégawatts
    Funkenschlag: EnBW

YUM is a real-time pattern recognition game where players use an identical startup deck of round cards of different ice cream flavors and must build the proper ice cream cones, in a central location. Once a player believes a cone is complete he selects the cone card and flips it along with all his used ice cream cards. Players can clock-out for additional points once they feel that their remaining ice cream cards can't build more cones, leaving the last player without bonus points. Players verify their cones and discard all incorrect cone cards. All ice cream cards are used again and more rounds are played until the deck of cones is depleated.
family: Organizations: The Game Artisans of Canada

description:
A game that combines a drinking game with top trumps. After winning a hand (as in top trumps) the winner also has to take a drink.

Players pit their selected characters against each other on qualities such as 'Favourite tipple' and 'Special skills' to determine points scored, with losers downing their respective drinks and a special forfeit forcing them to drink the 'Dirty Pint' (when players draw a hand all the drinks are poured into one glass, the 'Dirty Pint').

33 playing cards feature a charismatic illustration of a variety of parodies of notoriously drinkers, such as the WAG, Abby Goodnight, and Prime Minister, Wineston Burphill.

Absolution is a cooperative game for Stonehenge: An Anthology Board Game.  To quote the designer:
I like cooperative games, especially "Shadows Over Camelot" by Serge Laget and Bruno Cathala, so I designed Absolution as a cooperative adventure with a Stonehenge theme. Similarly to "Shadows Over Camelot," I wanted Absolution to be difficult, but not impossible to win with the right strategy among the players. Many Stonehenge scholars can agree upon two points: It looks like some sort of astronomical clock, and people died there. In Absolution, the clock is ticking. Whether anyone dies tonight will be determined by you. Save the Druid!

The usual "no telling what your cards are" rule for collaborative games applies.  Utilizing a central exchange field to swap cards, players try to set up their hands to be able to play a single card that matches the current turn number (1–30), or a pair of cards whose sum or difference equals the turn number.  Your success at doing this determines the pace at which the poor druid walks to the altar to be sacrificed.  Don't let it happen!

Absolution is an official game for Stonehenge: An Anthology Board Game.

18?? is an 1870 variant with a new map, a large selection of private companies, and some major rule changes designed to make each game play differently. The base 1870 game rules are used.  The game kit includes a summary of rules differences from 1870.

The private companies vary game-to-game and players choose their company's starting city when a company opens.

A copy of 1870 is needed to play 18?? because the kits only include the new parts.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game – Chasing Dragons
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The great Houses of Westeros marshal armies and allies to exert their influence throughout the Free Cities of Essos. Wars, blood, lies, and alliances fill the sixty new cards (three copies each of twenty different cards) of Chasing Dragons, the third exciting Chapter Pack in the "Beyond the Narrow Sea" cycle for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game. Veterans and neophytes alike gain exciting additions to their decks, their games, and the flavor of Essos!

Chasing Dragons introduces several of the most notable characters from Essos and A Dance with Dragons. New Lords and Knights advance their Houses' interests, Raiders cruise the 
coasts and storm the shores, and Armies mass outside the Eastern continent's great city-states. Even the cities are represented with a new Kingdom location and two strong and flexible City plots.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: A Game of Thrones – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
The board is comprised of 25 posts arranged in a 5x5 array.  Players take turns placing one ball of their color on any post on the board.  The winner is the first to get five balls of their color in a straight line - this can be accomplished vertically (on the same post), horizontally or diagonally.

From the instruction book - 
Cinco: The newly designed game employing 25 pegs for 109 possible win positions.  This fascinating game of strategy gives endless hours of pleasure and excitement.

Similar to:

    Score Four

Looks like it might be an abstract game but turns out to be only luck-based.

The object of the game is to travel from your outer star to the inner one. To do this a player must try to communicate with the other players telepathically. This is attempted by another player drawing a card from the deck and you have to identify it by only the power of the mind. Five such attempts (i.e. guesses) are allowed and the fewer used mean that you move further along the board in your journey to the centre.

Battlefield Auerstadt 1806 is cardgame for battle of Auerstadt in Napoleonic wars. This game issued with self-published version Game Journal magazine volume 42.

The game use three types of cards, unit , commander and action card. The unit card represent divisions of French and Prussian army. The commander card represent French commander Davout and Prussian commander Duke of Brunswick.　The action card represent various military actions and terrain.

In each turn, players can perform one free action and can also play action card for perform additional actions.

Winner determine by advantage number or elimination of opponent. The advantage represent control of battlefield and can improve by die roll test in each turn.

Aus Dem Traum (From the Dream in German) is an operational wargame on battle of the Bulge during World War 2.

Players control either the German Army or Allied Army, and manage movement and combat. The game sequence is a standard movement, combat and secondary movement. However, several actions like movement more than one hex, secondary movement or combined attack spend operational points. A few Operational points are given each turn.

The winner is determined by control of cities and the crossing of the Meuse river by German units.

Game Scale: 

    1 division, brigade, regiment per unit
    9 km per hex
    1 day per game turn.

SPIKE is a game of beach volleyball for two teams of two players. It is played on a square grid board, with a 4x8 court in the centre.

Players move on the grid by playing cards numbered from 1 to 5. The higher the card value, the further they can travel to receive the ball - but also the more inaccurate their pass. Most cards have symbols that allow special actions, e.g. Spike, Dig or Block.

The basic game is played with four preset decks, but in Tournament Mode each player can customise their deck to reflect their strategy and individual playing style by focusing on speed, power, accuracy...

Partners are not allowed to communicate during rallies, except by selecting a Blocking Strategy card that is hidden to their opponents.

As play progresses and points are scored, players remove cards from their decks, resulting in a boost at first as the weaker cards are weeded out, and representing fatigue and predictability in the end-game, where fewer options are available.

The winner is the first team to score 11 points, or 21 in Tournament Mode.

Players take one of 4 included pieces  (Foo, Whom, Wumbus and the Grinch) and using a spinner  move them according to the color that they spin. Once on a space they take the included fishing pole and try to catch what look like whales from the fishing pond to help move them along. First player to cross the finish line wins.

On a small gameboard the players have to us their piece to create a row of four.

Playing pieces for are shaped like egg timers. Each player has a side based on the color of the egg timers which are red and green.

There are 4 hexagonal and 4 round timers of each colour. There are 4 smooth topped times and 4 rough top times of each colour.

The object of the game is to create a row of four timers with the same feature.
 - Identical by colour, so 4 reds or 4 greens.
 - Identical by shape, so 4 round, or 4 hex.
 - Identical by surface, so 4 with a smooth surface or 4 with a rough surface.

Taking turns each player places one of his timers (i.e. his color) on the board.

If at one point a row of 4 timers with at least one identical feature (i.e. color, shape, or surface texture) is created, then the game is over and the winner is the player that formed the line of four.

boardgame: Het Huis Anubis: De weg der verdoemden
description: Adventurous and challenging! Dare to go on an adventure without fear on the road of the reprobates. Find your own special powercard by using your cards very smart. Beware! You will see only the cards from your opponenents and they see your cards. In order to know which cards you held you try to ask smart questions as much as possible. When you've found your powercards turn your summary so only you can see them and than you can go directly to the road of the reprobates. If you are the first who enters the road of the reprobates, you win the game.

Spone is a game for two players (White and Black) playable with a Series: Shibumi set.

Start: The board starts with four red balls in the inner 2x2 square on the board level.

Play: Players take turns either placing a ball of their color to any playable point or entering a ball of their color from the side on non board levels, pushing other balls. The entry may cause a drop but may not push any balls off the board.

End: When the pyramid is complete, White wins if the greatest group (white or black) on the surface is even, Black wins if it is odd.

Ties are not possible.

Implementing a deck of cards, some dice and a pocketful of loose change, take control of a Zombie Horde and work to overrun a Survivor Safehouse before you are exterminated!

The solo player divides a standard playing card deck into face cards and number cards, dealing relevant cards into piles representing the Survivors and their Safehouse, Items, and Zombies.  The player, as the Zombies, then attacks the Survivors in an attempt to wear their numbers down until no Survivors remain.

It is a game of chance and skill, and very portable.  Check out the files section for the full extended rules, a quick-reference printable version (which folds up to fit in your pack of cards), and an extraneous (but helpful) flowchart of gameplay (because flowcharts make the world a better place.)

Relatively recently added: Alternate card faces for all numbered cards representing both items and zombies, as well as zombified jacks, queens, kings, aces and jokers.

boardgame: Minden Playing Card Cricket Extra Teams
description:
Expansion for Minden Playing Card Cricket. This includes a lot more teams and their stats. Teams represented (and the dates they were first issued:
India - 1989
England II - 1989
New Zealand - 1989
Pakistan - 1989
Post-War England - 1990
Sri Lanka - 1990
South Africa - 1990
Post-War Australia - 1990
Post-War Rest of the World - 1990
Post-War England II - 1991

System for replaying individual football matches by minute-to-minute decisions. Statistics for all the 1990 World Cup teams are included, as it is a system for translating statistics from any match, so you can replay that great day for your team (unless your team is England, so there is no great day you can recreate!).

Each team is rated for ability in certain skills like 'shooting at goal' or 'passing the ball', 'movement' and so on. Once you decide the option you would like to take, you roll the dice and check on the ability. Sometimes you will retain possession and thus make the next decision, and so on, until you lose the ball and your opponent has the same choices for his team.

The one who scores the most goals, wins.

From the box:  "Playful Malice is a conversational frolic for adult adults - a game in which players compare views of one another's quirks.  The purpose of the game is to have fun - and, incidentally, to see who wins the most chips."

On a round, one person is called the "Target".  The Target takes one of the 64 cards from the deck and reads it aloud.  He reads the statements one at a time, giving players time to rate each item on their score pads from 0 to 5.  The Target also rates his own personality.

Now the Target will guesses aloud how each player rated him, and depending if he is near or far from the ratings he will receive chips.  The player with the most chips is the winner.

Buffy Trivia Game is the test of your knowledge of Buffy the Vampire Slayer!

In their continuing struggle against the forces of evil, Buffy and her friends must defeat a selection of vampires and demons particularly formidable. Boxes 'quiz' brighten the path to test the knowledge of the players on the main characters and key events in the series. Each correct answer and defeated  demon can accumulate points throughout the game. Issues affect all the episodes of the first 5 seasons.

The winner of the game is the first player or team to reach the finish and successfully face the last vampire.
family: TV Shows: Buffy the Vampire Slayer

description:
Cedric like to be photographed with Chen, his girlfriend. But Chen agreed only if he pass a perfect day to be a good boy. He must collect points to charm the girl and then run to the place where she waits to be photographed with him.

There is a journey through the day of Cedric on the gameboard. Beginning with the square "Start of the day" and ending with the square "end of the day" where players is able to pose with Chen.

In his turn player rolls the color die and move his game-piece 
to next square with rolled color. Then he may pass a test to roll a number dice and get a result greater or equal than the number on the box. If the test is successful he adds the points he has just won the score noted on their file and writes it on the notes (with erasable marker).

Each player has 3 event cards he may use in his turn to help provide bonuses to its dice rolls.

The first player who reaches the finish square and pose with Chen is the winner.

A free print and play dice game based off Shaun Derrick's excellent World Cup Game.

Moving the footballing battlefield from the World Cup to the UEFA European Championships, 2-4 players can print off tournament sheets for Euro '96 in England, Euro 2000 in Belgium/Netherlands and Euro 2004 in Portugal.

Set up is simple. Print off one A4 tournament sheet and the rules sheet. Grab six dice (preferably one red, one blue, one green, one yellow and two others). Find a pencil. You're ready to go.

The teams in the tournament will be divided out between the players who will then choose the dice they want to roll and designate the dice roll results to the teams, scoring goals, defending, scoring penalties and competing to get their teams through to the quarter finals, semi finals and ultimately the final to compete for the Championship trophy!

The European Championship Dice Game is designed to be highly portable with a short play time. Perfect to take on trips or to whip out during lunch breaks.

IntroductionKonobi is a drawless connection game for two players: Black and White. It's played on the intersections (points) of a square board, which is initially empty. The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white.

				
				
					DefinitionsTwo like-colored stones are strongly connected if they are orthogonally adjacent to each other, and weakly connected if they are diagonally adjacent to each other without sharing any strongly connected neighbor. Note that two stones of different colors are never considered to be connected.

A chain is a set of connected stones.

				
				
					PlayStarting with Black, the players take turns placing stones of their own color on empty points of the board, one stone per turn.

It's illegal to make a weak connection to a certain stone unless it's impossible to make a placement which is both strongly connected to that stone and not weakly connected to another.

It's also illegal to form a crosscut, i.e. a 2x2 pattern of stones consisting of two diagonally adjacent Black stones and two diagonally adjacent White stones.

If a player can't make a move on his turn, he must pass. Passing is otherwise not allowed. There will always be a move available to at least one of the players.

The game is won by the player who completes a chain of his color touching the two opposite board edges of his color. Draws are not possible.

				
				
					Pie ruleThe pie rule is used in order to make the game fair. This means that White will have the option, on his first turn only, to change sides instead of making a regular move.

6th century BC, China does not yet have a name and seven kingdoms are fighting the mandate of heaven: the right to rule over the rising empire.

The game turn consists of nine phases. Those are moving priors, development, production, policy, recruitment, commercial, Science, movement (and battles), counts during the player move his game-pieces, take actions according the cards or draw tiles.

During the counts, player must feed itself and pay the balance of forces. Each province not be able to eat or pay will receive a token of revolt. After 2 tokens of revolt, player loses that province.
Also players earn civilization points during this phase.
1 point per province owned 
1 point per occupied capital
1 point per level of development in most developed
1 point for general
3 points per wall funded
-1 Point for each pair of tokens of revolt

The game ends after one player has accumulated at least 50 points. Whoever has the most points wins.
family: History: Warring States Period of China

boardgame: Extension France (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
The board for this Edition is based on a map of France.
As with other Ticket to Ride games, players are collecting sets of cards to claim routes necessary to complete destination tickets.

Disaster on Kangchenjunga (or "K13" for short) is the third game in Tom Decker's 'Disaster on' series.

A plane has crashed high up on the slopes of Kangchenjunga, the world’s third highest mountain. One of the passengers was carrying plans that contain secrets that if exposed or delivered into the wrong hands, could threaten world security. Your nation has sent you to join an expedition intent on reaching the summit of Kangchenjunga (Kangch), but you have different orders. You must reach the plane wreck, find the secret plans, and continue the expedition as if you are just one of the team. However, there is a spy among you who will try to sabotage your efforts and steal the plans away from you. There may also be another nation’s team on the mountain with similar interests. It’s a race to the plane crash!

The game begins at midnight at High Camp on Kangch. You will control the movements for your entire team, most of whom are trying to reach the summit. Each turn, you will move climbers towards the summit (and plane crash) and then back down again. However, at some point in the game, the spy will be revealed, changing the rules drastically and forcing you to make decisions about who will summit, who will find and protect the secret plans, and who will try to just survive!

Your decisions will not only determine the lives of your climbers, but may also stop the Cold War from heating up. Think you have what it takes? Play Disaster on Kangchenjunga to find out!

boardgame: UNO: Limited Too It's A Girl's World
description:
From the box: Pack your bags and get ready for fun with the Limited Too edition of Uno!  110 Custom Cards featuring the Limited Too crew exploring their favorite cities with their best friends.  Same great Uno play plus the exclusive Around the World card that allows players to travel to a new place and gain an edge on their opponents.

Summary: Just like standard Uno.  The Around the World card allows you to swap all hands left, right or across, player's choice, and also choose the color like a Wild card.

User summary: Roll and advance along a board by answering Bible trivia questions.  Questions have three levels of difficulty.  Game also has Gift and Peril cards that can reward or hinder your progress.  Other spaces cause you to change spaces with another player or roll again.  Game board also has a bridge that can be used as a shortcut, but you have to roll the correct number.  First player to finish wins.

One of a series of Wit trivia games, National Park Wit has 105 cards with questions about National Parks.  On the back of each card is a different photo from a National Park and a point value from 1-10 and 15.  On the front of each card are three trivia questions and answers, as well as a description of the picture on the back.  In turn, each player is asked a question of the asker's choice from the three on the card.  If he gets the question right, he keeps the card.  If he gets it wrong, all other players in turn (except the question reader) are given a chance.  If all fail, the card is placed in the so-called "stumper" pile.  After all cards have been asked, the stumper pile is revisited.  Then, all players add up their points and he with the highest points wins.

One of a series of Wit trivia games, Yellowstone Wit has 105 cards with questions about Yellowstone.  On the back of each card is a photo of something found in Yellowstone (Lower Falls, Old Faithful, brown bear, Mammoth Hot Springs, big horn sheep, Morning Glory Pool, elk, Castle Geyser, bison, a meadow, geyser, stagecoach and hotel, swan and mud pot) and a point value from 1-10 or 15.  On the front of each card is a trivia question and answer.  In turn, each player is asked a question.  If he gets the question right, they keep the card.  If he gets it wrong, all other players in turn (except the question reader) are given a chance.  If all fail, the card is placed in the so-called "stumper" pile.  After all cards have been asked, the stumper pile is revisited.  Then, all players add up their points and he with the highest points wins.

"Lulu circus" in English.

Each player receives a different illustration representing the circus spectacle.
They must find all the misplaced items.
A player draw a tile with an item.
Each player must then find it in his picture as soon as possible.
The first who shout "Given Lulu!" wins the tile.

The player who has the most tiles when all objects were found wins the game.

Who will be the best fisherman?

Fish or worms? In his turn, players tries catch some fish with a fishing rod. You may get fish but You may get worms.
They roll dice and the color determine the fish: goldfish, yellow, blue, green or purple fish? To win the fishing contest, you must be the first to catch 5 fish.

Abstract game. There are 16 plastic triangular pieces and each turn you must add one to the table in Triominos fashion, that is, one against another, but not just matching numbers you must also match colours.

First person to use all their dominos, wins.

Players is building the castle by playing cards.

In his turn player places his cards on the table to match the colors on cards already placed for scoring maximum points.

He scores as many points how long is the edge of the castle in this color

The winner is the player who scores the most points at the end of the game.

Barrier is similar to Ludo/Pachisi. It is a roll and move game in which you try to get all your counters round the board before your opponents, however you can also use your counters to form barriers (by stacking multiple counters on top of each other). Forming a barrier blocks your opponents from moving past until they roll an exact number (which reduces your barrier by 1). The downside of this is that your barrier counters can not move, but can be disbanded at any time.

The game is played in teams of 2, and your roll can count for either you or your partner. Partners must be formed form opposite colours (i.e. red & yellow, blue & green, orange & purple). Both partners can contribute to a single barrier or create separate barriers. Landing on an opponents counter sends it back to the start.

The strategy of the game revolves around 6 key points:
1) Where to build barriers for the best effect
2) When to build barriers
3) When to break barriers
4) The order of colours in the barrier
5) When not to 'knock off' your opponents counters
6) How to keep counters moving

The rules also allow for the length of the game to be altered. Each counter each person has equates to 30 minutes game play - meaning a game where each person has 4 counters lasts 2 hours, a 6 counter game will last 3 hours. It is not recommended to play with less than 4 counters each, due to the need to create barriers.

The Heads on Tails Expansion includes 3 new tail tiles for the Cartoona game.  But these are not regular tails, these are little head tails that will allow you to build two-headed creatures!

This expansion will also include 3 new cards:

     Use a tail head as a main head or vice versa
     +4 to any tail head tile
     Use a tail head as front or back feet


This expansion will allow you to build even wackier creatures, and it also changes the game slightly to make 7 and 8 tile creatures easier to complete.

Money, adventure, & success in thirty minutes or less!

GettaLife is a fun card game illustrating the universal aspects of every day life. Players will draw and play cards to their individual Life Grid while simultaneously blocking their opponents from building theirs. As players build their Life Grid, they accumulate points, and after the deck runs out, the game ends and the player with the most points is crowned the winner and has GottaLife!

During play, however, players will also play Hard Knocks cards on their opponents' Life Grids in order to slow down their own progress, and will even play Damage cards on their opponents. Life Grids consist of four different tracks, or suits: Relationships, Wealth, Knowledge, and Experience. Each player must build the best Life Grid possible by balancing the development of all four suits with the hope of playing the Legend card and multiplying their points. Of course, the crowning achievement for any suit is for the player to achieve knighthood in one of their suits by playing all four cards in one suit without having any Hard Knocks.

A promo card published by Devir for the release of the Spanish Edition of Twilight Struggle.

Card number "111 Optional".

Neutral Event - Late War - 1 ops

Requires NATO.

Each player rolls a die. The active player adds +1 and the player with more influence in Spain/Portugal also adds +1. In case of a tie, roll again. The winner adds 1 VP and adds 1 influence in Spain/Portugal. If the winner is the USSR, cancel the effects of NATO for Spain/Portugal.

Remove from play if used as event.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The king's castle was attacked. The Dragodor Princess takes her prisoner. 
Players roll the dice on their turn and move to king's castle.
Who will be the first to overcome all obstacles and reach to castle to beat the Dragodor is the winner

Each player is an inventors who must be the first to patent three of his inventions. There is also consumers who goes around on a track schedule from store to store and pay money to its owners.

In his turn player rolls 2 dice, advances the number of spaces indicated and applies the effects of the square he stands:

     He may earns the royalties were deposited at least one patent
     He may buy or exchange different components to make a prototype or invention
     Move the consumer on the trail of stores and pay royalties to its possessor


etc.

Once its patents obtained nationally, you have to pay for these and go to the finish. Try to be the first at finish with 3 obtained patents to be the winner.

User summary

A Japanese small-press card game about building a baseball team and then performing a match. The first part of the game is about recruiting team mates of which there are 27 unique ones; which are available and in which order varies. Each player has an attack value, a defence value, some special effect, how fast they run, where they hit the ball etc. In the second part, players use the team mates and the training cards they've collected to play out the baseball match; which includes comparing the different numbers and special powers on the team individuals, and using some hand management to play the right card at the right time.

Translated box back blurb:
Players of all colours and with next to superhuman powers amass on the field, the devil curbs swirve through the air and the baseball bats burst out in fire!!!
This is a game based on such baseball comics.
With all the quirky rules of baseball, and using the characters' special powers, you can taste the charm of pitching and hitting in an easy way.

It's time to PLAY BALL!

The Battle of Leipzig, fought in 1813, is one of the largest conflicts ever and involved many countries / kingdoms fighting against each other.  This included a coalition of Russia, Prussia and Austria committed to defeat France and its allies, forging a domination of Europe that would lead to the Nationalism of Germany. While historians have drawn a myriad of contrary opinions about who were the good guys and bad guys, the battle encircles the city of Leipzig with 500,000 man armies facing each other.

The sheer size of this conflict can truly test a designer’s skill when representing it at a battalion level. However, Leipzig is actually a series of smaller battles fought west, east, south and north of Leipzig as the Coalition armies concentrically move toward the City. La Bataille de Möckern 1813 is the battle north of Leipzig.

Möckern focuses on the raging struggle for this northern town which guards the passable roads to Leipzig moving south. As such, Napoleon entrusted the defense to Marshal Marmont and his VI Corps. In addition to veteran troops, Marmont has conscripts and sailors recently removed from the ports. Supporting Marmont is the III Cavalry Corps of de Casanova.

Coming down on this position is the Coalition’s Army of Silesia commanded by Marshal von Blücher. This army has a Prussian Corps, a Russian Corps and a Russian Cavalry Corps. The Prussians are fresh from several victories including holding Berlin. The Russians are somewhat smaller units given their casualties in 1812. However the Russians have a strong contingent of Cossacks and line cavalry.

The player will note immediately the differences in the Coalition formations and how each must be managed to achieve success. The French, Prussians and Russians Corps all operate a little differently injecting some real historical significance to the simulation and advancing the player’s skills as Corps Commanders.
Marmont held and lost the town of Möckern several times in this battle while the cavalry of Casanova held back the Russians. The fighting was intense, and continued into the dusk and evening hours.

Each 20 minute turn starts with a Command Phase for both sides followed by the French Player sequence and then by the Prussian Player sequence. The sequence includes movement, cavalry charges, fire and melee. During each, morale plays a decisive role. This is a great one player on one player game but will also accommodate two players on a side also.

The original rules have been updated but not expanded to any extent. The original designers of the La Bataille Series believe the Premier Rules facilitate / expedite the most accurate and competitive play. The new Premier Rules are an update of the original rules but make it easier to find specific topics and includes some user requested rules such as command control.

New rules for the players to consider are the effects of Elite Units, Landwehr skirmishing, Russian Dragoon’s abilities, improved towns, dusk combat, the loyalties of certain units, command structures and plundering. Besides the historical battle, four scenarios are included. These provide for a cavalry on cavalry only battle, inspired attacks by von Bluecher and the ability to jump into combat for the struggle of Moeckern itself. This scenario will end whenever a player reaches a threshold casualties and disintegration of order.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Series: La Bataille (Marshal Enterprises / Clash of Arms Games)

description:
Bandits of the Crab Nebula is a game set in a chaotic universes where cunning rogues search for contraband  to sell, all while avoiding the wrath of the Space Cops. Players pick from one of ten different ships, all with their own unique ability, and try and avoid asteroid belts, mine fields, and even police barricades.

Players control their ships speed, manage what is in their various hulls, install upgrades to better their chances of winning, and even play special cards to sabotage others. But they must also be careful to not be too greedy, because hidden in the deck where they find their items and cargo are special events that rarely end up good for anyone. These events can be as simple as one player discarding one piece of cargo, and as complex enough to warp everyone to a new location, have two players swap minds, or even put a ship swallowing black hole into play.

The aforementioned Space Cops are also a hassle, but also what sets the game apart from its peers. Players must manage how much cargo they are carrying and where they store it, because they might end up being accosted by the Space Cops when passing through a Police Barricade or when a rival player blows the whistle on them. If a Space Cop searches the Players ship and finds contraband, they must then deal with the consequences!

This is a simple dexterity game where players flip a wooden ball at the McMurtle Turtle target. If a player flips him over with a good shot, that player advances his/her colored wooden peg one space along the start/finish track. A spin at the beginning of the turn determines how many shots at the target each player gets. No two players can occupy the same space along the movement track at the same time, and special star spaces along the way advance a player's peg ahead to the first open space. The first player to reach FINISH wins.

boardgame: Canada (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
The Canadian fan expansion to Ticket to Ride.


     45 Trains to start
     Locomotives are for tunnels and ferries only, but players can draw them as normal cards (à la Nordic Countries)
     Parallel pairs of links are always both in play:


  - for 4-5 player games, or
  - for 2-5 player games, if one link is a ferry and one is rail

     There is no single longest route award, but there are the:


- (7-point) Sir John A. Macdonald Award for longest rail line
  - (7-point) Louis St. Laurent Award for longest ferry line.

Ticket to Ride is a registered trademark of Days of Wonder Inc. and used by permission for this non-commercial variant. It may not be re-produced for any commercial use.

Wizard Dodgeball is a sports game covering the annual all-star game of the Interplanar Wizard Dodgeball League (IWDL). Just like the dodgeball you know, the players who don't dodge the ball are eliminated; a caught ball eliminates the thrower, and the winner is the last team left on the court — but that's just the beginning as Wizard Dodgeball allows teams to throw spells in addition to balls. Boost your team, freeze your opponents, teleport balls back to your side, and watch out for fireballs!

In the game, players are dealt a team of five wizards each, and the turn order is determined by the order of the deal. Players then select spell cards based on how many spells their team can have. Each wizard has two action points that can be spent on moving, throwing balls, casting spells, or carrying out some combination of actions. Picking up or putting down balls is a free action.

Each wizard has a physical and magical range, and physical and magical attack and dodge bonuses. The outcome of throwing/casting and dodging is handled by high roll on d6s, with ties going to the defender.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The Feast of Fools – Festum Fatuorum – a medieval festival for the young ones, begins! Today they will chose from among their own number a mock pope, archbishop, bishop, or abbot and parody high ceremonies in profane and extravagance celebrations. Dare you join them?

Festum Fatuorum, a mini expansion for Basilica, consists of new promotion tokens (one for each player) and new cathedral tiles. The new promotion tokens introduce a new rank for builders: Jester. Vault tile, where a builder promoted to Jester is present becomes a wild tile with one color more. Thus, the Jester can change a single-color tile into a wild one, and wild tile into three-color one.

The new cathedral tiles contain a new order, Altar, which allows a player to place two Altar tokens on two separate tiles of the cathedral. Altars increase the value of Stained Glass tokens and protect vault tiles from the effects of two orders: Disaster and Confuse. (A set of altar tokens is included in the expansion.)

Festum Fatuorum debuted in issue 22 of the Polish boardgame magazine Świat Gier Planszowych.

Religious Charades in a way although you don't have to mime. Must be played in teams of at least two people.

In a turn, a player draws a card and tries to get his team-mates to name a Biblical character from his clues. The first team to get a pre-agreed number of characters correctly, wins.

The rulebook promises a Family Devotions variant but the rules were missing from my set so I do not know this variant at all.

A soccer (football) simulation from this publisher who only makes statistical sports games.

Teams are rated for attributes like Shooting, Defense, Assists (which is not a record kept in traditional football but must occur in serious indoor soccer) and so on. Dice are used to decide if a certain skill is successful or not. The most goals wins the match.

Includes two seasons worth of teams that play in the North American Indoor Soccer Leagues.

Statistical game, allowing you to replay Ice Hockey matches. As usual with this publisher, teams are rated for skills such as Shooting, Defense, Assists, Saves, and so on. Once an option has been chosen, dice are rolled to see if the chosen action succeeds or, if it fails, to see if the opposition gain control of the puck. Most goals wins.

My copy suggests that Gamecraft, another statistical sport game publisher, made this game first but I have never seen it. Usually with Valgames, Bob Valvano designs the game but in this case, I guess he didn't.

Theme: "In 1808 at Oak City, a mutiny in the prison ends with the escape of all prisoners. The authorities then distribute posters depicting the fugitives, promising appetizing values for each catch. Each player represents a group of armed cowboys who wants to get the opportunity to enrich easily, while working with the sheriff and his co-workers. But the prices set for each capture are not invariable and it will take as much force as diplomacy to pocket."

Wanted! The rewards is a tactic game of area control, where players must capture the most fugitives they can, for the highest price. Each player has 16 cards (cowboys) with a specific strenght (0 to 5) and special habilities. Each card is played to influence the meeples (fugitives) that borders it. When an area containing meeples is completly surrounded, the player with more strenght wins the current money for each of them (if there is a tie, a duel happens!). Besides surrounding areas containing fugitives, players must also make the better use of their card habilities to obtain the best rewards. This happens because the prices are variable. The player who uses best the "prices market" will take an important advantage, because it's preferable to capture less if the price is higher. Cards may be played face up and face down. For each card side players may choose do to the card hability or not. When playing a card face down, besides the bluff, you may move the "sheriff" of your color up to two intersections. This pawn has a strategic interest, because he strenghts all the player cards of his color by +1 (only the ones he touches). Some strategic combinations of cards can be made to prevent other players to gain a lot of money.

Besides the basic game players may choose to play it in the advanced mode, which includes a Horse which helps the player who controls it to move his sheriff further, while being stronger, and a Prison (for an ending score).

In June 2012 the game was finalist in the Plateau d'Or Boardgame Design Contest (Canada).
In April 2013 the game was finalist among 138 in Granollers Boardgame Design Contest (Spain).
In April 2013 the game won the «Coup de Coeur» prize in the NTPSLM Boardgame Design Contest (Canada) among 124 games.

Small quiz game that tests your knowledge of Britain's second longest soap, Emmerdale. No board, just 54 question cards, with six questions per card.

In your turn, roll the die and the player on your left reads out the question to you that relates to the number thrown. There are six categories on each card. If you get the question right, you keep the card and you roll the die and you are asked the question indicated on the next card, and you keep going until you get an answer wrong. If you are wrong, the card goes back to the bottom of the stack and the turn passes to the player on your left.

The player with the most cards when the deck runs out, wins.

A solitaire game is also suggested. Turn over the cards one at a time. Roll the die and answer the question indicated. If you are right, place the card on your left and call cards won, the Winning pile. If you are wrong, place it on your right and call those the Duffer's pile. Keep going until all 54 cards are exhausted. Collect 28 or more cards in your Winning pile and you have won. If you have more than 27 cards in your Duffer's pile, you lose.

Hermann Köhl (1888 - 1938) was the first aviator to fly across the Atlantic from east to west, i.e. from Europe to North America (accomplished in 1927). This game celebrates that feat. The track is 50 spaces long, tracing Köhl's route, from Germany to North America and back home again.

The game is not very novel in the game-play. Roll a die and move along the track. Obey instructions when you land on a special square. Very nice illustrations illuminate these special points along the track.

First to get to the end, wins.

2 players are positioned on either side of the table and place the wooden cubes with pictures on 4 sides between them.

Object of the game is to get row of four identical pictures.

There are 3x3 grid game board and 2 types of game-pieces red cylinders and yellow cubes. Players draws a cards where are tasks how do position the game-pieces.

At the beginning of each round the board is empty.
Each player draws a card of his objective and in his turn player places or swaps the game-pieces.

The goal of the game, is to achieve the placement showed on the card, either during installation or by swapping two pieces later stage. The player gets a card as a point.

At the end of the game if the deck is exhausted, the player with the most cards is the winner.

Statistical cricket game from the inexhaustable Terry Goodchild, who has designed more sports games than you have had hot dinners.

This game rates every County Cricket side for two seasons, 1985 and 1986. Dice are used and checked against certain ratings of the teams. Very few dice rolls are needed to complete a match because this game was intended to be a game where you can play an entire season in an evening. I don't know how many games that would be, around 150 perhaps, so a routine sports simulation was not wanted.

Dice based Rugby game, The box folds out into the inside of a rugby stadium, and both players have an elastic band powered catapult they use to "throw" the five dice into the box.

The hope is that the five dice will form a combination of moves resulting in a try.

Most tries, wins

Nemo is captured by a diver. His friends decide to go on a long journey in search of him.

In this cooperation game children search together Nemo.
In his turn, player spins the wheel and move the game-piece
building a puzzle path.

The game ends when Nemo is found (the players have won) or when the puzzle is completed Darla (then the players have lost).

boardgame: Le Monde de Nemo: La Grande Evasion
description:
Object of the game is to escape from the aquarium first.

In his turn player draws a card and play a card.
There are usual cards to move his fish to the next available slot
or special cards to exchange his hand, force opponent to discard one card etc.

First fish who has completed his journey to ocean is the winner

Rover Rummy consists of a deck of 51 cards, divided into dog "parts" - fronts, rears, bodies, heads, legs and tails. Each set of parts (except the tail) is divided into colors which denote the correctness of the body parts. Green being poor, then Yellow (fair), then Red (good), with Blue being the best (excellent). Each player is dealt 6 cards per hand. Play progresses by a simple "draw and discard" method.

The object of the game is to win the required amount of points to obtain a Championship on your "dog". A Championship is obtained by accumulating 15 "points" (based on the combined values of the colored body parts forming one "dog") over the span of several hands, and 2 major wins ( A major = a hand of 3, 4 or 5 points). The rules of winning a Championship in this game are the same pointwise, as winning a real Championship. The first player to lay down a complete "dog" wins a hand. The hand is counted by adding up the point value of each of the body parts, or in the case of a complete "red" or "blue" dog, the point value assigned to that dog.

The set (3 cards) of Nunavut Weather Authority Cards for Fleet is a fun and exciting expansion that makes use of the custom Fleet die.

Nunavut Weather Authority
1) Calm Seas!
 2-4) All other players lose 1 crate of fish

5) All other player lose 2 crates of fish

Fleet) Artic Storm! - All other players lose a boat!

Which crate(s) of fish/boat(s) are lost will always be chosen by the owner of the Nunavut Weather Authority license.

Which fish/boat(s) are lost will always be chosen by the owner of the Nunavut Weather Authority license. Its a fun card that Ben and I have enjoyed bringing back to life!

1930's party game.

There are 5 large, cardboard horses. Each horse is attached to a length of string. Up to 5 players can play but more can be included if they just bet on the result. Each player with a horse stands about ten feet away from the agreed finish line. Their line of string is attached to something substantial, like a dining room table. At the cry "They're Off!", players jiggle their string and their horse should start to move to towards the finish line. It will get pretty frenetic but that adds to the fun. First owner to get their horse to the finishing line wins.

This is a long-waited sequel game of Manstein: A Brilliant Back-Handed Blow.
The game starts just after the battle of Kursk in Summer 1943 and ends in March 1944.

The game uses the same engine as its predecessor. Each player selects his chits each turn, then draw chits one by one and activate part of the force designated by the chit.
The Soviet player must break the German defense line until the end of the game. The German player must keep the defense line until the end of the game.
The interesting new idea is "no retreat" order by Hitler. There are 6 cities which have no retreat order at the start.  If German player lose one of them while it still has this order, the game immediately ends with Soviet sudden victory. So, German player must defend them or release the order by sacrificing their reinforcement.

Time scale: one month per turn
Map scale: 15-25 km per hex
Unit scale: German divisions; Soviet corps
Counters: 252 1.3cm/0.51 inch
Map: 72 cm x 51 cm
Duration: August 1943 - March 1944 (8 turns)


Issue game of Game Journal  #42
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Imperial Crusade Freedom is a tactical space combat game that uses plastic cards made of the same material as credit cards to represent each ship on the tabletop.  These cards have ship stats printed on them so that everything that happens during a battle can be tracked directly on the card with a dry erase pen.  These cards are moved around the table like a miniature and everything is recorded directly on them.  Battles between a few ships can be played in a couple of minutes or massive fleet battles can be played in about an hour.  
 This is an expansion to Imperial Crusade Armada that adds two more factions to the Imperial Crusade universe.  It is also a stand alone game that does not require Imperial Crusade Armada to play.  The Freetrade Coalition uses drone fighters and mines to destroy their enemies.  The Outland Alliance uses powerful gunships to assault enemy ships and their force manipulators to disrupt their opponent's plans.

This is a game about dwarfs knocking each other into pits and collecting gold. Dwarfs are natural fighters, and this game naturally contains a lot of fighting. It also contains forest elves to kick.

Players take turns moving their dwarfs around the board and use item cards to trip up the other dwarfs. Depending on what game mode is in play GOLD could be waiting at the end of a race, ready to be dug up, or even in the hands of a GNOME IN A MECHANIZED POWER SUIT. It's up to the players to use their dwarfs special skills to out maneuver the competition.

Tessels is a trivia game about homonyms (words with different meanings)
The game consists f 50 cards, each with 3 hints on a homonym.

Players simultaniously try to find the right hymonym. If a player can find the word with only the first hint, he scores 3 points, after the second hint, he gets 2 points and after the 3rd one point.

The first hint is a sentence with the homonym replaced by TESSELS, for example. "While the perents went to a TESSELS, their children played with a TESSELS.

The second hint consists of synonyms for the two meanings (like "orb dance")

And the third hint is a sketch.

Promotional Canadian game, issued by the tyre supplier.

The board is a track, taking you from Halifax to Vancouver. As usual in these sort of games, if you land on a space with instructions, then you must follow those instructions, be they good or bad.

When you get to one space from winning, you must stop there. You cannot get to Home without rolling a double on the dice. This probably means that you will be there for a few turns which helps others to catch up and make the end of the game a little more exciting. First to Home (Vancouver), wins.

Each player has a map overlaid with squares. One player is the Smuggler, the other is a Coastguard. Players start at oppositer ends of the map. Movement is governed by dice. Both players fill in the squares on the path they have taken that throw. Filled-in squares cannot be used again by the player who filled them in.

The objective is to avoid interception or to intercept, depending on which side you are on.

description:
WarTiles is a strategy game where up to 4 players face off, each commanding a unique faction from the world of Aëran. Every unit, terrain, and spell is represented by a tile placed on a 7x7 battlefield, where every move and action carries significant weight.

The game is defined by its core mechanic: Shift. Instead of moving freely, units are affected by the battlefield itself shifting whenever a tile is destroyed. An entire row or column slides to fill the empty space, constantly reshaping the layout of the board. This unique dynamic forces players to anticipate changes and seize every tactical opportunity.

The game ends when a player successfully eliminates an opposing hero, earning him victory.

boardgame: Rifles & Rebels: Fighting the Civil War 1861-1865
description:
“IN GREAT DEEDS SOMETHING ABIDES. ON GREAT FIELDS SOMETHING STAYS." —Joshua Chamberlain

It's July 1863 and the Army of the Potomac is holding on. Yesterday the Johnnies hit hard and the line held. Today you are the leftmost flank of the army and must not give an inch of ground.
Though dulled by the morning mist, the shrill sound of a Rebel Yell is unmistakable. The Johnnies are on the move again.

Will you hold?

Rifles & Rebels is a unit based game where units are composed of 5 to 10 figures. In Rifles & Rebels you can choose from two sides and two arms with a total of four forces. You can be:

• Union Infantry
• Confederate Infantry
• Union Cavalry
• Confederate Cavalry
Whichever you choose you must lead one or more units of men through the war, the American Civil War, fighting when needed because it's all about victory and glory, and in some cases, just survival!
The cannon are booming, the troops mustering, and the fight is coming to you. Welcome to …

RIFLES & REBELS
Rifles & Rebels uses a floating scale which allows you to count each unit as a platoon, company, or battalion. This allows you to fight large battles as well as smaller actions.
Rifles & Rebels and Muskets & Mohawks share similar mechanics with changes made to reflect the different periods.

(From the producers website)

Priest of Apollo, Athena, Demeter, Poseidon or Zeus, 3-5 players try to make their worship the one who will dominate the whole of Greece. As you gather up YOur followers, build monumental temples, free the cities of mythological monsters, use your political weapons for your own benefit.

The game consists three phases:

     Action Phase
     Agora Phase
     Stage season


In his turn in first phase player places his followers to build temples, gather resources and hunt monsters to get more followers.

Also it is possible to convert opponents' cities, or even paralyze them by sending Your monsters to attack them.

In Agora Phase player deifies his heroes, who offer more improved actions for the action phase

Finally the season phase is a phase of replacement.

Player must meet at least 10 worshipers ( or have Three shrines before the end of the game to be the winner of the game.

Valkyrie is a game of checkers with 2 boards. One is Midgard (Earth), the other Asgard (heaven). When the game starts, each player has three gods and a Valkyrie on first board 9 troops (human or monsters) on the second board.

Player wins the battle if he eliminate 3 opponent's gods or has last man standing on the second board.

In his turn player has 2 actions/movements according to the following rules:

     troops move one square in the 8 directions
     the gods move one square orthogonally and never back
     the Valkyries move freely on the set except the last line.


If the move ends on square with an opponent the battle begins.

The value of a pawn in combat is equal to the value of the piece (5 for the gods, 1 to 3 for the troops).

The Valkyries may move a troop from one world to another if it stands one of the adjacent square.

German game celebrating Germany's fame, as they were once the leading airship manufacturer in the world.

The game is a simple roll and move game, taking your Zeppelin across Europe and visiting the major cities. Obey instructions if you land on special spaces. First to the end of the track, wins.

description:
Take on the role of one of the Three Little Pigs as you try to build a house strong enough before the Big Bad Wolf arrives to blow it down and eat you.  Move around the board collecting straw, sticks, and/or bricks and then bring them back to your building site to build your house before the big bad wolf arrives.  Will you risk travelling further to get to the brick pile, or build a house of straw quickly and then sit back and relax counting on luck to save you when the big bad wolf comes calling.

Watch out, your brother pigs can steal what you’ve collected before you get it back to your house or speed up the wolf’s approach.  The pigs whose houses still stand at the end of the game are the winners.

Extra points are awarded for being lazy, but they only matter if your house can survive the coming wolf.

Do you want to take your chances by building a house of straw?

Do you have the time to gather the bricks for your house before the wolf arrives?

Move fast and be smart or it won't matter how much hair you have on your chinny chin chin.

description:
Horse racing game that takes its game-play from the traditional British card game of Racing Aces.

The cards are shuffled and a number are taken out of the deck and revealed to the players. In the race, cards will be revealed and the horse in that racing colours will advance one space. This is done for each card revealed until one horse crosses the finish line.

It will be seen that some horses will have less cards in the pack than others. This is because when you take out the cards before the race begins, those cards are no longer in the pack, so the horses affected have less cards available and thus have less chance of winning. The odds for them to win will be higher than the odds om horses with, for example, all their cards still in the deck.

Players bet on each race and after a pre-agreed number of races, the player with most money, wins.

John Rickman, British television's eccentric horse racing expert, pictured on the box.

Game that tries to encourage people into the hobby of Genealogy, ancestor hunting you might say.

Generations is a board game with playing method which uses logic reasoning (in this regard, very similar to Cluedo) to solve a mystery of family history. Players search 7 regions of the United States for their make-believe ancestors, U.S. birthplaces, careers and lifestyles.

The first player to complete a family history (i.e. work out which facts are hidden, by eliminating facts that have been revealed to that player), wins.

Several game options ensure that play never becomes boring and that different levels of skill can be achieved. Variations include:
Logic Reasoning: Adapt for the time you have and the playing skill levels by varying the number of search categories. .
Storytelling: At the end of the game, the winner can make up a story about the discovered ancestor's life using "facts" found during play. This variation develops imagination and communication skills.
True Family History: Introduce players to real family stories as they "travel" regions of the United States asking questions about their pretend ancestors, where they were born, how they may have lived and how they earned their living.

Contents;
playing instructions (for home and classroom)
game board
moving pieces
dice
cards
lifestyle tokens
career tokens
ancestor worksheets
realistic family tree charts

You must direct your team to the control Centre via an international network of safe houses. En route, you must eliminate the opposition whilst avoiding their murderous attempts on your agents.

Statistical cricket game with over 120 players rated in areas like batting, bowling and fielding. When this was designed, One Day International matches would last for 50 overs to be bowled by both sides. There is a certain hit and hope element when compared to full Test Matches. In 50 overs you don't have time to build an innings. So a player's One Day rating will be very different to a player's Test Match rating. This designer designed games for both codes of cricket.

Play is straightforward. A player metaphorically bowls a ball which has to be checked with dice against his rating, to see the outcome. Then a batsman rolls dice, against his batting ability, to see if he has hit it. Chances of 'Outs' could be decided by a briiliant delivery, or a mis-timed hit or a fantastic bit of fielding.

Two games in one box, the only link being that they are both designed to help 7 to 11 year-olds with their maths.

Ski Race is a dice game with four special dice that have very unusual number spreads on them. Children are encouraged to do simple maths with the numbers rolled and thus advance in the Ski Race.
